title: Trimodal Disentangled Representation Learning for Motion Understanding
authors: Myungin Kim, Ri Yu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Trimodal motion understanding systems that integrate motion, video, and text face a fundamental challenge: existing approaches typically excel at either generation or retrieval, but rarely both. This trade-off arises from conflicting optimization objectives—generation requires modality-independent representations to preserve diversity, whereas retrieval benefits from tightly aligned cross-modal embeddings for accurate matching. To address this challenge, we introduce a TRImodAl representation Disentanglement framework (TRIAD) that decomposes trimodal embeddings into task-specific components: a generative subspace for generation and a discriminative subspace for retrieval. A shallow dual-router Mixture-of-Experts architecture enables independent optimization pathways for stable task-specific disentanglement. Our results demonstrate that explicit task-specific decomposition allows a single unified model to perform both generation and retrieval. TRIAD provides empirical analysis on jointly supporting generation and retrieval within a single trimodal framework.

title: A Novel Transient Disturbance Waveform Compression Method in Distribution Networks Based on Dictionary Learning
authors: Diantao Sun, Hao Liu, Tianshu Bi, Kenneth Martin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: The distribution network with a large-scale integration of distributed renewable energy sources exhibits more complex dynamic behavior. The widespread deployment of high-resolution waveform monitoring in distribution networks is hindered by the massive data transmission and storage requirements. To mitigate this communication bottleneck, this paper proposes a novel transient disturbance waveform compression method in distribution networks based on dictionary learning. A new framework for disturbance waveform compression on terminal side and reconstruction on master station side has been proposed to outline the offline learning and online decomposition steps. Then a determination method of the start and end times of transient disturbance waveforms based on the relative energy acceleration operator (REAO) has been proposed for disturbance waveform segmentation, which clarifies the disturbance processes that need to be monitored. To improve the decomposition and reconstruction efficiency, an overcomplete dictionary design method that considers feature redundancy within the dictionary based on clustering pruning optimized K SVD (CPK-SVD) has been proposed. The redundant atoms in the dictionary are pruned based on Gaussian mixture model (GMM) clustering method after feature extraction. Simulation and field recorded waveform data tests show that the proposed method can achieve a compression ratio of over 10 while ensuring measurement accuracy.

title: Time–Power Optimization for Ultraviolet Communication-Positioning with Energy Harvesting System in NLOS Channels
authors: Xiaohuan Zhang, Huimin Lu, Danyang Chen, Xuecheng Wei, Zihuan Liang, Junyan Zhou, Jianli Jin, Jianping Wang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this work, an integrated ultraviolet (UV) communication– positioning with energy harvesting (UV-CPEH) system in non-line-of-sight (NLOS) channels is considered. Under constraints on positioning error, total transmit power, and harvested energy, a joint optimization model based on the time-switching and power-splitting (TS–PS) mechanism is formulated to enhance the achievable system rate. To address the inherent non-convexity, an efficient iterative algorithm leveraging successive convex approximation (SCA) is proposed to obtain a convergent solution. Numerical results show that the proposed time–power optimization strategy effectively balances the trade-off among communication, positioning, and energy harvesting.

title: LLT: Lossless Transmission using Local Recirculation for WANs
authors: Wenyi Wang, Junchang Wang, Yu Hong, Lei Han, Xin He, Weibei Fan, Zixuan Guan, Xiaolong Zheng, Fu Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: As distributed applications increasingly span geographically distributed data centers, the demand for high-performance, long-distance transmission has been continuously growing. While intra-data-center networks have employed techniques like remote direct memory access (RDMA) to meet these design goals, extending these techniques toWANs presents unique challenges. WANs notably suffer from inherent packet losses due to buffer overflows in routers and switches, leading to decreased throughput and making distributed applications barely usable. This paper proposes Lossless Transmission (LLT), a novel buffer management scheme for enabling lossless WAN transport. LLT intelligently integrates on-chip switch buffers with an off-chip caching system to absorb traffic bursts that would otherwise cause packet loss. Its data plane logic uses a multi-level threshold system to selectively offload only critical flows during congestion. A closed-loop control protocol, managed by a stateful flow table, ensures these offloaded packets are later re-injected with guaranteed lossless and in-order delivery, effectively protecting latency-sensitive applications from retransmission overhead. We evaluate LLT using both ns-3 simulations and P4-programmable devices. The experimental results show that in typical use cases (RTT > 30ms), LLT improves link bandwidth utilization by 1.9% to 29.5% and reduces the P99 percentile tail latency by 17% to 66% in WANs compared to the state-of-the-art solutions. Overall, LLT provides a scalable, efficient, and reliable framework for long-distance data transmission, addressing critical challenges in WANs. Additionally, LLT eliminates the need for expensive WAN infrastructure modifications.

title: A Novel Quadratic Ultra-High Step-Up DC-DC Converter Based on Coupled Inductor
authors: Saeed Azmoon, Ebrahim Babaei, Darioush Alizadeh, Mehran Sabahi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a novel high-step-up DC-DC converter featuring continuous input current and a quadratic voltage gain. The converter uses a three winding coupled inductor (TWCI), and a passive clamp circuit to reduce voltage stress across the power switches. Furthermore, the voltage gain is extended by integrating the TWCI's tertiary winding into the output stage alongside a voltage multiplier cell (VMC). By leveraging the leakage inductance of the TWCI, the converter operates in a quasi-resonant operation (QRO), resulting in resonant current waveforms for the switches and VMC diodes. This characteristic facilitates true and pseudo Zero Current Switching (ZCS) for these diodes, which eliminates reverse recovery issues and minimizes switching losses. Consequently, the reduced losses and low component stress lead to high overall efficiency. This paper presents the converter's steady-state analysis in both Continuous Conduction Mode (CCM) and Discontinuous Conduction Mode (DCM), dynamic modeling, and design considerations. A comprehensive comparison with recently reported high step-up converters demonstrates that the proposed topology provides a superior trade-off among voltage gain, semiconductor voltage stress, switching device power (SDP), power density, and cost. Finally, the analysis is validated by experimental results from 400 V, 226 W prototype, achieving peak efficiency of 96.2% at 200 W.

title: Broadband and High-Resolution Vector Spectral Measurement System for Integrated Photonic Chips Based on Asymmetric Interferometer and Phase-Retrieval Resampling
authors: Wenqi Li, Guoquan Li, Pengfei Wang, Hongsheng Niu, Shangqing Shi, Guohua Hu, Binfeng Yun
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: It is widely recognized that performing wide bandwidth, high-resolution amplitude and phase characterization for integrated photonic chips has long been a technical challenge. In this paper, we propose an optical vector analysis system constructed based on asymmetric Mach-Zehnder interferometry and balanced detection. A phase-retrieval resampling algorithm rooted in Hilbert transform is adopted, which not only eliminates nonlinear errors induced by frequency sweeping of the semiconductor laser but also significantly enhances the frequency resolution of the system. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves ultra-broadband characterization spanning 1480∼1640 nm, with a measured frequency resolution reaching 1.66 MHz. By characterizing the transmission spectra of a fiber Bragg grating and a high-Q silicon nitride micro-disk resonator, the system's capability to retrieve both amplitude and phase responses of photonic devices with simultaneous broad spectral coverage and high frequency resolution is verified, thereby providing an effective characterization solution for high-performance integrated photonic chips.

title: A Non-Invasive Path to Animal Welfare: Contactless Vital Signs and Activity Monitoring of In-Vivo Rodents Using a mm-Wave FMCW Radar
authors: Tommaso Polonelli, Manuel Glahn, Stefano Kron, Stefan Selbert, Marco Garzola, Michele Magno
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Monitoring physiological and behavioral parameters of laboratory rodents is fundamental for biomedical research; however, conventional techniques often rely on invasive sensors or frequent handling, which can induce stress and compromise data fidelity. To address these limitations, this paper presents a contactless and non-invasive in-vivo monitoring system based on a low-power 60GHz frequency-modulated continuous wave (FMCW) radar. The proposed system enables the detection of rodent activity and vital signs directly within home-cage environments, eliminating the need for implants, electrodes, or human intervention. The hardware platform leverages a compact Infineon BGT60 series radar sensor, optimized for low power consumption and continuous operation. We investigate sensor placement strategies and design a complete signal processing pipeline, including range bin selection, phase extraction, and frequency-domain estimation tailored to rodent vital signs. The system achieves 3 cm and 0.1 m/s sensitivity for motion and activity detection, while allowing discrimination of micro-movements associated with pulmonary activity with a 2 μm distance resolution. Experimental validation with two rodents in realistic in-vivo cages demonstrates that the radar can track animal position and extract respiration rates with ±2bpm accuracy. By minimizing stress and disturbance, this work improves both animal welfare and the reliability of physiological measurements, offering a refined alternative to traditional monitoring methods. This work represents the first demonstration of continuous radar-based vital sign monitoring in freely moving rodents within group-housed cages. The proposed approach lays the foundation for scalable, automated, and ethical monitoring solutions in preclinical and translational research.

title: Breakdown Mechanisms of Vertical GaN Junction Field-Effect Transistors and Enhanced Figure of Merit Using Gradient Doping Channel
authors: Daoyuan Li, Shenglei Zhao, Xiufeng Song, Naixuan Wang, Longyang Yu, Juan Gui, Ga Zhang, Xuejing Sun, Yichen Zhang, Linhuan Gao, Yue Hao, Jincheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this study, the parameters of a normally-off vertical GaN junction field-effect transistor (JFET) were simulated to analyze the breakdown mechanisms of the device. Key factors, including the electron concentration of the channel ( ${N}_{\text {channel}}$ ), hole concentration of the p+ region ( ${N}_{{P}-\text {region}}$ ), channel length ( ${L}_{\text {channel}}$ ), and channel width ( ${W}_{\text {channel}}$ ), were evaluated for their influence on the electrical characteristics and breakdown mechanisms of the device. The findings reveal the following: 1) JFET devices exhibit three breakdown mechanisms—drain-to-gate and drain-to-source breakdowns, and the coexistence of both; 2) under the drain-to-source breakdown mechanism, the device experiences higher leakage current and severe breakdown voltage (BV) degradation; 3) when the breakdown mechanism is drain-to-gate, the device demonstrates superior breakdown characteristics, with low leakage current and high BV, an albeit with increased specific on-resistance ( ${R}_{\text {on,SP}}$ ); and 4) when both mechanisms coexist, the device exhibits increased leakage current while maintaining a high BV. Concurrently, the forward characteristics improve significantly, enhancing the power figure of merit (FOM). To improve the overall device performance, a gradient doping channel JFET (GD-JFET) device was proposed, in which a three-layer gradient doping channel was adopted to balance the tradeoff between FOM and threshold voltage ( ${V}_{\text {TH}}$ ). Compared to a conventional JFET (C-JFET), the ${V}_{\text {TH}}$ of GD-JFET increased by 15.8%, whereas the FOM increased by 12.0%. These findings indicate that the GD-JFET has great potential in power device applications.

title: High-Efficiency, Broadband and Compact Si Crossing for Multi-Wavelength Systems
authors: Xiaoyan Zhang, Wu Xie, Chewping Leong, Feng Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: The growing demand for high-speed, energy efficient, and cost-effective broadband networks has positioned passive optical networks (PONs) as a cornerstone of modern communication. Silicon photonics offers a scalable platform for chip-based PON integration, yet the absence of compact and broadband crossings limits practical deployment. Here we introduce a multi-wavelength Si crossing, designed through inverse optimization and fabricated in a CMOS-compatible foundry process, that simultaneously routes upstream O-band (100 nm) and downstream S+C+Partial-L bands (>120 nm) signals across the entire PON spectrum. The crossing achieves a compact footprint of 8 × 8 μm², fabricated through a single-step CMOS compatible process, ensuring manufacturability at scale. Experimental results demonstrate low insertion loss (< -0.25 dB) and crosstalk suppression below -30 dB, with minima approaching -60 dB, in close agreement with simulations. Comparative analysis reveals that, while maintaining compactness and high performance, the device uniquely achieves broadband coverage unmatched by prior approaches. Beyond PON integration, this work highlights the transformative role of inverse design in bridging computational photonics with large-scale manufacturing, paving the way for next-generation optical systems in communication, computing, and quantum technologies.

title: Robust Beamforming for MIMO-OFDM ISAC Systems
authors: Kun Lan, Jian Sun, Meng Liu, Yuanwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: A robust integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) beamforming scheme is proposed for wideband multiple input multiple output (MIMO) orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) systems. In particular, the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for localization error within a coarse location area (CLA) is derived. The joint ISAC beamforming is then optimized to minimize the maximum CRB in the CLA subject to the minimum multiuser communication requirements. Although the resulting optimization problem is non-convex, a semidefinite relaxation algorithm is proposed to obtain globally optimal solution. The simulation results show that the proposed scheme achieves desired CRB and low communication performance loss.

title: Two-Layer Memristive Neural Network Circuit Based on Biological Hierarchical Information Processing Mechanism
authors: Qiuzhen Wan, Kunliang Sun, Peng Qin, Qinghui Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The brain efficiently processes information through hierarchical collaboration among multiple brain regions. Based on this biological hierarchical information processing mechanism, this paper proposes a two-layer memristive neural network circuit and applies it to the classification of the iris dataset. The proposed circuit comprises a first-layer competitive learning circuit and a second-layer radius-neighbors-classifier (RNC) circuit. In the first-layer, the competitive learning circuit separates the most easily distinguishable data type from the remaining ones. The forward calculation module of the competitive learning circuit implements competitive behavior based on the winner-take-all (WTA) mechanism, and it outputs the first-layer classification results. The weight adjustment module of the competitive learning circuit implements self-learning through the Hebbian learning rule, and it adjusts the resistances of memristors. In the second-layer, the RNC circuit takes charge of classifying the data types that exhibit high similarity. The memristive storage module of the RNC circuit stores the memory data. The RNC calculation module of the RNC circuit processes the input data and the memory data, and then outputs the second-layer classification results. Utilizing PSPICE to simulate the proposed circuit, this paper conducts the classification experiments on the iris dataset, which includes three iris types: Setosa, Versicolor and Virginica. The simulation results demonstrate that the competitive learning circuit successfully separates the Setosa from the others and the RNC circuit accurately classifies the remaining two types of irises. Moreover, this proposed circuit not only reduces energy consumption but also achieves high accuracy in classifying data with high similarity.

title: Low-complexity Grouped Sparse Superposition Coding Based on Binary Golay Sequences
authors: Linjie Yang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Sparse superposition coding (SSC) is one of the key enabling techniques for ultra-reliable and low-latency communications. To further improve the block error rate (BLER) performance of SSC, a grouped sparse superposition coding scheme is proposed in this paper. At the transmitter, the information bits are divided into two parts. The first part is conveyed through sparse vector coding, while the second part is modulated as quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) symbols. Specifically, the sparse-mapped bits are evenly partitioned into N segments, where each segment is uniquely mapped to a spreading sequence, resulting in a structured sparse vector representation. At the receiver, a modified multi-path matching pursuit (MMP) algorithm is developed to explicitly exploit the inherent group-wise sparsity structure for efficient data recovery. Moreover, binary Golay sequences are employed to construct the spreading sequences. Benefiting from their favorable algebraic properties, the proposed scheme eliminates the need for storing both the spreading sequence set and the index table required in conventional SSC, thereby significantly reducing the computational complexity and memory overhead.

title: A Phase-Expanded Propulsion Method to Suppress Attractive Force Difference in a Levitation-Propulsion-Integrated Magnetic Levitation Transporter System
authors: Akito Konishi, Shota Mitsui, Ken-ichi Kondo, Shunsuke Ohashi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The objective of this study is to develop a magnetic levitation transporter system with a simple system structure. In the proposed system, both levitation and propulsion are achieved using a unified mechanism. A linear stepper motor is employed to simultaneously generate attractive and propulsive forces. Propulsion is realized using a DC power supply, without the need for position sensors in the propulsion direction. In previous research, levitation was achieved, and the maximum allowable attractive force imbalance between the front and rear of the carrier which is necessary for stable levitation was identified. However, propulsion had not yet been realized. This paper proposes a novel excitation switching method to suppress attractive force imbalance and stabilize levitation during propulsion. In this new method, a four-phase excitation state was inserted between successive three-phase excitation states, thereby halving the propulsion step length. Experiments were conducted, and the results demonstrate that the transporter system achieves stable propulsion while maintaining levitation.

title: Robust Low-Tubal-Rank Tensor Sensing via Preconditioned Subgradient Descent_supp1-3698753.pdf
authors: Linyu Huang
publish: N/A
abstract: We study robust low-tubal-rank tensor sensing from corrupted linear measurements. We propose a preconditioned subgradient descent (PSGD) method for a factorized <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell _{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-loss formulation, which avoids repeated t-SVD computations during the iterative updates. Under the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell _{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-RIP assumption, PSGD enjoys a linear convergence rate independent of the tensor condition number. Experiments on both synthetic and real datasets demonstrate significant acceleration and robustness over the standard subgradient-based method.

title: CausalRAG-AD: Multimodal MRI Classification and Guideline-Compliant MRI Captioning for Alzheimer’s Diagnosis
authors: Ramisa Farha, Md Mahmudur Rahman, Fahmi Khalifa
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a degenerative disease, and current AI-based diagnostic procedures often lack calibrated probabilities, clear causal explanations, and comprehensive reporting. We propose CausalRAG-AD, an end-to-end framework that (i) trains calibrated transformer classifiers for AD staging from structural MRI and (ii) generates NIA-AA guideline–compliant, evidence-grounded MRI captions. The proposed architecture augments Video Vision Transformer (ViViT) with ROI-gated attention (CausalRAG-ViViT) from FreeSurfer hippocampus/ventricle/temporal z-scores and a light clinical gate; per-fold temperature scaling yields calibrated probabilities. It is evaluated against ViT+BiLSTM and ViViT-lite baselines using Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) cohort. For binary AD vs. Cognitively Normal (CN), CausalRAG-ViViT achieves the strongest calibration (ECE 0.078). In triclass CN / mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / AD, CausalRAG-ViViT reaches the best Acc (0.519) with competitive F1 (0.452). The proposed system produced 4,427 structured per-visit MRI reports. Every report contained all required sections, and most explicitly documented MRI provenance (scanner/vendor and field strength; 93.6%). All pipeline-generated numbers—MRI z-scores, MMSE, and CDR-SB—were replicated exactly from source tables (100% fidelity), and Aβ42 values matched the source (93.9%). When the necessary biomarkers were available, the AT(N) labels in the report consistently matched with our rule-based determination (100%).

title: Feature Extraction Methods in Semiconductor Manufacturing Systems
authors: Zhihui Ji, Huijie Mao, Ximeng Wang, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing process, analyzing vast amounts of stored data is essential for process control and management. Reliable feature extraction on massive process data is crucial for equipment health monitoring and Fault Diagnosis (FD), thereby informing proper maintenance decisions. The key challenge lies in overcoming data non-stationarity and distribution drift, driven by process variations, to extract robust key features. This paper presents a categorized review centered on the application of feature extraction methods in semiconductor manufacturing systems, and provides an analysis and summary of the results.

title: Decentralized Chain-of-Custody System for Forensic Evidence Integrity Using Blockchain
authors: Bharath Singh J, Surya K, Jebarson S, Udhayan V S, Sri Hari K, Vivekrabinson K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Several factors that bear on ensuring the reliability, transparency and integrity of physical and digital evidence remain a foundation on the criminal investigations of the day. Conventional chain of custody (CoC) systems tends to be built around centralized reservoirs, manual signature files and data-free file record updates which may be tampered with, lost by accident or in the absence of documentation. The set up proposed in this paper includes a chain of custody blockchain framework combined with decentralized file storage in the Interplanetary File System (IPFS), a safe authorization of identities to transfer the evidence, and auditable immutability of the interactions in any case. Not only the Investigating Officers (IOs) can register evidence, create unique identifiers of each item and upload the first images to IPFS, but also passwords backed transfer requests to the authorized custodians had been introduced. On acceptance, the receiving custodian is posting a compulsory photographic confirmation of condition to act as evidence of integrity of the evidence at the time of this transfer. Every operation, such as registering evidence, transfer (IO) approval and condition recording, is hashed with a cryptographic hash and stored in the blockchain to form a permanent, non-repudiable list of activity. Implemented results illustrate the platform ensuring verifiable traceability, resilient tamper resistance, transparent transition histories, and strong purposes of forensic compliance throughout all of the evidence handling interactions.

title: Modified Slip Coupling Design for High Temperature Conditions in HTGRs
authors: Sweejal Kafle, Nathalie Schelin, Mariam Lara, Elizabeth Wells, Bryan Kuhr
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: High-temperature gas-cooled nuclear reactors (HTGRs) are Generation IV reactors currently in development around the world that can provide efficient electricity generation and industrial heat. However, their high coolant temperatures also drive significant thermal expansion in coolant piping, which in turn creates high piping stresses and a need for expansion joints that accommodate large axial movements while maintaining the coolant pressure boundary. This project aims to develop a coupling to accommodate 4 to 6 inches of axial growth in HTGR coolant piping while withstanding internal pressures from 5 torr vacuum to 4.8 MPa internal pressure at temperatures reaching 900°C. Conventional expansion loops, bellows joints, and slip joints are challenged under these conditions. To improve flow characteristics and service life while meeting the operating requirements of the HTGR environment, the selected solution is a modified slip coupling with three key features: external pressurization to improve pressure and temperature performance of sealing, springs for thrust-load dissipation, and metallic seals instead of more temperature-limited packing materials. This design is evaluated using finite element analysis and requirements from ASME B31.1, B31.3, and BPVC Section VIII Division 2 for protection against collapse and failure from cyclic loading. The resulting design balances axial motion, pressure retention under high temperatures, leakage reduction, and maintainability, providing a practical solution for HTGR piping systems.

title: Data-Grounded LLM Framework for Intelligent Travel Planning
authors: Hayam R. Seireg, Amr M. Omar, Fares A. Eraky, Omar M. AbdelQader, Youssef A. Mousa, Fabio E. Sedik, Tasneem Abdelrahman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Tourist itinerary planning requires balancing personalization, geographic coherence, and feasibility, yet conventional recommender systems address these demands unevenly. Large language models (LLMs) support more context-sensitive planning, but without grounding in verifiable data they may produce implausible or hallucinated itineraries.This study presents an agentic LLM-based tourism planning framework that couples generative reasoning with structured grounding from locally sourced Egyptian business datasets. Itinerary construction is staged: candidate attractions and restaurants are first retrieved using spatial and semantic criteria, then the LLM selects among candidates, provides brief rationales, and synthesizes the final itinerary. This hybrid approach aims to improve realism and contextual fidelity while limiting hallucination.The framework is evaluated on five open-source LLMs using metrics for constraint satisfaction, preference alignment, grounding accuracy, and spatial efficiency. Hybrid grounding improves reliability overall; Mistral attains the highest aggregate score (0.669), whereas smaller models such as Phi-2 are more spatially efficient but weaker in alignment and grounding.The results support integrating LLM reasoning with structured data and clarify trade-offs among model scale, computational cost, and planning quality, with implications for intelligent tourism system design.

title: Rabies diagnosis in low-data settings: A comparative study on the impact of data augmentation and transfer learning
authors: Khalil Akremi, Mariem Handous, Zied Bouslama, Farah Bassalah, Maryem Jebali, Mariem Hanachi, Ines Abdeljaoued-Tej
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Rabies remains a major public health concern across many African and Asian countries, where accurate diagnosis is critical for effective epidemiological surveillance. The gold standard diagnostic methods rely heavily on fluorescence microscopy, necessitating skilled laboratory personnel for the accurate interpretation of results. Such expertise is often scarce, particularly in regions with low annual sample volumes. This paper presents an automated, AI-driven diagnostic system designed to address these challenges. We developed a robust pipeline utilizing fluorescent image analysis through transfer learning with four deep learning architectures: EfficientNet-B0, EfficientNetB2, VGG16, and Vision Transformer (ViT-B-16). Three distinct data augmentation strategies were evaluated to enhance model generalization on a dataset of 155 microscopic images (123 positive and 32 negative). Our results demonstrate that TrivialAugmentWide was the most effective augmentation technique, as it preserved critical fluorescent patterns while improving model robustness. The EfficientNet-B0 model—utilizing Geometric & Color augmentation and selected through stratified 3-fold cross-validation—achieved optimal classification performance on cropped images. Despite constraints posed by class imbalance and a limited dataset size, this work confirms the viability of deep learning for automating rabies diagnosis. The proposed method enables fast and reliable detection with significant potential for further optimization. An online tool was deployed to facilitate practical access, establishing a framework for future medical imaging applications. This research underscores the potential of optimized deep learning models to transform rabies diagnostics and improve public health outcomes.

title:
Study of the Switching Dynamics of Ta
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>5</sub>
                    -Based RRAM Cells
authors: Usman Isyaku Bature, Ali Alzahrani, Haider Abbas, Faisal Bashir, Muhammad Sani Yahya, Furqan Zahoor
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Resistive random-access memory (RRAM) stands out as a highly promising memory technology due to its outstanding performance characteristics. However, comprehensive modeling and understanding of its resistive switching mechanisms remain key challenges. This study investigates the resistive switching behavior of a Ta/Ta<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf>/Pt RRAM device through a combination of experimental characterization and multiphysics simulation using COMSOL. The fabricated cell exhibits robust and repeatable bipolar resistive switching, attributed to the voltage-induced formation and rupture of conductive filaments (CFs). The finite element model incorporates field-driven oxygen vacancy migration, quantum tunneling conduction, and self-consistent Joule heating effects to capture the switching dynamics. Simulation results reveal a tunneling-dominated SET process and a thermally assisted RESET mechanism, aligning closely with experimental I-V characteristics. The strong agreement between simulation and measurement highlights the model’s predictive accuracy and its effectiveness in guiding RRAM design optimization, particularly in terms of biasing strategies and thermal reliability.

title: DynaGNN-3D: Dynamic Graph Neural Networks with Velocity-Aware Edge Reconfiguration for Real-Time LiDAR-Based 3D Object Detection in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Vijayakumar Venganti, Seetaram Rao Rayarao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for LiDAR-based 3D object detection have achieved strong performance on autonomous vehicle benchmarks, yet existing approaches construct static k-nearest-neighbor (k-NN) graphs that ignore object motion dynamics between frames. This static edge assignment limits both detection accuracy for fast-moving objects and inference efficiency, since background point clusters receive the same computational budget as foreground objects. We present DynaGNN-3D, a novel architecture featuring velocity-aware dynamic edge reconfiguration: graph edges are re-drawn per inference frame using per-point velocity estimates, concentrating connectivity within moving object clusters while sparsifying background regions. DynaGNN-3D incorporates a dedicated velocity prediction head whose output feeds back into edge construction, enabling temporally coherent 3D bounding box estimation. We also propose an anchor-free center-based detection head compatible with dynamic graph topology. Experiments on nuScenes and Waymo Open Dataset benchmarks demonstrate that DynaGNN-3D achieves 58.5% mAP and NDS of 63.2 on nuScenes validation, surpassing CenterPoint by +2.5 mAP and +3.6 NDS, while reducing inference latency by 26% (38 ms → 28 ms) on an NVIDIA A4000 GPU. INT8 TensorRT deployment achieves 90 FPS with only −0.8% mAP degradation, demonstrating practical edge deployability. Ablation studies confirm that dynamic edge reconfiguration and the velocity head each contribute independently to detection quality.

title: Results of Incorporating the World Skills Professional Standard and Assessment Standard into the Curriculum
authors: Munkhjargal Nergui, Ganbat Danaa
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This study explores the integration of the WorldSkills Mechanical Engineering-CAD professional standard into the CAD-II course curriculum within the Technical Vocational Education Program for Industrial Equipment Maintenance and Operation. The training process was redesigned to align with this standard, and its effectiveness was evaluated through the results of the Mongol Skills-2025 school-level competition and the CAD-II course assessments. Findings from these evaluations were used to inform and plan future research initiatives aimed at further enhancing the curriculum and student outcomes.

title: An Optimized Ressource Allocation and Recommendation Algorithm for Classroom Occupancy Management: A Case Study at Alioune Diop University of Bambey
authors: Youssou Kasse, Fatoumata Balde, Ndeye Penda Mbaye
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The continuous growth in student enrollment in public universities has significantly increased pressure on pedagogical infrastructures, particularly classroom availability. In Senegalese universities, such as Université Alioune DIOP (UAD), classroom allocation and timetable management are still largely manual, leading to frequent scheduling conflicts, inefficient space utilization, and course cancellations. This paper proposes an optimized resource allocation algorithm combined with a recommendation mechanism to ensure optimal classroom occupancy under multiple academic constraints. The proposed approach is based on greedy optimization principles and is formally validated using loop invariants. Unified Modeling Language (UML) diagrams are used to specify system behavior and structure, while algorithmic complexity analysis evaluates performance. The solution is embedded in a web-based platform to support academic administrators in making rapid, consistent, and conflict-free allocation decisions.

title: Dynamic SIC Ordering in Downlink NOMA With User-Dependent CSI Uncertainty
authors: Zainab Abdul Hassan, Asaad S. Daghal, Wafaa M. R. Shakir
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: This paper investigates how uncertainty in channel state information (CSI) affects the performance of a three-user downlink non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) system. Conventional NOMA designs rely on fixed successive interference cancellation (SIC) ordering based on ideal or perfectly known channel gains. This leads to severe performance degradation when CSI is imperfect. To address this, the paper proposes an estimation-aware, robust framework in which the SIC decoding sequence is dynamically determined according to estimated channel gains rather than assumed ideal ordering. This framework incorporates structured CSI uncertainty modeling to reduce ordering mismatch and mitigate residual interference accumulation along the SIC chain. Comprehensive Monte Carlo-based performance evaluations demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly enhances decoding stability, improves resilience against CSI distortion, and exhibits strong robustness in spectral efficiency and reliability metrics compared to conventional fixed-order systems. These results confirm that adapting the decoding hierarchy dynamically to estimated channel conditions substantially improves system robustness. Thus, the proposed design is well-suited for high-reliability, low-latency communication scenariosin (5G) and beyond network.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Breast Cancer Histopathological Image Classification
authors: Sanjana Rout, Priyanka Nayak, Prachi Anand, Prasant Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths that have been causing the highest number of deaths among women across the globe. The clinical gold standard used in the diagnosis of benign and malignant breast tissues is the analysis of histopathological images which is time-consuming and the results are prone to inter-observer bias. This paper will provide a comparative analysis of deep learning networks to perform automated binary classification of breast cancer histopathology images over 40x, 100x, 200x and 400x magnifications on the publicly available BreaKHis dataset. There were four convolutional neural network architectures that were explored: EfficientNetB1, ResNet50V2, VGG16 and a Custom CNN baseline. All the models were trained and assessed in the same experimental environment to make the comparison fair. Among experimental results, EfficientNetB1 attained the highest overall performance with 90% accuracy, 89% precision, 88% recall, and 93% F1-score. VGG16 achieved 87% accuracy at 87.2% precision and 88% recall and ResNet50V2 reached 86% accuracy, 83% precision, and 89% recall. The Custom CNN had a competitive baseline performance of 87% accuracy and 85% precision. Overall, EfficientNetB1 demonstrated the most balanced trade-off between classification performance and model resilience in breast cancer histopathological image classification.

title: Detection of Leaf Health in Carissa carandas Using Deep Learning
authors: Patatri Acharya, Sudipta Acharya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: In the agriculture sector, Carissa carandas are highly valued for their significant application in the making of jams, pickles, and traditional medicine. Unfortunately, many diseases that affect plants and may reduce their productivity become an obstacle for their growth. Automated diagnosis of the health of Carissa carandas leaves is needed to minimize this threat. This research offers a solution based on machine learning algorithms aimed at diagnosing the health status of leaves of Carissa carandas through photographs. A dataset with 715 leaf images was obtained from the Mendeley Data website. Data preprocessing was done by rescaling the images, thus normalizing the pixel values. The data preprocessing process was accomplished by rescaling the images, hence ensuring normalization of the image pixels. Data augmentation also took place in order to enlarge the training dataset. The three models of pre-trained convolutional neural network based architectures used include VGG16, VGG19 and ResNet50 architectures that were trained on the augmented preprocessed image dataset. The experiments were done using 80:20 train-test ratio split. The best performing model is the VGG19 architecture which yielded an accuracy of 82.39%, followed by ResNet50 model yielding an accuracy of 81.69%. The worst performing model is the VGG16 model which had an accuracy of 80.28%.

title: Survey on Privacy-Aware Federated Learning Frameworks for Energy Systems within Smart Grids
authors: Damilola Agboola, Tamador Mohaidat, Md Rahat Kader Khan, Samiul Islam Niloy, Zhiqi Niu, Kasem Khalil
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: The global transition towards Smart Grids (SGs) has brought about significant advancements in energy management and efficiency. To sustain these advancements, robust data-driven models trained on large-scale energy datasets are essential. However, the bidirectional flow of data and increasing connectivity within SG infrastructures have introduced cybersecurity and privacy vulnerabilities, raising concerns about the protection of sensitive consumer and operational information as well as regulatory compliance. Federated Learning (FL), a decentralized machine learning paradigm, has emerged as a promising solution to these challenges, enabling models to be trained at the edge while ensuring the accuracy of a global model. Although numerous studies have explored the application of FL in the Internet of Things (IoT), this paper presents a survey of most recent and impactful literature on the application of FL algorithms within the SG domain. Our analysis integrates findings from key studies, categorizing the primary applications of FL to address common challenges such as communication overhead and statistical heterogeneity arising from non-independently and identically distributed (non-IID) data. We also explore promising avenues for future research in FL and anomaly detection. Furthermore, we examine various FL architectures, threat models, privacy-preserving mechanisms, and the gaps in existing datasets and protocols.

title: ASL Alphabet Recognition Using a Deep Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Seerat Singla, Deepak Kumar, G Sunil, Sonal Malhotra, Kamlesh Kukreti, Aryan Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: The handicapped, especially those with hearing or speech impairments, are definitely not able to communicate without using American Sign Language (ASL). As because the automation for ASL alphabets helps the interpreter sign to communicate flawlessly with the handicapped person, recognition for alphabets is lately receiving much attention. In this research, recognition for ASL alphabets is attempted by employing a specific CNN architecture that is given below. Next is the convolutional model for maximizing both the accuracy level for each letter classification and processing speed for resulting outputs; as if to effectively identify all 26 letters for ASL with high precision. Two methods for fortifying our theories and overcoming possible overfitting are using data augmentation approaches in this approach. After applying our model for training and testing with a considerable amount of alphabet image instances for ASL, we reached an incredible accuracy rate of 94%. By conducting a considerable amount of experiments, authors have recommended a CNN model that is fully trained and provides higher levels of accuracy compared to other bugaboo models depicting high precision, recall, and F1 measure for all classes within the alphabet. Lower error rate and capability for recognizing all varying inputs as a pointer for indicating that our model is competent for using within real-time recognition for alphabet recognition within ASL. Its light model also promises a speedy processing time as required for embedded systems and mobile applications. here is certainly a precedent set by our research for those requiring recognition using alphabet letters within sign language and advancing their recognition system and combating communication barriers for all handicapped or even physically fit and abled folks for sure.

title: A Comparison of Deep Learning and Machine Learning Models for Toddler Autism Spectrum Disorder Screening
authors: Dipak Mishra, Rakesh Ranjan Kumar, Subhadra Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder(ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that affects communication, social interaction, and behavior of a toddler[3]. For prompt intervention and better results, early detection is essential[2]. Ten behavioral characteristics (A1–A10), demographic characteristics (Age, Sex), medical history (Jaundice, Family ASD),and a binary class label (YES/NO) are all included in the dataset of toddler screening records used in this study[1]. We assess and contrast a variety of classification models, including both sophisticated techniques (Artificial Neural Networks, Convolutional Neural Networks, XGBoost) and conventional machine learning algorithms (Naive Bayes, Decision Tree, SVM, Random Forest, Logistic Regression)[4] - [7]. Performance measures like Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and log loss are some of the very common evaluation measures used to evaluate performance[9]. According to the experimental data analysis results, Random Forest[11] and ANN shows the highest accuracy and robustness, while ensemble and deep learning techniques achieve superior predictive performance. This particular work emphasizes the potential of machine learning in early diagnosis of ASD among the toddlers and advances automated screening for ASD[8]. In order to improve children’s long-term developmental outcomes, early detection of autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is essential[3]. In this work, we assess several machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models using a combined dataset of behavioral questionnaire answers and demographic information for toddler autism screening. Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Decision Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost[5], Artificial Neural Networks (ANN)[6], and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN)[7] are among the models used. Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, ROC-AUC, and Log Loss were used to assess performance[9]. With an accuracy of 98.12 % , the CNN model outperformed the other ML models, but XGBoost and Random Forest showed superior predictive ability[5], [11].

title: Gait Analysis for Disease Detection using CNN and LSTM
authors: Anil Kumar Meher, Rajesh Kumar Ojha, Gopi Krishna Panda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Gait analysis focuses on in which way people are walking by analyzing different factors such that distance of each step, average distance between each step, the speed or velocity of walking style, the position or angle of everyone’s feet as well as hips, the movement of pelvis and the bend structure of knee position at the time of walking. It is also focusing on the force generated by the feet while walking with different styles. From these factors we can find out the health issues with muscles, nervous systems or bones and use to diagnose them and also keeping records of all the factors affected by walking. Gait analysis is here mostly dealing with the nervous system or the musculoskeletal system for identifying the major or minor health issues. Generally traditional gait analysis is more costly and requires special kind of sensors which are very difficult for the persons to use. In this paper, we introduce an AI-based method that uses deep learning as well as computer vision and to learn walking styles and predict possible health issues. Our approach uses OpenPose to detect key body points from walking videos and combines a hybrid CNN-LSTM model to understand both the spatial as well as time-based gait features. The method is tested by us using the GAVD dataset, which we made more realistic by adding simulated abnormal walking patterns. Our system was able to correctly classify normal, limping, and shuffling walks with 92% accuracy. We also developed a real-time system using a Flask API and a React.js interface for showing results. For both clinics and remote healthcare environments these are used as a low-cost, non-invasive gait analysis tools.

title: A Mirror-Gate SONOS Memory with Program Operation by Hot Carrier Injection
authors: Jingsong Peng, Yang Ding, Kegang Zhang, Ning Wang, Binghan Li, Shijin Ding
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Owing to the operation based on Fowler-Nordheim (F-N) tunneling, the Silicon-oxide-nitride-oxide-silicon (SONOS) memory needs to surmount the challenges such as high operation voltage and slow programing time. Herein, we proposed a SONOS cell based on mirror-gate profile via hot carrier injection (HCI) program. The operation voltage and programing time can drop to 7 V and 3-6 μs, respectively. And the programing current can be few nanoamperes, indicating a low consumption of programing power. The threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>) window between erase and program can read up to 2 V, which is sufficient for various non-volatile memories (NVMs).

title: Toward Real-time 4D Animation for Multiple Surveillance Cameras with AI Vision
authors: Chao-Lieh Chen, Yi-Hong Zhan, Chang-Hui Chang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Multimedia (ICSM)
abstract: We present a near real-time 4D animation framework for transcribing large-scale surveillance environments using multiple camera inputs. This system is designed to address key challenges in intelligent surveillance, including privacy protection, low-latency processing, efficient data transmission, scalability across multiple areas, and seamless integration of heterogeneous AI vision models. Privacy is safeguarded by substituting personal imagery with 3D virtual assets. To reduce latency and bandwidth, structured text is streamed per frame to describe object states, eliminating the need to transmit raw 2D or 3D image frames. Multi-camera setups are employed to expand spatial coverage, while object trajectories and interactions are reconstructed through multi-model fusion involving deep learning, vision-language models (VLMs), and traditional computer vision techniques. The resulting structured scene representations support virtual and physical object interactions. A two-camera case study running on a commodity PC with an RTX 4070 Ti GPU achieves 18-25 FPS with only WebSocket-level transmission delay, demonstrating the system’s feasibility for real-time browser-based visualization and control. This work offers a scalable and extensible solution for intelligent multi-camera surveillance applications.

title: Addressing Electroplating Challenges in Advanced Packaging Using Modern Methods
authors: Andreas Möller, Alexander Gaiduk
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Advanced packaging (AP) is critical to sustaining Moore’s Law, driven by heterogeneous integration, high-aspect-ratio (HAR) structures such as through-silicon vias (TSVs), redistribution layers (RDLs), and hybrid bonding. However, electroplating faces challenges from additive complexity, contamination, and process integration. This paper presents modern solutions: LED-based UV/Vis spectroscopy for stable, low-maintenance chemical monitoring, and HPLC-driven byproduct detection to optimize bleed-and-feed strategies. These innovations enhance process control, reduce waste by up to 30%, and ensure defect-free copper fill, enabling scalable, reliable AP manufacturing.

title: High Precision Table Structure Recognition Using Transformer-Based Detection: Fine-Tuning TATR on Large-Scale Cropped Dataset
authors: Mohamed Touati, Mohamed Wiem Mkaouer, Laurent Nana, Sultan Frikha
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a high-performance system for table structure recognition based on a fine-tuned Table Transformer (TATR), an adaptation of a DETR-style transformer architecture. The model integrates a lightweight ResNet-18 backbone for feature extraction, positional encoding to preserve spatial information, a transformer encoder–decoder for object query processing, and a prediction module composed of a classification head and a three-layer multi-layer perceptron for bounding box regression. To enhance its performance the model undergoes fine-tuning through a massive dataset which contains more than 835,000 table images that have been labeled with row and column and cell span and header information. The assessment framework for evaluation depends on Standard metrics which use Intersection over Union (IoU) and Average Precision (AP) at different threshold levels and Average Recall (AR) to perform assessments. The proposed method achieves strong results, with AP@[IoU=0.50:0.95] reaching 0.971, AP@0.50 achieving 0.998, and AP@0.75 reaching 0.995. The research outcomes show performance which approaches state-of-the-art levels because the system maintains stable precision when testing different IoU values and verifies its effectiveness with objects that range from tiny to large sizes. The paper presents a comparative evaluation of recent transformer-based table structure recognition methods while also describing the practical aspects of deploying these systems and identifying future research opportunities in this domain.

title: Feature-Based Machine Learning for Real-Time Detection of Malicious QR and NFC Content
authors: Eisa Mohammed Al-Mannai, Nabil Litayem, Abdelhak Belhi, Mustafa al Samara
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Quick Response (QR) codes and Near Field Communication (NFC) tags are widely used in payments, transportation, retail, authentication, and public services. However, their convenience introduces a critical security challenge: users often interact with embedded links or actions without visibility into the underlying destination or content. This paper presents a practical web-based security scanning platform that performs pre-interaction analysis of QR and NFC content and classifies associated URLs as safe, suspicious, or malicious. The system integrates a browser-based frontend, a backend analysis service, feature-based URL processing, and Machine Learning (ML) classification, while external threat-intelligence services (e.g., VirusTotal) are used as a secondary verification layer. A dataset of 20,000 URLs is transformed into structured feature vectors using a standardized tri-value scoring scheme and used to evaluate nine ML models under a stratified 80/20 train-test split. Results show that XGBoost achieves the best performance with 90.15% accuracy, outperforming Random Forest (89.98%) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (89.53%), while also achieving the highest precision, recall, and F1-score values. The prototype is further validated in realistic QR/NFC usage scenarios using Android-based browser testing, with iOS/device-policy and hardware compatibility limitations documented. The results demonstrate that proactive, browser-accessible screening can effectively enhance security in everyday QR/NFC interactions.

title: Why Do Admission Attempts Fail? An Analysis of Bangladeshi University Admission Applicants Using ML and XAI
authors: Anwar Hossain Efat, S.M. Mahedy Hasan, Minhaz F. Zibran, Farjana Z. Eishita
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Each year, over one million students in Bangladesh compete for limited undergraduate seats at universities, with intense competition for the 53 public universities where about 36 candidates vie for each seat. This fierce rivalry often results in unmet expectations, leading to depression and other psychological challenges for many students. Contributing factors include educational background, family circumstances, distractions, unproductive activities, and socio-economic conditions.This study aims to identify the primary reasons behind unsuccessful admission attempts and evaluate the impact of these factors using data from 600 students across 15 public and private universities. Questionnaires were used to gather data, which was analyzed with Machine Learning (ML) techniques. Among various models, the proposed Feature-Tuned Support Vector Machine (FT-SVM) achieved a 96.47% accuracy in predicting admission outcomes based on preparatory activities.To enhance model interpretability, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques were employed, highlighting critical factors that influence outcomes. These findings offer actionable insights for students, helping them optimize their preparation and improve their chances of success in future admission tests.

title: Optimizing Plasma Ignition in ICP Reactors for Electronegative Chemistries at Low Pressure
authors: Wei Su, Jay Young, Lian Ye, Hao Lu, Saisai Zhou, Song Guo, Zheng Tao, Kaidong Xu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Stable ignition of inductively coupled plasmas (ICP) is essential for advanced etch processes but becomes unreliable at very low pressures and in electronegative chemistries. We studied ICP ignition in Cl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>, and Ar at 2 mTorr with 350 W source power. Baseline tests showed robust ignition in Ar but frequent mis-strikes in Cl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>, consistent with strong electron attachment. Three strategies were compared: (1) bias-assisted ignition to enhance local capacitive fields, (2) striking at higher source power followed by ramp-down, and (3) adding a small Ar fraction to reactive gases. All methods improved ignition probability, with Ar addition provided the most robust startup at constant low source power. Results are interpreted using a reduced-field (E/N) threshold picture with attachment-limited net ionization, clarifying practical guidelines for reliable ignition in semiconductor etch applications.

title: ARIA: An Agentic Release Intelligence Architecture for Autonomous End-to-End Mobile DevOps - From Build Prediction to Incident Resolution
authors: Satyanarayana Gudimetla, Suresh Gangula, R Adinarayana, Chandrakanth Challa, Sudhakar Vunnam, Sudhamadhuri Buhyavarapu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Enterprise mobile delivery pipelines remain fragmented: build, CI, release, deployment, and incident response operate as disconnected stages, with post-deployment learning rarely propagating upstream. We present ARIA (Agentic Release Intelligence Architecture), a multi-agent architecture whose central contribution is the ARIA Feedback Protocol (AFP)—a confidence-gated, time-decayed mechanism providing two complementary benefit pathways: (i) per-incident observability acceleration that enriches diagnosis context for every incident, and (ii) multi-incident feedback accumulation that improves upstream test selection over time. ARIA coordinates five specialized agents through a shared provenance substrate and a graduated autonomy model whose governance components are mapped to NIST AI Risk Management Framework practices. A controlled simulation study parameterized from the public CI/CD histories of Signal Android, Firefox for Android (Fenix), and Wikipedia Android reports baseline, ablation, and sensitivity analyses over 30 stochastic replications. The per-incident mechanism yields a statistically significant MTTR reduction from 51.0 to 44.6 minutes (−12.6%, p<0.001, d=0.86) and governance improvement from 8.3 to 9.1 (+10.1%, p<0.001, d=1.30). The multi-incident mechanism (DDR +1.0%, RIR −5.8%) shows favorable trends; a post-hoc power analysis determines that ≈670 observations (≈22 applications) are needed for 80% statistical power, providing a concrete sample-size target for future production studies. An offline replay against 30 held-out CI failure events yields 83.3% prediction accuracy (vs. 60.0% baseline), supporting simulation fidelity.

title: Explainability of Large Language Models in Healthcare Information Systems: A Review
authors: Robin Sullivan, Nelly Elsayed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have produced unprecedented results in areas such as language understanding, machine translation, and question and answering. However, a major challenge with adopting these and other forms of deep learning models into information systems is their opaque nature. Answering the question of how the model drew its conclusions is imperative for adoption in many disciplines, particularly in healthcare and medicine, finance, and legal, where transparency, trust, and regulatory compliance are critical. This study investigates approaches being used to explain transformer-based LLMs independent of any particular model that supports different organizations in improving governance, trust, and accountability while fostering these models into decision-making systems.

title: HeteroSTA3D: A CPU-GPU Heterogeneous Static Timing Analysis Engine for 3D ICs
authors: Yuhao Ji, Zizheng Guo, Zuodong Zhang, Yibo Lin, Bei Yu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Three-dimensional integrated circuits (3D ICs) based on hybrid bonding have emerged as a key technology for sustaining performance scaling beyond the limits of conventional transistor shrinkage. However, the timing analysis of 3D ICs introduces challenges absent in two-dimensional (2D) designs, including combinatorial explosion in multi-corner analysis across heterogeneous technology nodes, inter-die parasitic modeling for hybrid bonding interconnects, and cross-die critical path extraction through a unified timing graph. No existing static timing analysis (STA) engine addresses these challenges natively. In this paper, we present HeteroSTA3D, the first CPU-GPU heterogeneous STA engine for 3D ICs. Targeting hybrid-bonding-based face-to-face two-die integration, HeteroSTA3D supports dual-die Liberty library management with independent process nodes, configurable bonding terminal parasitic modeling, multi-corner analysis across per-die PVT combinations, and end-to-end GPU-accelerated cross-die critical path search. HeteroSTA3D is released as an embeddable shared library with both industry-standard file formats and a zero-overhead flattened heterogeneous API for seamless integration into GPU-accelerated EDA flows. Experimental results show that GPU acceleration reduces runtime compared to CPU-only execution. A case study integrating HeteroSTA3D into a timing-driven 3D placement flow further demonstrates its effectiveness, achieving improved timing with affordable runtime overhead. HeteroSTA3D is freely available for academic use at https://heterosta3d.pkueda.org.cn/.

title: AI in Requirements Engineering, Software Design, and Software Architecture: A Systematic Mapping Study (2020–2026)
authors: Ritesh Ruparel, Vigneshwarr Venkatesan, Vinod Bottu, Sai M Dasari, Suraj Vangala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: AI support for requirements engineering, software design, and software architecture has shifted from niche NLP tooling to widely used large language model (LLM) assistants. Teams now use AI to draft and summarize requirements, propose UML-style design artifacts, and create or review architecture decision records (ADRs). The value is real, but so are risks such as invented constraints, inconsistency across artifacts, missing nonfunctional requirements (NFRs), and broken traceability after change. This paper reports a systematic mapping study of work from 2020 to early 2026. We describe a reproducible search and screening protocol across IEEE Xplore, ACM Digital Library, Scopus, and Google Scholar (as a supplement). We map 20 primary studies into a task taxonomy and summarize inputs, outputs, datasets, and evaluation methods. Results show heavy use of small public proxies and human rating rubrics, with limited evidence on end-to-end impact, consistency checks, and industrial cost and risk. We provide actionable mitigations and a research agenda focused on benchmarks, traceability metrics, and realistic evaluations.

title: Evolutionary Osmotic Migration for Real-Time Urban Traffic Control: Balancing Delays and Power in Multi-Tier Networks
authors: Rishit Kamboj, Ayush Goyal, Ritesh Ranjan, Benazir Neha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Many smart city systems now depend on a lot of resource-limited devices to monitor, evaluate, and improve services of a city, like traffic control, etc. These devices require real-time data acquisition and control, but due to high latency, too many computations and network congestions, the devices run into problems. This study proposes a solution which uses an osmotic offloading method to reduce these problems by dynamically controlling the movement of microservices and their operations across different edge-fog-cloud layers. This study uses genetic algorithm which optimizes this distribution of tasks at cloud-fog-edge. It ensures that the devices which need computing power and require low latency are handled at the best layer accordingly. The solution reduces latency, balances the energy usage and eases network congestion by breaking large applications into smaller parts. It then distributes these smaller parts in an apt way across edge-fog-cloud layers. The simulations of the algorithm shows that the osmotic offloading based on genetic algorithm greatly improves average latency and energy efficiency.

title: The Hidden Complexity of Mental Health: Multi-Entropy Analysis of Response Patterns in Depression Severity Assessment
authors: Ayesha Siddika, Ahnaf Atif Rafi, Maria Jahan Noon, Md Junayed Hossain, Ashraful Islam, Sanzar Adnan Alam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) is generally used to determine the level of depression, and clinical decisions are usually made based on the overall score. Nevertheless, there can be significant variation in the response pattern of individuals who have the same score, indicating that there are variations in the organization of symptoms. To overcome this drawback, this paper presents an entropy-based computation system that describes PHQ-9 responses in a way that is not limited by a score measure. Responses to individual items are fused with information-theoretic quantities, such as Shannon entropy, Sample entropy, Permutation entropy, and Multiscale entropy, to form a more detailed feature space to classify depression severity. The proposed framework significantly increased the accuracy of the sum-score baseline in fivefold stratified cross-validation of the existing data, with the Random Forest adding 18.0 percentage points to the accuracy of 81.2 percent, reaching 99.2 percent. Statistical analysis also shows that the relationship between response entropy and the severity of depression is not linear, where entropy is at its highest when depression is moderate and reduced with severe depression. A potentially useful screening signal in this data set was observed to be an entropy threshold (0.439). These results suggest the complexity of the response patterns as a concomitant measure of digital depression.

title: A Case Study on the Feasibility of Solar PV Integrated Energy Storage System for Modern Distribution Grid Networks
authors: Swati Sharma, Mohammad Amir, Musa Bashir, Mahipal Bukya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: In renewable rich power distribution networks, the intermittent nature of temperature and solar irradiance can compromise voltage stability and distribution grid reliability. This study presents a 100 kW photovoltaic (PV) array modelling and strategic simulation for next-generation 25 kV grid using a three-level, three-phase voltage source converter (VSC) and DC-DC boost converter. The incremental conductance method and integral integrator are designed with maximum power point tracking (MPPT) using MATLAB 2024a. The MPPT algorithm under dynamic temperature conditions provides an optimal power extraction. The simulation results confirm the robustness of the proposed scheme and validate the output power at unity power factor, achieving 100.39 kW with stable DC link voltage regulation. A three-branched PV-storage modelling-based framework comprises of robust codes for ensuring safety, interoperability and scalability. Furthermore, this study analyses the comparative benefits of distributed energy storage designs across residential scale applications. For evaluating the cost-benefit trade-offs and stability of the grid, this proposed method provides a pathway for the stakeholders to adopt a reliable, efficient and economical PV-energy storage systems that enhances grid resilience.
