title: Image-Based System for Forested Area Analysis and Optimal Path Computation
authors: Sunita G. Rawat, Aditya Gaikwad, Kashish Pawar, Tejas Mahakalkar, Vidhi Dhakate
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Environmental management depends a lot on monitoring the forested areas, but being able to assess them accurately is hard. Large-scale field experiments or special equipment is typically needed for such surveys. In this study, an image-based prototype is developed which is capable of passively detecting growth and development from a distance. Trees are being assessed to identify optimum paths and to assess the extent of the vegetation cover, making the technology for automatic forest analysis from satellite imagery a technology of great potential. The system estimates vegetated cover, extracts tree crowns using YOLOv8, and takes the least-cost route using a density-aware cost function and Dijkstra’s algorithm. This prototype is optimized for being light, modular, and scalable for a rapid initial forest evaluation.

title: A Survey on Recent Federated Learning Techniques in Healthcare: Challenges and Future Trends
authors: Rami Khoder, Khouloud Samrouth, Prathibha Prasad, Syed Arif Ali, Nader Bakir
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Deep learning algorithms typically require access to large volumes of raw data. However, in the medical field, such data cannot be shared due to privacy constraints. Consequently, medical AI-based systems use Federated Learning (FL), which enables the processing and training of models on sensitive data without requiring it to leave local devices. In this paper, we outline the very recent developments of FL in the medical field, particularly in medical oncology, imaging, and drug discovery. We conclude that with the advantages of processing data in a federated system, FL does encounter the federation opacity issue. This latter causes training data to be opaque to the stakeholders, thus creating a lack of transparency that can lead to data poisoning, lack of accountability to institutions, and novel types of vulnerabilities. In addition, in this paper, we highlight the ethical issues that need to be resolved, in particular data heterogeneity, and the privacy-utility trade-off. Without an ethical approach that focuses on improving access to healthcare for all patients, we will not see the benefits of the technological advancements that are in the field of FL.

title: RDSwinBTC: Radiomics-Guided Swin Transformer for Brain Tumor MRI Classification
authors: Sireesha Kakulla, Satish Kedari, Rajeswari Kavali, K Kishore Kumar, A Radhika, Kota Venkateswara Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of brain tumors from MRI requires models that capture both anatomical structure and subtle patterns of texture. Current deep learning methods primarily focus on visual features alone, ignoring radiomic descriptors, which encode statistical and structural properties of tumors. This work proposes RDSwinBTC, a radiomics-guided Swin Transformer that fuses hierarchical transformer features with handcrafted radiomic representations through an end-to-end fusion head. The integration of intensity, texture, and edge-based descriptors in one model improves feature discrimination and enhances robustness across tumor subtypes. Experimental results demonstrate superior accuracy and AUC compared to standard CNN and Transformer baselines, and the radiomic-aware hybrid architectures provide a more reliable pathway toward automated brain tumor classification in clinical workflows.

title: Smart Urban Transit Management System
authors: Milan Saju, Milan Vargheese, Saran Sankar, Tresa Maria Denny, Juby Mathew
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Urban public bus transportation systems often have problems because of fixed schedules, which leads to overcrowding and not using the fleet as well as it could be. This paper proposes a Smart Public Transport Scheduling System that employs a Quantile Long Short-Term Memory (QLSTM) model to predict passenger demand and dynamically adjust bus schedules. The proposed model simultaneously predicts four demand quantiles (Q10, Q50, Q90, Q99), reducing mean absolute prediction error by approximately 18% compared to standard LSTM point-forecasting baselines. An integrated load-factor decision engine classifies demand into four operational tiers and allocates supplementary buses during severe overcrowding (load factor > 1.2), reducing simulated peak-hour overcrowding incidents by up to 35%. The system is validated on both synthetic and real-world electronic ticketing datasets spanning over 200 time intervals and six bus routes. The results demonstrate that the quantile-based uncertainty bands cover 91% of actual demand observations, thereby proving the success of well-calibrated probabilistic forecasting. The proposed framework cuts the time it takes to respond to operational alerts and boosts fleet use by about 22% compared to static schedules. This shows that it is useful for making urban mobility more sustainable.

title: Enterprise Cloud AI Infrastructure for Agent-Centric Column-Aware Prompt Optimization for Data Tasks with LLMs
authors: Uman Ahmed Mohammed
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are used as an increasing number of enterprise applications involving reasoning over structured data (tables and databases) on cloud AI infrastructure. But naive few-shot prompt selection tends to disregard the underlying schema, causing lower accuracy and more hallucinations. The current paper introduces a column-conscious few-shot prompt optimization model, which utilizes the schema knowledge and a multi-objective search to sample, rank, and compiles demonstrations in structured data problems. Proposed system has tunable parameters (column selection, example selection, and prompts formatting) and optimizes them based on desired metrics (e.g., task accuracy and hallucination rate). Table question answering, report generation and analytics-assistant tasks experiments demonstrate convergence speed and higher performance of column-aware optimization than random selection and best-of-one/best-of-three baselines. Lastly, we are applying the framework as a part of an agent optimization platform, which enables automated adaptive prompt management of production cloud environments in enterprise.

title: A Decentralized Healthcare Records Management System built with Solidity Smart Contracts and React
authors: Pooja Singh, Vipin Kumar Kushwaha, Shiva Gupta, B. Sharan, Murari Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: The objective of this work is to present a decentralized healthcare records management system. The system is built on the Ethereum blockchain using Solidity smart contracts and a React-based web interface. And this system addresses security, integrity, and privacy limitations of traditional centralized electronic health record (EHR) systems. By enforcing the role-based access control, immutable storage of patient records, and transparent audit trails for all operations. A smart contract ‘HealthcareRecords’ manages patient data operations and provider authorization, while a MetaMask-integrated frontend enables secure interaction with the blockchain through an intuitive interface. The local Hardhat Ethereum network is used to deploy the proposed work, outcome of the prototype demonstrates a secure healthcare record creation and retrieval of the patient data. This local network demonstrating the strict access control, low gas consumptions and low latency, which are suitable for real usages in healthcare environments.

title: AI Governance in the Data Collection Phase of Building an AI Model
authors: K.Bennouk, D. Mahouachi, N. Ait Aali, Y. El Bouzekri El Idrissi, B. Sebai, A. Z. Faroukhi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: With the objective of ensuring a compliant, ethical, and secure building of an AI (Artificial intelligence) models, it becomes imperative to apply a governance layer during all phases related to the AI life cycle. The massive integration of AI models into existing information systems or the generation of new ones may generate serious impacts related to human well-being, societal and environmental impacts. To avoid bias and discrimination, misuse or dual use, and unintended consequences, there are significant calls for a systematic review of practices during the building of an AI model. In addition, the complexity of digital systems and their fast interactions with AI models represent a serious challenge to facing the high level of tactics and techniques used in diverse cyberattacks. The study aims to propose a fundamental understanding of AI governance relative to building AI models. It introduces a thorough comprehension of the existing ten AI principles in the literature. The position paper highlights major risks of AI models, underlying their impact, scope, and potential solutions. Additionally, the research proposes a holistic mapping between the AI principles and eight proposed stages of the AI life cycle. It presents ten main metrics to be assessed during the data collection stage before training the AI model. Empirical evaluations are conducted on three real-world datasets representing vulnerability records and operational logs. Results demonstrate a strong need for AI governance to avoid data drift, biased models, privacy infringements, and reduce AI risks during all phases of building any future model of prediction or classification.

title: Smart Room Automation System
authors: Siddhartha Srivastava, V Arvind, Rajarshi Mukhopadhyay, Priya B K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This paper aims for the implementation of Smart Room Automation System where it improves the energy efficiency, comfort and also security. The system works when a person enters the room, it is detected by ultrasonic sensor, then activates the other components accordingly. Temperature sensor is used for temperature reading and accordingly the fan is turned ON or OFF when compared to the current temperature and the predefined limits. The LDR sensor or photoresistor is used for lights to be turned ON if the room is dark or OFF if there is ample amount of light. A fingerprint module is used for opening the cupboard securely by the fingerprint authentication. An air purifier is also included with the temperature and humidity sensor, dust sensor, gas sensor and the filters such as Pre filter, HEPA filter and Carbon filter for air cleansing. The system is implemented with Arduino which gives practical and efficient solution for indoor automation.

title: Slice-Aware AoI Control for UAV-Assisted 5G and Beyond-5G Waste Hotspot Mapping Using Kernel Fused Density Updates
authors: Mohammed J. Alhasan, Karar H. Hussein
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Long-lived waste (e.g., plastics and slow-degrading residues) often accumulates into spatial hotspots, yet periodic IoT reporting can leave hotspot knowledge stale at the time municipal decisions are made. This paper proposes a slice-aware Age-of-Information (AoI) framework for UAV-assisted waste hotspot mapping that explicitly targets actionable freshness at the server. A UAV samples a waste-density proxy over a gridded area and generates an update only when either server-side staleness or measurement innovation exceeds a threshold, thereby avoiding redundant transmissions while preserving informative reports. Each generated update is then mapped to one of two logical service classes consistent with 5G/Beyond-5G slicing: hotspot-critical packets are prioritized on a URLLC-oriented slice, whereas routine traffic uses a routine mMTC/eMBB-oriented slice. At the server, sparse samples are fused through kernel-footprint aggregation to maintain a continuous waste-density field and support density- and freshness-driven municipal alerts. In a system-level simulation with slice-dependent delay, delivery, and energy characteristics, the proposed method improves end-of-run coverage from 0.6269 to 0.9443 and reduces RMSE from 0.1692 to 0.1328 over the full grid (0.3814 to 0.2826 over hotspots). It also reduces mean AoI from 547.27 s to 405.62 s and hotspot AoI P95 from 1010.0 s to 756.5 s, at the cost of higher communication energy. These results indicate that selective slice-aware AoI control can substantially reduce hotspot staleness and improve map fidelity in UAV-assisted waste monitoring.

title: Seismic Activity Prediction using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Usha C R, Yuvraj Singh, Swapnil Sinha, Jyotishko Boral, Riti Tripathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: A challenging problem in accurate in accurate prediction of seismic activity is caused due to complex spatial and temporal patterns of earthquakes. This study presents a machine learning based framework that focuses on forecasting earthquake magnitudes using integration od spatial clustering with time series deep learning models. This study utilizes the "All the Earthquakes Dataset(1990-2023)" which contains global seismic records, having the attributes such as latitude, longitude, depth, magnitude and temporal information. In previous years, initially spatial clustering techniques that included KMeans, DBSCAN, HDBSCAN and Gaussian Mixture models were evaluated, with miniBatch K-Means selected based on superior silhouette score and scalability to segment the dataset into different seismic zones. In order to capture GRU, LSTM were implemented and compared. Results experimentally demonstrated that deep learning models, particularly LSTM, achieved superior prediction performance that contains lower Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Squared Error(RMSE) across different training testing ratio. The final findings highlights the effectiveness of integrating spatial clustering with temporal sequence modeling that improves seismic forecasting and also improves risk assessment.

title: Toward Trustworthy Audit Automation: Explainable Meta-Learning for Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Antonin Chevalier Kremper, Malak Daher, Abdellah Ziade, Nancy Chendeb
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: With the increasing volume of transactions and sophistication of fraud schemes, automation of financial auditing presents significant challenges, especially conditions surrounding the need for explainability required by the regulatory environment. While there are many advances in machine learning providing excellent opportunities for improving methods of anomaly detection, there are two primary reasons that limit their adoption in practice: (1) no universally applicable optimal detection algorithm and (2) no method implementing to achieve needed transparency in a regulated audit setting.In this paper, we propose a contextual and explainable meta-learning framework for financial anomaly detection. Our approach combines multiple supervised and unsupervised detectors, within a unified meta-model architecture. Transactional data are enriched with contextual features, and model outputs are aggregated through supervised meta-learners that adaptively weight each detector based on the transaction profile. To ensure transparency, we integrate multi-level explainability using feature importance analysis, SHAP, and LIME, providing both global and local interpretations of model decisions.Experiments conducted on realistic financial fraud datasets demonstrate that the proposed meta-model significantly outperforms individual detectors and traditional aggregation methods, achieving an improvement of approximately 50% in the F2-score. The results also highlight the dominant contribution of supervised models, whereas unsupervised methods provide limited complementary value in this setting.Overall, this study shows that combining contextual feature engineering, meta-learning, and explainable AI enables more accurate, robust, and trustworthy anomaly detection systems, paving the way for their effective integration into real-world audit processes.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Renewable Energy: Enterprise Systems in Digital Regulation and International Sovereignty
authors: Alshaimaa Mohamed Mahmoud Hassan, Alhanof Almutairi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper explores the role of artificial intelligence (AI) and enterprise systems in renewable energy governance, with a focus on digital sovereignty. It adopts an interdisciplinary approach combining information systems and political science. Technologically, it examines how enterprise systems enable AI-driven optimization and regulatory compliance in smart energy infrastructures. A comparative analysis of Egypt and Saudi Arabia illustrates distinct national strategies: modular integration in Egypt and centralized platforms in Saudi Arabia. Politically, the study introduces the concepts of Collaborative Sovereignty and Functional Digital Sovereignty Institutions to explain how states engage in digital governance while preserving autonomy. The findings highlight the need for integrated technical and institutional frameworks to support effective, sovereign, and globally connected clean energy transitions.

title: Enhancing Cognitive Load Assessment Using AI Models
authors: Chethana B, K Aditya Shastry, Harini N, Ramya S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Accurate measurement of cognitive load is essential and needed to explain human mental effort and improve performance in various fields, including driving, education, and decision-making. The traditional single-modality methods, such as electroencephalographic (EEG) analysis, are often simply not sufficient to capture the multidimensional nature of mental workload. In this regard, the present study presents a multimodal and explainable artificial-intelligence (AI) model, which combines physiological and behavioural measures, such as EEGs, functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), eye-tracking, and driving efficiency, to achieve a strong prediction of cognitive load. The architecture uses one dimensional convolutional layers (Conv1D) and gated recurrent units (GRU) to ensure that a temporal dynamics and cross modality interdependencies are captured. The pre-processing phases include data normalisation, the balance between the classes and generation of sequences, which ensures that the input to the model is of high quality. Empirical analysis demonstrates that the multimodal fusion construct achieves a reasonable accuracy of around 79 per cent and provides interpretable predictions; explainable-AI (XAI) analyses outline modality-specific and time-dependent feature interactions. In addition, the system can also help to monitor the periods of cognitive overload, thus leading to the future incorporation of adaptive feedback mechanisms. The findings indicate the potential of explainable multimodal learning in transparent user-focused cognitive monitoring. Future studies will aim at trying to make real time deployment extended, the generalisation more accurate and developing adaptive feedback strategies under high cognitive load conditions.

title: Investigation of Channel Stress in Monolithic CFET with Different SiGe Sacrificial Layers for A7 Technology Node
authors: Lei Cao, Jie Xu, Qingkun Li, Yuzhe Yu, Jiaxin Yao, Libin Liu, Zhaohao Zhang, Qingzhu Zhang, Kun Luo, Kun Yang, Zhenhua Wu, Xiaolei Wang, Huaxiang Yin
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: In this paper, to investigate the channel stress of the top NFET and bottom PFET in monolithic complementary field-effect transistor (mCFET), the integrated process and the stress engineering are simulated by TCAD method. The results show that the Ge concentration in the SiGe sacrificial layer (SL) increases from 10% to 40%, and the channel tensile stress of the top NFET can increase by 3.49X. However, the bottom PFET requires SiGe SL with lower Ge concentration to promote the formation of channel compressive stress. Meanwhile, by increasing the thickness of SiGe SL on the top NFET, the channel tensile stress is further optimized, which also provides a key path for continuously improving the performance of mCFET.

title: Real-Time Intrusion Detection in Fog Computing Environments Using Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Hamza H. Sulimani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and edge computing, fog computing has emerged as a key paradigm for enabling low-latency, distributed data processing. However, this decentralized architecture also introduces significant cybersecurity vulnerabilities. In this paper, we propose a machine learning-based framework for real-time intrusion detection tailored to fog computing environments. The system employs lightweight classification models deployed at edge nodes to detect malicious activities with minimal computational overhead and response delay. Experimental evaluations on benchmark intrusion detection datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves promising results, with the Random Forest model attaining the highest performance 89.3% accuracy, 99.7% precision, 76.2% recall, and an F1-score of 0.8639 – followed closely by Gradient Boosting and XGBoost classifiers. These results confirm the effectiveness of our approach in maintaining high detection accuracy while optimizing latency and resource efficiency, making it suitable for real-world fog computing scenarios.

title: Study of Surface Treatment Impacting High-K Film Properties with ALD Method
authors: Yanwen Luo, Xiaojian Qiao, Wenhui Yang, Mengyu Fu, Jiaheng Feng, Yingming Liu, Hao Deng
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: To achieve greater threshold voltage (Vt) tunability in advanced logic chips, High-K dipole process has been adopted in HKMG (High-K Metal Gate) loop. The surface treatment before and after atomic layer deposition (ALD) loop plays vital important role in impacting High-K film properties. The paper discussed the influence of pre/post O3 surface treatment on the uniformity, growth per cycle (GPC) of La2O3 thin-film. The result shows that O3 pretreatment could effectively improve the uniformity of La2O3 thin-film. Secondly, proper pulse/purge time is important for controlling the GPC. Thirdly, the post treatment of O3 is benefit for impeding the water absorption in atmosphere.

title: Evaluating the Efficacy of Microsoft Teams as an Online Learning Platform: A Case Study in a Philippine State University
authors: Julius Cesar O. Mamaril, Jerry C. Diaz, Clark Kim C. Castro
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic profoundly disrupted educational systems worldwide, compelling higher educational institutions to transition rapidly to online learning modalities. At Pangasinan State University (PSU), a Philippine state university, Microsoft Teams was adopted as the primary learning management system (LMS) to facilitate both synchronous and asynchronous instruction. This study evaluates the perceived usability and user acceptance of Microsoft Teams among N = 162 PSU stakeholders (118 students and 44 faculty members) using data collected during the first quarter of 2022 (Q1 2022), a period marking the transition phase of online learning in the Philippines. A dual-methodology approach was employed: the System Usability Scale (SUS) as a Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) instrument (Cronbach's α = 0.84) and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) as an information systems adoption framework (Cronbach's α = 0.88). TAM was applied in a post-usage context, theoretically grounded in Bhattacherjee's Expectation-Confirmation Model (ECM) of information systems continuance, which legitimizes post-adoption measurement of Perceived Usefulness (PU) and Perceived Ease of Use (PEOU) as predictors of continuance intention. The Likelihood to Recommend (LTR) and Overall Experience (OE) metrics were incorporated to capture post-usage satisfaction on a 0-10 scale. Findings reveal that both SUS (Mean = 66.83) and TAM (Mean = 67.15) yielded equivalent below-average scores (CGS grade: C). The consumption platform (laptop vs. smartphone) was the primary differentiating factor in user experience—a finding attributed to a 'Mobile-Desktop Mismatch' in features availability and interface design. Significant interaction effects involving user role, gender, and age were identified, including a 'Reliability Anxiety' pattern among female students. Regression models explained 20.1–34.7% of variance in LTR and OE (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.201–0.347), confirming that contextual factors beyond TAM constructs substantially shape user experience in resource-constrained Philippine higher education settings.

title: Extreme Learning Machine Based System for DDoS Attacks Detections on IoMT Devices
authors: Nelly Elsayed, Lily Dzamesi, Zag ElSayed, Murat Ozer
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: The Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) represents a paradigm shift in the healthcare sector, enabling the interconnection of medical devices, sensors, and systems to enhance patient monitoring, diagnosis, and management. The rapid evolution of IoMT presents significant benefits to the healthcare domains. However, there is a rapid increase in distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks on the IoMT networks due to several vulnerabilities in the IoMT-connected devices, which negatively impact patients’ health and can even lead to deaths. Thus, in this paper, we aim to save lives via investigating an extreme learning machine for detecting DDoS attacks on IoMT devices. The proposed approach achieves a high accuracy at a low implementation budget. Thus, it can reduce the implementation cost of the DDoS detection system, making the model capable of executing on the fog level.

title: Skin Cancer Prediction Using Deep Learning And Transfer Learning Techniques
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Riti Tripathi, Asha Latha C. R, Rahul Raj, Bhagyashree Kulkarni, Nandita Yambem
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: One of the fastest growing cancers in the world is skin cancer and also the most aggressive and life threatening cancer if it’s not detected early is melanoma. Traditionally the diagnosis was depended on experts examination of dermoscipic. To face these challenges, this study presents a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for accurate classification of benign and malignant skin lesions from dermoscopic images. State of the art transfer learning architectures, including ResNet50, MobileNetV2 and EfiicientNet50 using identical pre-procesing and augmentation strategies the model is evaluated. Results have shown that the presented CNN achieves higher balanced accuracy, lower false negative rates and faster inference that deeper pretrained model which makes it safer for early melanoma screening. Deployment of this system is done using a flask backend and a PyQt5 desktop application, enabling early real time diagnosis. This proposed system offers a solution in a practical and scalable approach for primary healthcare, tele dermatology and point of care screening

title: A lightweight CNN Model for Detecting Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) Cyber-attacks
authors: Dhabia Turki, Hind Muidh, Salha Mohammed, Muneera Fayhan, Wahiba Ismaiel, Omer Dawood
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Nowadays, the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) is a rapidly evolving technology with diverse applications. Its infrastructure comprises numerous sensors, controllers, industrial machines, and other devices connected to 5G networks and integrated with cloud computing for data storage. This technology faces challenges from adversaries attempting to infiltrate its networks to access its data and devices. Consequently, this study investigates a methodology that integrates Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for feature dimensionality reduction with a streamlined Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture. The objective is to detect cyber-attacks within the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) environment, utilizing the WUSTL-IIOT-2021 dataset to classify reconnaissance, command injection, denial of service (DoS), and backdoor attacks. Furthermore, the proposed method utilized evaluation metrics such as accuracy, precision, F1-Score, and Recall with a confusion matrix to measure the model's performance in detecting cyber-attacks. Through multi-class evaluation experiments, CNN achieved 100% accuracy for command injection, DoS attacks, and normal traffic, while achieving 92.2% for command injection and 58.1% for backdoor attacks. It also achieved binary classification based on the distinction between legitimate and attack traffic. The prediction accuracy reached 99.96% for testing with an error rate of 0.0149%, while the training achieved an accuracy of 99.88% with an error rate of 0.018%. Through the prediction results, the model confirms its ability to detect cyberattacks with a high percentage in datasets that have balanced distributions of their categories.

title: Motion Artefact’s Tolerant Hybrid Deep Learning-based Artery wall tracking
authors: Amit K Gupta, Sujay Shanthakumar, C Ajay, V Raj Kiran, Jayaraj Joseph
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Affordable point-of-care ultrasound technologies with automated capabilities for vessel analysis are integral to wide-spread adoption of assessing early vascular ageing, where A-mode technologies present simpler and cost-effective alternatives. Automating artery detection, a task that is intrinsic to such technologies, still suffers robustness issues due to low magnitude arterial pulsations, extreme heart rates, and motion artifacts in general, posing even higher challenge for A-mode 1D signals. In this work we present a hybrid deep-learning and classical signal processing approach for robust artery detection from A-mode ultrasound frames, that is feasible to deploy on an entry level edge-computing system. The method uniquely employs spatio-temporal features of the ultrasound frames recording arterial dynamics, by stacking temporally acquired 1D A-mode frames to form motion mode images on which detection is performed. It’s a hybrid method that uses deeplearning framework for the region of arterial walls and then classical signal processing for accurately locating the walls’ positions within the region, with a high resolution of 10 micrometer. We employ YOLO-based deep-learning models for the artery detection, benchmarking multiple variants for accuracy, model size, and inference speed. The models were deployed on an edge-computing system to ensure real-time feasibility. Among them, YOLO-11n achieved the best balance between performance and efficiency, with 2.6 million parameters, 83.8% precision, 93% recall, and an average inference time $\sim \mathbf{1 0 ~ m s}$ per image. This balanced attribute of 11n, makes it a potential choice for the deployment on edge systems. Further, 11n exhibited comparable high performance even on the edge cases where classical signal processing methods typically would fail, with a precision of 77.5% and a recall of 81.4%. These results establish the feasibility of an AI-powered, edge-computing A-mode ultrasound system for real-time vascular assessments.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Wafer Predictive Pickup Scheduling Optimization
authors: Kai Zhang, Shangwei Zhao
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing, predictive wafer pickup—where the transfer module retrieves a wafer from a process chamber before the process completes—is widely applied to reduce equipment idle time. However, determining the optimal pickup timing parameters for different recipes is typically reliant on manual experience and extensive on-tool trial-and-error, which can be costly and time-consuming. To address this challenge, we propose a discrete-event-simulation-based framework combined with a reinforcement learning (RL) optimization approach, specifically an improved Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm, to optimize predictive pickup parameters automatically. The proposed method builds an accurate simulation model of the target tool configuration and adapts the RL algorithm to the scheduling optimization context. Experimental results demonstrate that the method can achieve parameter optimization entirely in simulation, significantly reducing tuning time from days to minutes and eliminating the need for prolonged on-tool experiments, while enabling scenario-specific throughput enhancement.

title: Quaternion Convolutional Neural Networks: Enhancing Color Image Classification through Inter-Channel Relationships
authors: Ambika Sharma, Ajit Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: A key challenge in color image classification is the efficient representation of Red, Green and Blue channels while preserving the inherent inter channel relationships. Traditional Convolutional Neural Networks(CNNs) treat each channel independantly. Quaternion Convolutional Neural Networks(QCNNs) address this limitation by extending the real valued neural network framework into a four-dimensional space. In this work, we propose a Quaternion Convolutional Neural Network architecture that leverages quaternion convolution to facilitate interactions between all channels of the input and filter simultaneously using the Hamilton product. A weight initialization strategy to ensure convergence during training is adopted for the quaternion framework. The proposed model is evaluated against an equivalent real-valued CNN counterpart on the image classification task using CIFAR-10 dataset. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed QCNN achieves comparable classification accuracy while requiring approximately four times fewer trainable parameters.

title: Comparing Pose Estimation Models For Lower-Body Joints
authors: Bhavya Sehgal, Thomas Truong, Irene Cheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Multimedia (ICSM)
abstract: Whole-body pose estimation models are commonly evaluated and compared for all joints. While this provides insight into the performance of pose estimation models, it can be misleading for applications that rely on specific sets of joints, such as gait and mobility assessment, which generally depend on lower-body joints. In this work, we aim to prove that considering all joints for comparison and evaluation of pose estimation models for joint-specific applications can be misleading. Our work highlights that even models with high overall accuracy can show poor performance with joint-specific evaluation, specifically for lower-body joints, as these are more prone to occlusion, poor contrast with the background, and fast motions. This emphasizes the need for evaluation of pose estimation models for lower-body joints for clinical and biomechanical applications and for a targeted methodology to achieve this evaluation. In this work, we propose an approach to evaluate the performance of pose estimation models for lower-body joints necessary for gait and mobility applications. We tested six state-of-the-art human pose estimation models on HumanEva and MoVi datasets. Our results show that models like MediaPipe show superior performance for whole-body evaluation, but underperform when evaluated only for lower-body pose estimation

title: Forest Fire Detection and Classification using YOLO v4, YOLO v11 & YOLO v12 - A comparative Study
authors: Bolem Divya Sri, M. Niveditha, Jawaji Jerusha Angeline, A. Diana Andrushia, Mary Neebha T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: A deep learning Based Approach is implemented using three Advanced Object detection Architectures (YOLOv12, v11 & v4) for classify and identify the manifestation from the Visual Data. A data set comprising of both Fire and non-fire images was trained and evaluated on each model using the transfer learning techniques, and their performances were compared in terms of mAP and speed. This is to Develop and Intelligent Forest fire. Detection System. Experimental results indicate that YOLOv12 achieves higher compared to v11 &v4 due to its improvement in architectures and image enhancement strategies. YOLOv12 achieves greater results with precision (91%), mAP (93.7%) and recall (89.6%) whereas YOLOv11 has balanced results with precision (88.9%) and mAP (90.2%) but YOLOv4 has lower compatibility with mAP (84.5%) compared to both versions. The Significance of this paper is to showcase that YOLOv12 is more suitable for instantaneous forest fire situations for real-world scenarios like automated early warning systems and surveillance systems.

title: Applications of Picosecond Laser Acoustics for Advanced Packaging
authors: Johnny Dai, John Tan, Jay Chen, Kwan-Soon Park, Cheolkyu Kim, Priya Mukundhan
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Picosecond laser acoustic (PULSE™) Technology is an industry benchmark for metal film metrology[1]. The non-contact, non-destructive technique is well-suited for providing simultaneous multi-layer measurements in-line on product wafers. The technology has found widespread adoption across multiple device segments supporting both leading edge and specialty process monitoring and control.Thin film thickness control in advanced packaging is vital for ensuring the electrical, thermal, mechanical, and process-related performance of semiconductor devices. Inaccurate film thickness can lead to performance degradation, higher defect rates, and increased production costs, which makes precision metrology essential in the modern semiconductor manufacturing process. This paper highlights the advantages of the application of PULSE Technology in advanced packaging process monitoring.By presenting specific examples, we showcase PULSE Technology's capability to measure multiple-layer stacks, with excellent repeatability, easy to match between tools, and long-term stability. The small spot size makes it possible for direct measurement on BUMPs for advanced packing. Recent upgrades to the system include extending the measurement range to cover very thick, rough films and improvements to signal to noise ratios making it more suitable for advanced packaging to use a single metrology tool to cover a wide range of applications. Additionally, we present examples of the non-destructive Young’s modulus measurement capability that provides critical information about the mechanical strength of the packaging material and residue detection.

title: AI-Assisted Bias Detection and Fairness Auditing in Engineering Performance Evaluation Systems: A Responsible AI Governance Framework
authors: Bhaskar Manchuri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Performance evaluation systems play a critical role in the retention of engineering talent and career progression. Despite formal organizational policies promoting equal opportunity, evaluation outcomes often remain susceptible to subjective managerial bias through narrative performance reviews, forced ranking mechanisms, and subjective promotion decisions. This paper proposes an AI-assisted auditing framework that combines machine learning prediction models, natural language processing (NLP) of evaluation narratives, and anomaly detection algorithms to detect bias in engineering performance evaluation systems. Using a synthetic dataset of 1,000 engineer records representing performance metrics, peer feedback, and managerial review narratives, the framework identifies inconsistencies between objective performance indicators and final ratings through a hybrid AI approach integrating gradient-boosted performance prediction, transformer-based sentiment analysis, and ensemble anomaly detection using Isolation Forest and Local Outlier Factor. The experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed hybrid AI approach can identify biased evaluations with up to 87% detection accuracy and a 7% false positive rate across the simulated scenarios. Unlike prior work focused on ensuring fairness in algorithmic decision-making, this framework applies AI to audit human evaluation decisions, addressing a critical gap in responsible AI governance. This work contributes to the emerging fields of algorithmic fairness and people analytics by providing a practical, scalable approach for organizations seeking to enhance trust, transparency, and accountability in technical performance management systems.

title: Designing Retrieval Systems for LLMs: A Controlled Comparison of Chunk-Based and File-Centric Methods
authors: Siddhanth Kalyanaraman, Ravi Gupta, Ashish Sandeep Joshi, Kaushik Sandeep Joshi, Jayesh Suryawanshi, Vidney Jadhav
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a widely used approach for enabling large language models (LLMs) to access proprietary or private data, but its pipelines require complex infrastructure including chunking, embedding, and vector database management. Structured Sequential File Retrieval (SSFR), in which documents are ranked at the file level using lexical scoring and the best snippets extracted as context, offers reduced complexity and latency but lacks empirical comparison with RAG under controlled conditions. This study introduces a decision framework for selecting retrieval architectures by comparing RAG with SSFR across two open-weight models (LLaMA-3.3-70B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct), two quantization levels, and two corpus sizes. A benchmark of 69 prompts spanning factual, analytical, and multi-hop reasoning queries is evaluated using quality metrics (hallucination rate, groundedness, answer correctness) and operational metrics (latency, GPU utilization, throughput, cost). Results show that SSFR matches RAG on small to medium corpora while reducing latency and cost, but structured chunk-level retrieval provides stronger grounding as corpus size and query complexity increase. The study produces a decision matrix linking workload characteristics to recommended configurations.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition Algorithm Based on Multimodal Bottleneck Fusion Network
authors: Yaxi Chen, Jinzong Hu, Qianchuan Zhang, Chang Liu, Yutong Sun, Yimei Long, Jiahao Chen, Cheng Qian, Xin Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition (MER) improves the accuracy, robustness, and adaptability of emotion detection by capturing modality-specific emotional cues and exploiting cross-modal complementarity. Nevertheless, existing approaches still face limitations in learning deep feature representations, aligning heterogeneous data, and performing efficient multimodal fusion. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel Multimodal Bottleneck Fusion Network (MBFN). Our framework utilizes pretrained models, including BERT, Wav2Vec 2.0 and FabNet, to extract rich semantic features from text, audio and visual modalities. We further design an LSTM-based information aggregation module equipped with a collaborative attention mechanism to dynamically align temporal features and suppresses redundancy via adaptive weighting. Moreover, a bottleneck fusion mechanism embedded in the Transformer architecture is introduced to restrict cross-modal interactions, thus alleviating computational redundancy and noise interference. Experimental results on the IEMOCAP and CMU-MOSEI datasets demonstrate that MBFN outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods (e.g., TFN, LMF, MulT), achieving top performance on metrics such as ACC-7 and ACC-2 with reduced computational costs. The proposed approach advances MER by balancing fusion efficiency, noise suppression, and context-aware modality integration.

title: Selective Silicon Boride Etch to Form Titanium Nitride Pillar during Capacitor Memory Cell Manufacturing
authors: Xuehua Wang, Litian Xu, Tao Shen
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Aa scaling down the feature size of memory cells, silicon boride (SiB) is used as hard mask (HM) in the etching of deep capacitor holes to addresse the pattern ruin issue within the limit of HM thickness. It will be a challenge for SiB HM removal after deep capacitor holes etch and titanium nitride (TiN) electrode fill. In this paper, we propose a selective SiB etch process by using SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>-based plasma. The bias power is a critical factor for the trade-off between TiN electrode pillar damage and the etching by-product residue issue. Furthermore, Quasi atomic layer deposition (ALD) process is implemented in order to achieve free of TiN pillar loss during the capacitor memory cell manufacturing.

title: Comprehensive Etching Solution for Patterning Layer Profile Control and Titanium Residue Removal on Advanced Logic Trench Processes
authors: Chia Lin Lu, Jiu Yang Zhu, Hui Sun
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Hard mask profile including patterning layer (PL) and titanium nitrite (TiN) is a key factor for dual dimension all in one etch (DDAiO) about critical dimension (CD) and trench profile control on back-end-of-line (BEOL). Besides, reducing titanium byproduct and residue generated by both hard mask and DDAiO etch are also a key challenge for yield improvement [1]-[2]. In this paper, a typical trench process for hard mask etch has been evaluated to demonstrate hard mask profile control ability and delivered titanium byproduct solutions. By using AppliedPRO™ [3], a systemic investigation was performed to find the tuning knobs of PL height and rounding control. At the meantime, efficient flush step has been developed for removing titanium residue. This study demonstrates efficient way to find the hard mask profile tuning knobs and develop relative etching recipe with titanium residue free.

title: A Study of Dielectric Etch in Deep Via
authors: Ya Zhou, Changwei Pei, Zinuo Chen
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: AI and 5G have driven the development of advanced packaging in the past few years, providing opportunities and challenges to packaging technology. More efforts are carried out for optimizing wafer bonding performance, resulting in increased dielectric etch process. Many of these processes show higher etch depth (>5μm), thus lead to different etch performance. In this work, we have studied the dielectric etching in deep via and found depth dependent etch performance including profile angle and sidewall roughness. Gas component and power tuning according to via depth is necessary to realize vertical profile with less damage.

title: Enhancing Citizens' Information and Communication Technology Competencies: Opportunities for Lifelong Learning
authors: Buyandelger Banzragch, Lut-Ochir Vanganjil, Oyungoo Enkhbold, Munkhmagnai Altangerel
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This study examines the critical role of developing citizens' information and communication technology (ICT) competencies in promoting participation, productivity, and equity in the knowledge-based society of the 21st century. Drawing on case studies and policy analyses, the paper identifies effective programs and opportunities for enhancing fundamental ICT skills. Strengthening citizens' digital competencies is shown to be a key factor not only for personal growth but also for supporting the sustainable development of society. The study further underscores the importance of fostering lifelong learning to enhance personal development, employment, social participation, access to digital education, and the formation of digitally capable citizens.

title: High Performance Computing for Effective CT & MRI Image Segmentation
authors: Murugan S, Arpitha J, Veda N, Archana M, Prema Arokia Mary G, Nageswara Guptha M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: High Performance Computing (HPC) is a new development in computer science that brings together computer architecture, programming, algorithms, and system software to quickly process and analyze data. It is designed to reduce the time it takes to complete tasks of paramount importance, such as medical research needing exact results in a timely fashion. Through using image fusion methods in tandem with HPC, results can be obtained from combining data from various sources, further improving the ability to quickly extract meaningful answers. In this research developed a novel pre- processing technique for predicting disease by using CT & MRI images at initial stage. Bin-aural approach classifies the input image in to smaller blocks and evaluates the intensity of pixel based on gray scale level. After the estimation of image scale those pixel less than the median values are eliminated which effectively removes the noise. Further, image segmentation is performed through application Gaussian operator for improving registration performance. This article presents the performance of a parallelized image segmentation algorithm running on a computer cluster with 10 processing units. The algorithm is evaluated using mean-squared error (MSE) and peak signal-to- noise ratio (PSNR) metrics. The PSNR value for proposed bin- aural processing provides 74.7669, 72.766, 78.48, 82.49 and 78.45. In case of MSE, it is observed as 0.02, 0.04, 0.005, 1.79 and 0.0066. The PSNR analysis of existing technique is observed as 55.75 for bilateral, 62.46 for adaptive filter and granular and Gaussian exhibits PSNR value of 62.54 and 69.45. Simulation results exhibited that proposed bin-aural technique offers significant performance in terms of PSNR and MSE.

title: RegGNN: Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for AML Detection via Regulatory Signal Integration
authors: Theophilus F, Sanjay R, Sanjay Kumar R, Sanju P G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Money laundering networks are getting increasingly complex, and typical AML systems struggle to analyze them, thus making financial fraud detection more complex. This paper presents an explainable heterogeneous graph neural network (RegGNN) for Anti-Money Laundering (AML). RegGNN models Customers, Accounts, Transactions and Regulatory Complaints as graph entities that are interconnected to describe hidden financial relationships. The model passes regulatory risk signals throughout the financial system, enhancing fraud detection, with the help of a Relation-Aware Attention mechanism and Curriculum Fidelity Training. The proposed framework is good at reflecting the transactional behavior and external regulatory factors, which can help to accurately evaluate the risk. Experimental results demonstrate that RegGNN improves the risk detection accuracy of connected entities by 20× and the accuracy rate by 10.6% over the current topology-based model on real-world financial datasets, with a 94.8% accuracy rate and an AUC of 0.93.

title: Development of a simplified FEM model for cellECM mechanical interaction
authors: Maria Vittoria Martire, Ludovica Apa, Barbara Peruzzi, Eleonora Lancia, Luca Cortese, Zaccaria Del Prete, Emanuele Rizzuto
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Cellular mechanical properties have a significant impact on the regulation of various physiological and pathological processes. Research has demonstrated a correlation between pathological conditions and alterations in the mechanical properties of cells, which respond to mechanical stimuli in a non-physiological way. In this work, we propose a finite element method (FEM) numerical model, developed in the Ansys environment, with the aim of simulating the mechanical behaviour of an adherent cell on elastic substrates of different stiffness, replicating experimental conditions of stretching. Indeed, in a previous study, we developed and validated a noninvasive optical technique for measuring the strain drop of cells adhering to substrates of different stiffness, a parameter related to cell elasticity and cell-substrate interaction. The cell was modelled using a simplified three-dimensional ellipsoidal geometry, which includes cytoplasm, nucleus and extracellular membrane (ECM). Adhesion to the substrate was obtained by discrete contact points, representing real focal adhesions. Results highlighted a distribution of deformations in the substrate consistent with experimental observations. A close correspondence is observed for the $\mathbf{3 0 ~ k P a}$ substrate with an applied deformation of $\mathbf{1 \%}(\mathbf{0. 7 5} \pm \mathbf{0. 1 5 \%}$ vs $\mathbf{0. 7 4 \%}$, respectively experimental and numerical data). They emphasised the importance of cellular elasticity and the related mechanical contributions, providing a basis for extending the analysis to other cell types and cell-matrix interaction scenarios.

title: Edge-optimized deep learning framework for multispectral textile waste classification and automated robotic sorting
authors: Padmavathy R, Reena R, Subha G, Shylash L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This paper presents a method for automatic classification and separation of textiles. The rising amount of post-consumer textile waste represents a serious recycling challenge due to the visual similarity and diverse composition of fabrics. Manual processing is a time-consuming and ineffective method for large-scale recycling. The article will discuss a method for automatic classification and separation of textiles by implementing a convolutional neural network-based edge computing system with an electromechanical system on a conveyor belt. The processing of high-resolution images of moving samples of textiles is performed on a motorized conveyor belt and then processed by an optimized convolutional neural network running on a low-power edge device.The results are then sent to a microcontroller that operates a servo-based sorter to allocate textiles to selected bins for collection. Bin levels are constantly checked for overflow by ultrasonic sensors, while its status is depicted on an LCD display for monitoring. In experimental assessments on a sample image dataset for textiles, classification results were attained at 93.8% accuracy, with an F1 score of 94.1% for its real-time performance capability.

title: Distributed Power Analytics: Leveraging Federated Harris Hawks Optimization for Confidential Energy Prediction
authors: Nader Bakir, Ahmad Gawish, Khawla Al Hassan, Vadim Pak, Marcelle Merhy, Abdallah Chakik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Smart buildings consume for roughly 20% of global energy consumption which makes accurate predication of energy consumption essential for operational efficiency improvement. Most of the traditional machine-learning models for operational efficiency use custom algorithms that require knowledge of sensitive data about building usage and occupancy, raising significant privacy concerns. While federated learning methods provide better data privacy, they often limited with fixed optimization settings that limits the ability of responding to the varied energy consumption patterns in buildings, making them less accurate in prediction. In this paper, We propose a new model, called FL-HHO, that integrates federated learning and the Harris Hawks Optimization algorithm for data privacy and model building energy consumption prediction. Our model implements an adaptive approach based on the ’escape energy behavior’ of hawks, balancing exploration of new and best existing solutions avoiding the problems of localized and limited solutions of complex consumption patterns. The experiments of our proposed model using a CU-BEMS dataset that contains 18 months of actual data of buildings, with a data loss rate of up to 25.9% showed a Minimum Squared Error of 1.18, outperforming existing federal standards. This paper balances privacy and smart energy management in smart buildings, solving a long-standing problem in the adoption of smart buildings.

title: Lightweight Carbon-Conscious Machine Learning Framework for Lung Cancer Risk Assessment
authors: Hifsah Nasir, Wajahat Riaz, Ihtisham Ali, Khalil Khan, Maqbool Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has shown strong potential for early lung cancer risk screening; however, most existing approaches prioritize predictive accuracy while overlooking energy consumption and environmental sustainability. This paper presents an empirical Green-AI evaluation framework for lung cancer risk assessment using Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost models combined through a lightweight voting ensemble strategy and evaluated under identical experimental and energy measurement conditions based on clinical and lifestyle factors. Rather than proposing a novel learning algorithm, the study systematically evaluates the trade-off between predictive performance, energy consumption, and carbon footprint during both training and inference. Experiments conducted on a publicly available dataset demonstrate that lightweight ensemble learning can achieve clinically relevant predictive performance while significantly reducing computational and carbon costs. The results highlight the importance of carbon-aware evaluation for deploying sustainable AI solutions in resource-constrained healthcare environments.

title: Comparative analysis of DT and KNN for AMC
authors: Ahmed Ali, Hesham Ibrahim Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of Decision Trees (DT) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) for Automatic Analog Modulation Classification (AMC) in wireless systems. We utilize features extracted from the RadioML2016.10a dataset, focusing on Amplitude Modulation (AM) and Frequency Modulation (FM) across various Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNRs). Our methodology includes signal preprocessing and instantaneous feature computation. Experimental results show both DT and KNN significantly outperform a random baseline and are competitive with a recent lightweight deep learning model (61.20% average accuracy). KNN achieves 65.82% overall accuracy, while DT offers notably faster inference (approaching ms/sample). The study highlights practical trade-offs, demonstrating the utility of these traditional approaches for resource-constrained AMC applications, while establishing a crucial baseline for the integration of higher-complexity methods in future work.

title: Sym3™Y In-situ ALD Solution for High Aspect Ratio Etch Profile Control
authors: Tianyuan Liu, Qiang Ge
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: The Centris™ Sym3™ Y etch system from Applied Materials® integrates in-situ Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD), a key capability for fabricating high aspect ratio (HAR) structures [1]. During HAR etching, ion deflection from the mask can lead to sidewall bombardment and profile distortion, such as bowing (Figure 1). Additionally, limited etch selectivity between the bottom, sidewalls, and top surfaces may cause structural damage and degrade device performance. In-situ ALD enables precise sidewall passivation and defect control, offering a proven solution for maintaining profile integrity and electrical reliability.

title: A Lightweight Explainable Deep Neural Network for Multi-Class Intrusion Detection
authors: Manjula M, Venkatesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: For a modern intrusion detection system, it is challenging to detect multiple types of cyber attacks, especially when these attacks need to be identified with consistent accuracy. Many existing approaches perform binary classification for attack detection and also struggle to maintain stable performance across diverse attack patterns. This paper presents a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP)-based framework for multi-class cyber-attack detection using flow-level network features. The model aims to categorize traffic into six categories: normal, phishing, malware, denial-of-service (DoS), SQL injection, and man-in-the-middle (MitM). A balanced dataset is generated with these attack types in a controlled experimental environment. The dataset was normalized, and features were normalized using z-score, and the model was optimized using Categorical Cross-Entropy Loss with L2 regularization. Evaluation in this experiment was conducted using the following methods: Overall Accuracy and Normalized Confusion Matrix with the use of ROC curves, the Softmax prediction and Sensitivity analyses, and the Precision-Recall F1 Score (R). The framework that was designed achieved the following results: 98.44% for the total accuracy, and it provided the model with a high confidence level. The framework was designed based on the observations and evidence of the framework’s effectiveness, with a balance of accuracy and robustness. The framework provides practitioners with a model that has a high level of transparency as well as confidence in Cyber Attack detection in their networks.

title: AI Based Air Quality Forecasting in the Gulf Region
authors: Mohammed Fakrudeen, Hatem Tamimi, Jim Otieno
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Air pollution presents a formidable environmental and public health threat in arid regions due to exacerbate particulate matter fluctuations. In order to address these challenges, this study introduces a machine learning framework for dissecting and projecting air quality across the Gulf region by drawing on annual observations of PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf>, PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf>, and NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. Using XGBoost model featuring radial basis function kernels derived from engineered temporal features (lagged concentrations and rolling averages), we evaluated forecasting performance through conventional error metrics while probing the merits of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction. To strengthen methodological rigour, we additionally benchmarked SVR against Persistence (lag-1), Linear Regression, and Random Forest baselines under time-series cross-validation. Predictive uncertainty was quantified using split-conformal prediction intervals, and model interpretability was assessed via permutation feature importance. Results show that normalised SVR-RBF configurations generally outperform PCA-augmented versions for each pollutant examined, while NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> remains more predictable than particulate matter due to dust-driven volatility. Country-level breakdowns expose spatial variations, alongside long-term NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> outlooks that indicate stabilisation or gentle downturns by 2030. Despite annual data sparsity and the absence of meteorology/dust-transport covariates, the proposed framework provides a repeatable foundation for long-term air quality monitoring and AQI-orientated policy interpretation in the Gulf.

title: Removal of H-Containing Byproducts To Enhance Pre-Coating Quality in Etching Chamber
authors: Songmo Du, Yunhe Dong, Xian Du, Yuan Deng
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Pre-coating applied between waferless auto-clean and regular etching processes is used to enhance wafer-to-wafer consistency and extend chamber component lifetime. However, H-containing byproducts generated during process adversely affect pre-coating quality, leading to increased particle level and reduced yield. In this article, an additional cleaning step before the standard waferless auto-clean is added to address this issue. The results demonstrate a significant improvement in pre-coating quality. The underlying reaction mechanisms before and after the implementation of the new step are investigated.

title: CsPbBr Perovskite Quantum Dots and One-Dimensional ZnO nanorods composites applied for Ultraviolet/Visible Broadband photodetectors_supp1-3696249.pdf
authors: Sheng-Joue Young
publish: N/A
abstract: Broadband photodetection covering the ultraviolet and visible spectral regions remains a critical challenge for low-cost optoelectronic devices. In this work, a hybrid photodetector architecture was realized by integrating one-dimensional (1-D) ZnO nanorods (NRs) with CsPbBr3 perovskite quantum dots (PQDs), forming a sensitized heterostructure for enhanced visible-light response. The structural and compositional features of the pristine 1-D ZnO NRs and PQDs-capped NRs samples were systematically examined through electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, and optical spectroscopies, revealing successful decoration of CsPbBr3 PQDs on the surface of 1-D ZnO NRs. Photoluminescence and absorption analyses demonstrate that the introduction of PQDs effectively extends the optical response of ZnO from the ultraviolet into the visible region. Electrical measurements further show that the PQDs/1-D ZnO NRs heterostructure exhibits markedly improved photoresponse under blue and green illumination compared with unmodified 1-D ZnO NRs, while maintaining low dark current characteristics. This enhancement is attributed to efficient interfacial charge transfer and suppressed carrier recombination enabled by favorable energy-level alignment between CsPbBr3 PQDs and 1-D ZnO NRs. The proposed hybrid structure provides a viable route toward compact, cost-effective, and low-power broadband photodetectors suitable for ultraviolet and visible-light sensing applications.

title: Hardhat-$\alpha$: Efficient Hard-Hat-Wearing Detection for Industrial Production Environments via Feature Calibration and Task-Matched Learning
authors: Yusen Zhang, Guosun Zeng, Mingming Huang, Yi Zhang, Yufei Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Rapid and accuratehard-hat-wearing detection is critical for safeguarding workers’ lives in industrial production environments. However, the largest open-source dataset for hard-hat-wearing detection, safety helmet wearing dataset (SHWD), lacks enough samples from practical industrial workshops and nonstandard instances, such as wearing ordinary hats and holding hard hats, and cannot effectively support hard-hat-wearing detection in manufacturing sites. Therefore, in this article, we expand the SHWD to an industrial-specialized hard-hat-wearing detection dataset, Indu-SHWD, by adding a series of necessary data lacking in the former dataset. Furthermore, mainstream object detection models exhibit a high missed detection rate in this task due to their ambiguous feature expression of tiny objects and mismatched prediction between classification and localization. To address these issues, we introduce a novel feature calibration network to adaptively suppress the conflict information in feature maps output by the multiscale feature fusion module and offer precise representations for tiny objects. We also propose task-matched learning to explicitly align tasks of classifying and localizing in the model training stage via a learnable task-matched weight and refining losses. After equipping the popular object detection model YOLOv7 with the aforementioned two works, we present an advanced hard-hat-wearing detection model Hardhat-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Extensive experiments on Indu-SHWD demonstrate that Hardhat-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula> surpasses YOLOv13 along with a range of other mainstream detection models, achieving a new state-of-the-art with an accuracy of 94.5% and a detection speed of 175 frames per second.

title: Design and Modeling of a Sandwich-Enhanced Chain Mail Jamming Mechanism for Robotic Applications
authors: Abdullah Ahmed, Zhengtao Hu, Weiwei Wan, Tetsuyou Watanabe, Kensuke Harada
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Developing robots capable of interacting with and adapting to their environment through their morphology allows for the realization of embodied intelligence. One promising strategy for enabling such intelligent behavior is the use of variable stiffness mechanisms. This article introduces a jamming-based variable stiffness mechanism that integrates chain mail fabrics within the sandwich jamming structure. Incorporating chain mail fabrics contributes to the structure’s lightweight while enhancing its flexibility in the unjammed state. Meanwhile, the sandwich structure significantly increases stiffness in the jammed state. The presented sandwich jamming approach significantly increases stiffness with a minimal increase in thickness. We experimentally investigate the effect of the applied vacuum pressure, the number of chain mail core layers, and the number of face layers on the overall stiffness of the proposed sandwich structure. We achieved a 58-fold increase in the structural stiffness at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-$</tex-math></inline-formula>80 kPa compared to the initial configuration of the sandwich structure at 0 kPa. An analytical model of the stiffness of the sandwich jamming structure is developed to facilitate the design process of soft machines using the proposed structure. Finally, we present two potential applications of the sandwich chain mail jamming structure: An adaptive elbow exosuit and a soft robotic finger.

title: Deep Transformer based Structural Semantic Network for Document Clustering
authors: Umair Amir, Faizan Ali, Ali Ahmed, Muhammad Rafi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Document clustering is very sensitive to document representation schemes. Traditional document clustering approaches used document content to model features and used representation based on these features thus suffer from problems like vocabulary mismatch and word sense ambiguities, and fail to cater with word-to-word relationship in longer dependency. Consequently, the clustering results lack semantic values. Deep document clustering simultaneously learns semantic repesentation and does clustering using deep neural networks. In this paper, a deep transformer-based structural semantic network for document clustering (DTSN) is proposed, it uses a transformer based approach to learn a document-level semantic representation with long-term text dependency, and exploits graph convolution network in order to capture semantic between documents going layer-by-layer, utilizing reinforcement learning. Extensive series of experiments are performed, and this proposed approach performs substantially better than state-of-the-art deep document clustering approaches. This paper reports a new state-of-the-art on unsupervised text document clustering tasks.

title: System-Level Analysis of Physical Realization in Algorithmically Generated Scents
authors: M. Aleixandre, D. Prasetyawan, T. Nakamoto
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Algorithmic scent generation has recently become capable of producing chemically consistent odor representations, opening new opportunities for automated and data-driven aroma design. However, translating generated representations into physically delivered scents that remain perceptually meaningful to human users remains a critical challenge. This work presents, for the first time, an end-to-end scent generation framework that maps odor descriptors to human-perceived scents and is analyzed at the system level. The proposed system employs a generative diffusion neural network to synthesize mass spectrometry profiles conditioned on odor descriptors, followed by mixture approximation using either a reduced set of odor components or a full essential-oil basis, and final presentation via an active multi-channel olfactory display or vial-based headspace delivery. Sensory experiments were conducted to evaluate how mixture formulation and delivery modality influence perceptual outcomes. The results demonstrate that the proposed model can robustly generate diverse and novel aroma profiles that align with specified odor descriptors, confirming the practical feasibility of algorithmic scent creation with physical output. While perceptual accuracy varies with formulation and delivery choices, discrimination performance remains consistently above chance across all realization conditions, indicating that meaningful perceptual structure is preserved. The analysis provides actionable guidance for system design, supporting the development of scalable scent generation platforms and olfactory displays for applications in fragrance design and human–machine interaction.
