title: Voltage Balancing in Series-Connected GaN Devices using Scalable Transformer-Coupled Gate Driver
authors: Sohrab Ghafoor, Reza Mirzadarani, Hitesh Dialani, Peter Vaessen, Mohamad Ghaffarian Niasar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: This paper presents a scalable transformer-coupled open-loop gate-driving technique that enables voltage balancing across series-connected GaN devices. High-voltage pulse generation with short rise times at kilovolt levels is a significant challenge. Conventional solid-state devices such as IGBTs and SiC MOSFETs, though capable of high blocking voltages, are limited in switching speed and cannot reach the nanosecond regime. Gallium Nitride (GaN) High-Electron-Mobility Transistors (HEMTs) due to their lateral structure offer low gate charge and ability to switch faster than 50 V/ns, are an attractive potential candidate. However, due to their lateral architecture, GaN HEMTs also have limited voltage-blocking capability, with most commercially available GaN power devices rated up to approximately 650 V, limiting their direct use in fast high voltage waveform generation at kilo-volts level. Series-connecting GaN devices can overcome this limitation but introduces severe voltage balancing challenges, as even minor gate-signal mismatch at nanosecond timescale can cause destructive imbalance. Conventional closed-loop balancing methods, are difficult to implement at GaN switching speeds due to feedback latency. This paper presents a simple open loop technique for driving GaN devices in series with a transformer-coupled gate driver. The proposed gate drive ensures simultaneous turn-on/off with identical gate signals across all devices. An ultrafast full-bridge GaN based inverter excites wideband gate-drive transformers designed to preserve the ultrafast transition speeds of individual devices, while providing high voltage isolation and near-equal voltage sharing establishing a scalable solution. Experimental results with two series-connected GaN HEMTs confirm nearly balanced voltage sharing at 1 kV across varying loads and currents.

title: Attack-Resilient Secure Localization for Underwater Acoustic Networks via Frequency-Diverse DRSS
authors: Rong Fan, Azzedine Boukerche, Zhigang Jin, Yishan Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Differential received signal strength (DRSS)-based localization is increasingly preferred for resource-constrained underwater acoustic sensor networks due to its low measurement complexity and ease of implementation. However, DRSS measurements are inherently sensitive to power perturbations induced by malicious anchor attacks, and these effects are further aggravated by severe ambient noise and strong multipath propagation, leading to degraded localization accuracy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a multi-frequency DRSS-based underwater secure localization scheme that explicitly accounts for power-attack-induced distortions. By integrating a multipath propagation loss model with multi-frequency DRSS measurements, a robust localization model is established to suppress both power attack effects and multipath-induced uncertainties. Frequency diversity is exploited to replace spatial redundancy, enabling three-dimensional localization using only two anchors while maintaining localization reliability. Furthermore, channel impulse response simplifies the localization model by establishing relative multipath constraints, converting it into a worst-case uncertainty-aware optimization problem to handle power attacks, with a semidefinite programming (SDP) estimator proposed to turn this into a convex SDP problem for accurate sensor position estimates. Simulation results and field experiments demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves improved localization accuracy and reliability under power attacks and other adverse measurement conditions.

title: Inverse-Supervised-Learning-Based Secure Adaptive Consensus Control for Distributed Permanent-Magnet Synchronous Motor Systems
authors: Jiayue Sun, Siyu Guo, Zeyi Liu, Zhi Lu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: This work introduces a unified framework for secure consensus control and intelligent evaluation in distributed permanent-magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) systems. To begin, an entropy-driven adaptive chaotic masking encryption scheme is developed, which secures cooperative control signals by dynamically weighting multiple chaotic states according to their real-time sample entropy. By prioritizing high-complexity states, the proposed scheme maximizes key entropy and enhances resistance to statistical attacks. Building on this, a distributed neural-network consensus controller is formulated to achieve synchronized operation of multiple PMSMs over an encrypted communication layer. Complementing the control strategy, an inverse-supervised-learning-based small-intelligence evaluation model, driven by a bespoke performance–energy cost function, is introduced for online fault detection and performance appraisal. By training solely on data from nominal operating conditions, the model's dependence on labeled fault instances is markedly reduced and generalizability is enhanced. The model architecture utilizes convolutional neural networks with Transformer modules to extract salient temporal features and distinguish between normal, excellent, and fault states. Furthermore, a mixture-of-experts scheme is applied to bolster resilience against noise and outliers. Experimental validation confirms that the proposed framework accurately infers the operational status of distributed PMSM systems and delivers reliable performance evaluations in line with design requirements.

title: Thermal Processing Limits in Oxide-Channel Ferroelectric Field Effect Transistors
authors: Lance Fernandes, Yu-Hsin Kuo, Chengyang Zhang, Priyankka Ravikumar, Ranie Jeyakumar, Dyutimoy Chakraborty, Jiayi Chen, Taeyoung Song, Kai Ni, Woohyun Hwang, Kwangyou Seo, Suhwan Lim, Wanki Kim, Daewon Ha, Julia Medvedeva, Suman Datta, Shimeng Yu, Asif Khan
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: In this work, we report a systematic study of the impact of high-temperature post-capping thermal annealing on the memory characteristics of Oxide-semiconductor channel ferroelectric field-effect transistors (OS-FeFETs). Using an identical engineered ferroelectric gate stack—8nm Hf<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>Zr<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO) / 3 nm Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> / 8 nm HZO (8/3/8)—and a hybrid capping layer (3 nm HfO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> + 3 nm Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>), 10% Ga-doped In<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> (IGO)–channel and 4% W-doped In<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> (IWO)–channel FeFETs remain functional after annealing at temperatures up to 650 °C for durations of up to 30 min and 10 min, respectively; further annealing results in irreversible loss of conduction and device failure. Detailed electrical analysis reveals that the MW enhancement originates from a preferential positive shift in the erased-state threshold voltage, while the programmed-state threshold voltage remains comparatively stable. Grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction measurements further indicate structural evolution in the IWO and IGO oxide channels with increasing annealing temperature, supporting the observed electrical trends.

title: ZORAN-Sec: A Zero Trust–Compliant Intrusion Detection Framework for Open RAN with a Lightweight Transformer
authors: Loreen Mahmoud, Madhusanka Liyanage, Sugata Gangopadhyay
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) address vendor lock-in and limited flexibility in traditional Radio Access Networks by introducing open interfaces, disaggregation, and programmability. While these properties improve scalability and interoperability, they also significantly expand the security attack surface of the RAN. Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) has emerged as a suitable security paradigm for O-RAN, as it enforces continuous verification, least privilege, and adaptive policy enforcement. However, existing ZTA-enabled O-RAN security solutions remain limited by three key challenges: intrusion detection methods that operate on individual samples and ignore temporal attack context, inference latency that violates near–real-time control requirements, and high computational overhead that prevents deployment on resource-constrained edge components. This paper presents ZORAN-Sec, a Zero Trust–compliant intrusion detection framework that addresses these gaps through system-level co-design rather than isolated algorithmic improvements. ZORAN-Sec operationalises ZTA’s Policy Enforcement, Decision, and Information Points by explicitly mapping them to O-RAN entities and coupling detection outputs to adaptive policy enforcement. At its core, we introduce LightSecFormer, a lightweight encoder-only transformer trained from scratch on tabular sequential O-RAN traffic, avoiding the domain mismatch of NLP-derived distilled or pruned variants, with linear embedding and CLS-token pooling calibrated for edge constraints. Empirical evaluation across four heterogeneous datasets (5G-NIDD, NetsLab-5G-ORAN-IDD v1/v2, and N-BaIoT) demonstrates strong detection fidelity in binary and multiclass settings, with inference latency well below O-RAN near-real-time control constraints and competitive or superior performance relative to established baselines. Statistical validation across ten independent runs confirms that performance gains are reproducible and not attributable to favourable initialisation. Cross-dataset generalisation experiments reveal strong forward transferability across evolving traffic distributions, and adversarial robustness analysis under FGSM and PGD perturbations demonstrates graceful degradation under increasing attack strength. Separately, a prototype Near-RT RIC testbed deployment using a real srsRAN gNB and FlexRIC Near-RT RIC confirms operational compliance with near-real-time constraints under live E2SM-KPM telemetry ingestion, with measured P99 xApp processing latency of 0.75 ms (13× below the 10 ms Near-RT RIC budget) and stable resource consumption of 501MB RAM, and mean CPU usage under half a CPU core. Full live O-RAN deployment with over-the-air RF validation remains a direction for future work. Together, these results substantiate architectural viability, policy-driven enforcement integration, and operational robustness within O-RAN security workflows.

title: A Novel Location Method for Early-Stage Cable Fault Integrating Spread Spectrum and Frequency-stepped Wave Reflection Technology
authors: Longlei Bai, Hanlin Tao, Jingyi Zeng, Zejun Ma, Bo Luo, Liang Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The harsh laying environment and performance degradation of two-core cables over time significantly increase the risk of power transmission failures. However, accurately capturing the subtle characteristic impedance variations caused by early-stage cable faults remains a significant challenge for existing reflectometry methods. Existing reflectometry methods have limitations: spread spectrum time-domain reflectometry (SSTDR) is restricted by chip duration and shows low sensitivity to early-stage faults, while stepped-frequency wave reflectometry (SFWR) is often constrained by signal attenuation and aliasing, resulting in a limited measurement range. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel spread spectrum frequency-stepped wave modulation (SSFWR) technique. The method combines the wide-ranging capability of SSTDR with the high sensitivity of SFWR, while simplifying data processing. In addition, variational mode decomposition (VMD) is applied to the autocorrelation of the incident signal and the cross-correlation of the incident and reflected signals. Fault-related modal components are then analyzed using the short-time Fourier transform (STFT) and the reassigned spectrogram (RSP). Dispersion effects are mitigated by spectrum extraction and secondary cross-correlation, enabling high-precision detection and localization of early-stage cable faults. Simulations and experiments verify that the proposed method achieves wide-range fault detection and accurate localization of open-circuit, short-circuit, and early-stage cable faults.

title: DiagConTA: Continual Test-Time Domain Adaptation for Industrial Fault Diagnosis Under Dynamic Working Conditions_supp1-3691586.pdf
authors: Xinmin Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Dynamic working conditions in industrial systems cause continual distribution shifts in test data that degrade fault diagnosis performance, while vanilla domain adaptation for distribution shifts faces limitations like source data dependence and single-stage adaptation. Continual test-time domain adaptation, an emerging paradigm that integrates continual learning and test-time domain adaptation, has the potential to continually adapt a pretrained model to a series of domains during testing without using source data. In this article, we propose DiagConTA, a novel Continual Test-time Domain Adaptation framework for Industrial Fault Diagnosis under dynamic working conditions. DiagConTA consists of two stages: source domain pretraining and sequential target domain adaptation. In source domain pretraining, we adopt prototype contrastive learning to pretrain the model on the labeled source domain to learn the initial model parameters with better generalization ability. During sequential target domain adaptation, for effective adaptation, we introduce top-$k$ pseudolabeling to provide more reliable labels for the unlabeled instances and maximize mutual information to align the distribution shifts without source data. Meanwhile, considering the catastrophic forgetting and the stability-plasticity dilemma in continual learning, we propose stochastic recovery and prototype-guided contrastive alignment, which explicitly and implicitly constrain the update of the model parameters, respectively. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of DiagConTA in achieving robust fault diagnosis under dynamic working conditions.

title: Scoring Students’ Answers Using OCR and GPT
authors: Abdulaziz Algablan, Raghad Alshammari, Sadeem Alresaini, Rana Alnughaimshi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has immense potential to revolutionize grading and the delivery of efficient feedback to students, all while reducing the workload for instructors. Existing automated grading systems are typically limited to specific question types, such as true-false and multiple-choice questions, and often do not address the need to assess more diverse forms of student responses. This paper proposes ExaGrade, an open-source platform that integrates optical character recognition (OCR) with the Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT). It enables the use of AI models to automate the grading of student answers. The platform supports both paper-based and online exams. The early results presented in this paper show promising capabilities in handling flexible formats for true/false and multiple-choice questions. However, further investigation and refinement are necessary to effectively address open-ended responses. The study observed that GPT’s scoring of student answers faces notable limitations compared to instructor evaluations. The experiment revealed a low average Euclidean distance, indicating significant differences between the two grading methods. While the average cosine similarity of 0.628 suggests moderate alignment in grading patterns, the average exact match ratio of 0.648 reflects a moderate overall agreement between ExaGrade and the instructor’s evaluations.

title: Collective Bargaining in Karnataka Public Sector Undertakings: A Computational Analysis of Indian Court Judgments Using NLP and Machine Learning
authors: Aakash Y Raj, Gopi Ranga Nath V, Achyutha Nanda Mishra, Md Shaik Amzad Basha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Collective bargaining in Indian public sector undertakings has been studied through doctrinal and sociological lenses, yet computational approaches to understanding how courts adjudicate these disputes over time remain largely absent from the literature. This paper presents a four-phase NLP and machine learning pipeline applied to 682 Indian court judgments on collective bargaining in Karnataka public sector undertakings, spanning 1950 to 2026. The pipeline covers corpus analytics, rule-based outcome labeling, supervised text classification, and Latent Dirichlet Allocation topic modeling. Non-parametric statistical tests confirm that the 1991 economic liberalization produced measurable structural shifts in judicial discourse. Post-1991 judgments are significantly longer on average, and named entity recognition reveals that retrenchment and employment security emerged as the dominant post-liberalization concern, appearing in 161 judgments compared to just one before 1991. Among six classifiers evaluated, Logistic Regression with TF-IDF features achieved the best macro F1 of 0.5961. The findings offer methodological and substantive contributions to computational legal studies in the Indian context.

title: Adapting MCP and A2A in the Banking Ecosystem
authors: Muthu Selvam
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) integrates MCP and A2A for privacy-preserving and intelligent mobile banking personalisation. FL allows decentralised model training on client devices, protecting sensitive user data. MCP manages federated context modelling and model orchestration, whereas A2A coordinates A2A (automation) and A2A (transactional) interactions in the paper's dual-layer design. This reduces privacy leakage and enables dispersed financial institutions to update their models continually. An experimental assessment utilizing synthetic banking activity datasets and anonymized UPI transaction streams reveals a 35% latency reduction, a 42% gain in A2A settlement efficiency, and a 22% decrease in false positives for fraud detection. Further statistical verification and replication are needed to confirm these empirical results, a study limitation. With permission verification and cryptographic auditability, the modular architecture can support regulatory compliance and intelligent banking personalization.

title: Random Forest and Decision Tree-Based Predictive Maintenance of Solar Panels with Real Time Data Acquisition System
authors: Kasi Viswanathan P, Monica K M, Parameshwari Shirdhar Gowda, Veena B G, M.Balamurugan, Marish Kumar. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) performance is affected by the environmental and operational conditions, such as dust deposits, temperature changes and weakening of the materials. The work provides a live data acquisition system to predict the maintenance of solar panels. The system sustains the key performance parameters of voltage, current, surface temperature, and dust concentration by using a microcontroller. The system acquires log real-time data, whereas Python and Excel are used to analyse offline data, visualise, and draw the trend. The Offline implementation uses the Machine learning models, specifically Random Forest and Decision Tree classifiers, to assess performance degradation. With an accuracy of 96–4 percent and an R2 of 0–97 percent experimental results show that the Random Forest model accomplishes superior predictive reliability. Dust concentration and energy output are strongly correlated in the study which also identifies certain thresholds at which efficiency sharply declines. By detecting performance degradation brought on by environmental stressors machine learning models offer a highly accurate and dependable solution that makes proactive maintenance possible.

title: Optimization of STI corner rounding by In-situ Steam Generation process in 50nm NOR device
authors: Yuan-Feng Xu, Ye-Tao Lu, Jing-Wen Nan, Guan-Cheng Qiu, Yu-Ling Shao, Rui Zhao, Min Zuo, Cheng Yu, Xing Wang, Fang-Ming Xu, Yi-Hua Shen, Zhen-Sheng Xue, Huang Liu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Shallow trench isolation (STI) technology serves as a crucial element in semiconductor process technology and finds extensive application in integrated circuits with process feature sizes of 0.25μm and smaller. The fabrication of the STI structure primarily encompasses etching to create the STI trench, sidewall oxidation of the trench, chemical vapor deposition (CVD) of SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> to fill the trench, and chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) to remove the excess oxide. The sidewall oxidation following etching (Liner oxide) functions to repair etching-induced damage and round the top and bottom corners of the STI structure. The in-situ steam generation (ISSG) oxidation process, owing to its superior film quality and isotropic growth characteristics, is the favored approach for STI liner formation. This paper delves into the means of achieving improved bottom rounded corners while maintaining the roundness of the top corners through the adjustment of ISSG process parameters.

title: Efficient Error Control Coding using A Novel SPDEC Scheme with Soft-Decision Decoding
authors: M M Raghavendra, Suman Turpati, T.Syed Akheel, A Swetha, M.V Sruthi, K Siva Praveen Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Reliable communication in modern digital systems demands effective error detection and correction mechanisms to counteract channel noise and interference; therefore, Error Correcting Codes (ECCs) are essential for preserving data integrity. This research presents a new Single and Partial Double Error Correcting (SPDEC) code, to modify all the errors of single-bit and selectively address double-bit, thereby achieving an efficient balance between redundancy and error-correcting capabilities.The PDEC component of the SPDEC framework is realized through parity-check matrices (H-matrices) for both 4-bit and 8-bit word lengths, enabling an examination of complexity-performance trade-offs. Furthermore, Soft Decision Decoding (SDD) is utilized to improve reliability by leveraging probabilistic information derived from the received signal. To support the practical hardware implementation of multi-level error protection, the system uses a BCH(15,7) encoder-memory-decoder design, which complements the advanced coding method. The BCH encoder employs polynomial division via an LFSR-based structure to expand 7-bit data into a 15-bit codeword, thereby ensuring single-error correction and multi-error detection. After the codeword is created, it's stored and then retrieved using a synchronous memory module. This method mimics how communication and data storage work in the real world. Following this, the BCH decoder calculates syndromes to identify potential error patterns, which then guides the correction process. Therefore, the SPDEC framework, when combined with BCH hardware, offers a strong, scalable, and efficient solution for digital communication systems that require high reliability. This results in better coding gains, fewer error floors, and the ability to be used in both wired and wireless systems.

title: Passenger-Comfort-Aware Hierarchical Multi-Mode Braking Control with Physics-Based Feedforward Compensation for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Berke Miraç, Osman Sezgin Temel, Can Ercan, Elif Toy Aziziaghdam
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: The ability to accurately maintain longitudinal motion control is important for autonomous vehicles to operate safely and effectively in many traffic and road situations. This study proposes a hierarchical control algorithm in order to manage cruise and braking behaviors by utilizing planned stop locations and real-time obstacle perception. The control algorithm consists of four operating brake modes: Cruise, Smooth Stop, Hard Stop and Emergency Stop. These four modes provide rules for transitioning between modes dynamically that depend on requested stops, obstacles in the environment, and available stopping distance. In cruise mode, a PID controller is employed to minimize velocity tracking error, and the reference velocity is adapted according to road geometry and traffic constraints to ensure safe and comfortable motion. For both smooth stop and hard stop, a particular deceleration profile is followed that matches the requirements of the situation. Finally, regardless of mode, a single feedforward compensation term was integrated in all modes to track and compensate for aerodynamic drag, rolling resistance, and slope grade of the road, respectively. The inclusion of the feedforward compensation term improved both speed tracking performance and braking performance with varying load applied and at changing terrain. The proposed control algorithm was implemented and evaluated on the Otokar e-CENTRO autonomous vehicle platform, successfully demonstrating accurate velocity tracking and safe stopping behavior in urban driving environments.

title: Hyper-Localized Weather Forecasting Using Spatial Interpolation and Deep Learning
authors: Abdullah Junejo, Muhammad Khubaib, Owais Waheed, Kushal Chandani, Faisal Alvi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Hyper-localized weather forecasting is essential for effective disaster preparedness, agricultural planning, and public safety in regions with diverse terrain and limited data infrastructure. This paper presents a hybrid approach that integrates spatial interpolation using Universal Kriging with deep learning-based temporal forecasting using models such as LSTM, XGBoost and SARIMA-X to address sparse weather station coverage. We utilize high-resolution weather data recorded at minute intervals from various cities in northern and central Pakistan. Model performance is evaluated using standard metrics including RMSE, MAE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Universal Kriging, enhanced with elevation-based drift, achieved near-perfect interpolation with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values approaching 1.0. For 48-hour temperature forecasting, LSTM with multi-station lagged features reached an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.961. Our results demonstrate that combining geostatistical interpolation with temporal forecasting substantially improves prediction accuracy, offering a scalable solution for localized weather intelligence in data-sparse regions.

title: Regional Attention-Enhanced Swin Transformer for Clinically Relevant Medical Image Captioning
authors: Zubia Naz, Farhan Asghar, Muhammad Ishfaq Hussain, Yahya Hadadi, Muhammad Aasim Rafique, Wookjin Choi, Moongu Jeon
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Automated medical image captioning translates complex radiological images into diagnostic narratives that can support reporting workflows. We present a Swin–BART encoder–decoder system with a lightweight regional attention module that amplifies diagnostically salient regions before cross-attention. Trained and evaluated on ROCO, our model achieves state-of-the-art semantic fidelity while remaining compact and interpretable. We report results as mean±std over three seeds and include 95% confidence intervals. Compared with baselines, our approach improves ROUGE (proposed 0.603, ResNet-CNN 0.356, BLIP2-OPT 0.255) and BERTScore (proposed 0.807, BLIP2-OPT 0.645, ResNet-CNN 0.623), with competitive BLEU, CIDEr, and METEOR. We further provide ablations (regional attention on/off and token-count sweep), per-modality analysis (CT/MRI/X-ray), paired significance tests, and qualitative heatmaps that visualize the regions driving each description. Decoding uses beam search (beam size = 4), length penalty = 1.1, no_repeat_ngram_size = 3, and max length = 128. The proposed design yields accurate, clinically phrased captions and transparent regional attributions, supporting safe research use with a human in the loop.

title: Tools and Frameworks for AI on the Edge
authors: Johannes Geier, Mikhael Djajapermana, Phillipp van Kempen, Ulf Schlichtmann
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: EdgeAI deployment requires retargeting neural networks to diverse, resource-constrained platforms while meeting strict latency, memory, energy, and reliability constraints. This work overviews open, compiler-based toolflows that enable performance-driven design space exploration across models, compiler configurations, and targets. We highlight AI compiler retargeting with TVM and IREE and their trade-offs in memory and runtime, automated benchmarking and deployment with MLonMCU for scalable exploration, hardware-aware NAS for TinyML, and the role of optimized kernel libraries and hardware abstraction (e.g., RISC-V vectorized kernels). Furthermore, we showcase our compiler- and kernel-level fault-tolerance techniques for safety-critical inference. Together, these components form a practical toolbox for efficient and reliable AI at the edge.

title: A GNN-based Surrogate Device Simulation Model for MOSFETs
authors: Jiayi Xia, Dong Ni, Siwei Fu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: To accelerate TCAD simulation without the errors from mesh interpolation, this paper introduces a graph neural network (GNN) that operates directly on unstructured mesh data. A custom pipeline converts TCAD simulations of a 2D 180nm MOSFET into a graph representation. The developed GNN model then accurately predicts the device's current-voltage characteristics, achieving an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.96. This approach demonstrates an efficient and accurate alternative to conventional simulation methods.

title: AI-ML and IoT-Enabled Fog-Assisted Image Processing Framework Employing CNN’s
authors: Rani Ramrao Patil, A. Shaji George, Ganesh S. Wedpathak, Supriya Shree, Harishchander Anandaram, T.C. Manjunath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: In this research paper, we present the AI–ML and IoT-Enabled Fog-Assisted Image Processing Framework Employing Convolutional Neural Networks for Automated Detection along with the simulation results. Early and effective detection of pneumonia will ensure good clinical management of patients. A Fog- Assisted CNN Model is proposed in this study to quickly and precisely classify pneumonia from CXR images. The model uses Convolution Neural Networks implemented in TensorFlow for pneumonia or normal classification. A dataset of 6,000 images of X-rays was used; this was augmented with various data augmentation techniques to allow for generalization and to reduce overfitting. To leave room for real-time deployment, it combines fog computing, thereby bringing computational resources closer to the sources of data. This reduces latency, optimizes the use of used bandwidth, and increases privacy with data, making the model suitable for limited cloud-connected healthcare environments. Integrating deep learning and distributed fog computing, FACM-CXR provides an efficient solution for the automated diagnosis of pneumonia. Simulations are carried out in Matlab & the results obtained justifies the power of the work developed & presented here.

title: Gain-Based Neural Secure Protection Control for Feedforward Nonlinear Systems with Unknown Control Coefficients and Impulsive FDI Attacks
authors: Debao Fan, Qingrong Liu, Rong Su, Xianfu Zhang, Wenjie Zhang
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: This paper proposes a gain-based neural secure protection (GBNSP) control scheme for feedforward nonlinear systems subject to unknown control coefficients and impulsive false data injection (FDI) attacks. Notably, the nonlinear functions of the systems are relaxed to any continuous functions and the control coefficients are permitted to be constants with both unknown sizes and signs, a scenario not covered in existing works. Furthermore, the uncertain abrupt changes in system states caused by impulsive FDI attacks inevitably exacerbate the challenges in control design. To this end, this paper integrates the neural network technique and the gain control method to propose a novel GBNSP control scheme. Specifically, the neural network technique effectively compensates for strong nonlinearities and uncertainties, while the gain control method quantifies the tolerable frequency of impulsive FDI attacks and avoids the tedious design procedures. It is shown that, under the designed GBNSP controller, all closed-loop signals remain bounded and the system states eventually converge to an adjustable neighborhood near the origin. Moreover, an enhanced GBNSP control scheme incorporates an improved gain scaling mechanism to withstand unknown external disturbances. In the end, the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed scheme are validated by a theoretical example and a practical example.

title: Optimized Machine Learning Approach for Neonatal Jaundice Detection Using HOG-SVM Classification
authors: Poshitha M, J Maruthu Pandi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Newborns frequently suffer from neonatal jaundice, which is evidenced by a yellowish staining of their eyes and skin. For the extraction of features from neonatal's skin and eye the The Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HoG) is utilized. In numerous automated vision assignments, the objective is to express complicated visual input in an increasingly concise and comprehensible format; in these situations, extraction of features performs an essential part. This is accomplished using HOG by concentrating on the gradient orientation distribution inside an image. HOG's main goal is to identify the orientations and local intensity gradients which are essential for labeling the forms and structures of objects. Support Vector Machines (SVM) could be utilized in the context of identifying neonatal jaundice from photos once pertinent features have been extracted. With an accuracy of 98.68%, the suggested HOG-based SVM classifier can identify between neonatal photos with jaundice and those without.

title: A Speech Measurement Component for a Serious Games–Based Patient Digital Twin
authors: André Antunes, Rui Neves Madeira, Octavian Postolache
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Speech and audio signals provide non-invasive access to cognitive and motor aspects relevant to therapeutic assessment. This paper presents a speech-based measurement component integrated into a serious games-based patient digital twin (DT) for therapy. The component supports audio acquisition, preprocessing, and word-level speech classification via a remotely deployed machine learning service. As a use case, a convolutional neural network-based speech classifier is integrated into a production serious-game speech therapy application and evaluated across pediatric, adult, and large-scale public speech datasets. Experimental results highlight the impact of data quality, dataset scale, and population characteristics on classification performance under realistic acquisition conditions. Our solution demonstrates the feasibility of operationalizing speech as a realtime measurement modality within an interactive DT platform.

title: Transformer-Based Predictive Maintenance for Risk-Aware Instrument Calibration
authors: Adithya Parthasarathy, Aswathnarayan Muthukrishnan Kirubakaran, Akshay Deshpande, Ram Sekhar Bodala, Suhas Malempati, Nachiappan Chockalingam, Vinoth Punniyamoorthy, Seema Gangaiah Aarella
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Accurate calibration is essential for instruments whose measurements must remain traceable, reliable, and compliant over long operating periods. Fixed-interval programs are easy to administer, but they ignore that instruments drift at different rates under different conditions. This paper studies calibration scheduling as a predictive maintenance problem: given recent sensor histories, estimate time-to-drift (TTD) and intervene before a violation occurs. We adapt the NASA C-MAPSS benchmark into a calibration setting by selecting drift-sensitive sensors, defining virtual calibration thresholds, and inserting synthetic reset events that emulate repeated recalibration. We then compare classical regressors, recurrent and convolutional sequence models, and a compact Transformer for TTD prediction. The Transformer provides the strongest point forecasts on the primary FD001 split and remains competitive on the harder FD002–FD004 splits, while a quantile-based uncertainty model supports conservative scheduling when drift behavior is noisier. Under a violation-aware cost model, predictive scheduling lowers cost relative to reactive and fixed policies, and uncertainty-aware triggers sharply reduce violations when point forecasts are less reliable. The results show that condition-based calibration can be framed as a joint forecasting and decision problem, and that combining sequence models with risk-aware policies is a practical route toward smarter calibration planning.

title: Spam Email Detection Using a Custom Multi-Head DistilBERT
authors: Wadaq Tarek Eghsais, E. Emary, Hala Abbas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The exponential growth of spam emails poses a persistent and evolving threat to cybersecurity, organizational productivity, and user privacy. Traditional machine-learning approaches that depend on surface-level keyword patterns miss the contextual and semantic signals embedded in modern spam. This paper presents a novel custom Multi-Head DistilBERT classifier with three parallel projection heads (256, 128, and 64 dimensions) concatenated into a 448-dimensional fused representation. The model is evaluated on the Enron corpus (33,650 emails) against TF-IDF+Logistic Regression, BiLSTM, and Text-CNN, achieving 99.13% accuracy and 0.9995 ROC-AUC. Under 10-fold cross-validation with full per-fold retraining, it attains 0.9890±0.0021 mean accuracy, the highest across all models. Paired t-tests and DeLong tests confirm statistically significant superiority over all three baselines. The false negative rate of 0.97% underscores the model’s value in cybersecurity applications where missed spam carries operational risk.

title: University Evaluation Process: Structure Oriented Data Analyze Based on Sure Model
authors: Uranchimeg Tudevdagva, Ulam-Orgil Choijiljav, Erdenekhuu Norinpel
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This paper describes the evaluation process of Mongolian University of Science and Technology (MUST) and its data analyze based on the structure-oriented evaluation (SURE) model. The survey questions are divided into six main groups. Each group has sub questions which measures qualitative units from very bad to very good in six levels. By this survey data is collected from all nine schools of the MUST in each semester. In this paper explains data analyze of Winter semester 2023/2024 by the descriptive statistics methods and by the SURE data processing methods. In total 14993 students are responded to the survey voluntarily. The findings showed that quality of the survey questions need to be improved based int international accreditation criteria. By the comparison of data processing two methods found out that school ranking by mean cannot be confident by the SURE model. Therefore, for the next round of the evaluation need to apply at least three different data processing methods and need to do comparison analyzes to check and proof the final results of the school's evaluation and ranking.

title: Advancing Image Generation: Integrating Transformer-Based Latent Diffusion With Semantic Clip Embeddings
authors: Suthir Sriram, Karthikeyan, Nivethitha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Blending together the word descriptions to come up with images is a complicated process that borders both language comprehension and creativity. It is a Lexico-Visual Synergy Diffuser A Hierarchical Transformer Framework for Contextual Image Synthesis (LVSD-HTFCIS) that is a new framework, which combines CLIP-trained semantic embeddings with a hierarchical transformer-based latent diffusion regimen. Our model has a high ability to turn complex textual inputs into visually consistent outputs with a bountiful output of FID of 13.21. Through Swin transformers, squeeze-and-excitation and multi-scale feature fusion signals LVSD-HTFCIS skillfully focuses on all the appropriate features of the image, which can lead to high-resolution images that can accurately depict the input semantics. The development marks the dawn of a new era in generative modeling, and it has applications in creative AI applications in content generation, among others.

title: Development of an Artificial Intelligence-Based Framework for the Detection and Classification of Papaya Leaf Diseases
authors: Ahmed M. Salaheldin, Neven Saleh, Yasser Ismail, Shimaa M. Khder
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Papaya is a high-value tropical fruit crop that suffers from multiple foliar diseases. Conventional disease detection relies on manual inspection, which is often subjective, time-consuming, and prone to error. This study proposes a transfer learning–based framework utilizing fine-tuned AlexNet and VGG16 networks for the automated classification of papaya leaf diseases. Image preprocessing includes resizing and normalization to ensure compatibility with the applied networks. The networks were trained using stochastic gradient descent with momentum as an optimizer, with settings optimized for efficiency and generalization. A dataset of 6,618 samples, obtained through image augmentation, has been employed. The proposed AlexNet model outperformed the VGG16, achieving promising performance, with an overall accuracy of 99.20%, a sensitivity of 97.79%, specificity of 99.49%. The findings confirm the effectiveness of transfer learning in enhancing papaya leaf disease detection and classification. The approach can also be extended to other crops for broader agricultural disease management.

title: Flexible Scheduling of Hyperscale Data Center Loads Under Transmission Constraints
authors: Emmanuel Baskerville, Kamar Mann, Cyrus Booker, Brenden Forrest, Moses Garuba, Cunzhi Zhao, Xiang Huo
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of hyperscale data centers (DCs) introduces large, geographically concentrated, and highly uncertain electrical demands that challenge existing transmission operation practices. Unlike traditional load growth, which evolves gradually and is spatially distributed, hyperscale DC deployments exhibit sharp demand increases at specific network locations, significantly impacting power flow patterns, congestion, and system costs. To address these challenges, this paper develops a network-constrained optimization framework to manage the influence of DC load growth on transmission system performance. The objective function incorporates key components of electricity cost minimization, congestion mitigation, and temporal smoothness of DC load variation. Simulations are conducted on the modified IEEE 14-bus system under different DC load control scenarios. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach compared to the conventional greedy (non-coordinated) DC load scheduling.

title: Design and analysis requirements for an electrical stimulation research platform for infection control in osseointegrated bionic interfaces
authors: Maite Gil-Prandi, Max Ortiz-Catalán, Julián Oreggioni
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Infections at the bone-implant interface remain a major limitation of osseointegrated bionic limb prostheses, often leading to prolonged treatment, additional surgeries, or implant failure. Electrical stimulation has emerged as a promising adjunctive strategy to prevent bacterial colonization and mitigate biofilm formation, provided that stimulation is delivered in a safe and controlled manner suitable for longterm use. This work presents the design rationale and requirements analysis of a research platform intended to deliver alternating, charge-balanced electrical stimulation through an existing osseointegrated prosthetic interface. The proposed system is designed for autonomous, long-duration therapy delivery during periods when the prosthesis is not worn, while supporting supervised clinical interaction for configuration, monitoring, and data retrieval. A structured requirements engineering process was followed, informed by clinical use cases, existing prosthetic system constraints, and applicable medical device standards. Key functional, safety, and usability requirements are identified, and a modular hardware and embedded software architecture is proposed to isolate safety-critical stimulation functions from communication and telemetry tasks. Preliminary experimental results obtained on the prototype validate core system functionality. This paper represents a work in progress, focusing on system requirements and architectural design of the research platform. Experimental validation and clinical evaluation will be addressed in future work.

title: A comprehensive deep-learning method for the Multi-level Diabetic Retinopathy-classification with Clinical Interpretation using Grad-CAM
authors: Tripty Singh, Chebrolu Sai Kumar, Narahari Sai Jaswanth, Payel Patra, Prakash Duraisamy, Rishabh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: One of the most serious complications of diabetes that may result in irreversible loss of sight unless prevented at an early stage is Diabetic Retinopathy (DR). Early detection of DR is a decisive factor in how well blindness can be removed by treatment. The present paper is devoted to the design of an automated system for the detection of DR based on the fundus images of the retina, which is the primary source of the diagnosis of retinal damage. The collected images are pre-processed and downsized to enhance the quality and variety of the received data in a proper manner to reach the right and trusty detection. The system classifies the imaging according to the grade of DR and identifies the areas with the involvement in the retina that provide retinographically readable and actable output. This approach will not only improve the work of medical professionals and simplify and accelerate the early diagnosis of the disease but also reduce the risk of losing visual capabilities among diabetics due to the high rate of the disease, as medical specialists will conduct a timely and regular check-up.

title: Compliance or Profit? Decomposing Green Investment and Firm Performance
authors: Xinying Fu, Aram Bahrini
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Green investment is often treated as a single aggregate measure, which can obscure whether firms are building underlying capabilities or primarily paying for compliance. This paper proposes a dictionary-based textual measure that extracts and decomposes capitalized environmental expenditures disclosed by Chinese A-share firms from 2018 to 2023 into pollution-prevention and pollution-control investments. The measure is constructed by mapping investment-related disclosure text to these categories, retaining only capitalized environmental expenditures and aggregating them to the firm-year level. Using panel regressions with industry and year as fixed effects, we find that total green investment is positively associated with contemporaneous profitability. When decomposed, both prevention-oriented and control-oriented investments are significantly associated with better environmental performance. The decomposition yields an important insight: prevention-oriented investment exhibits a stronger association with both environmental and financial performance, consistent with efficiency and capability-building channels. These findings are associative rather than strictly causal. This pattern suggests that, in China’s regulatory and policy environment, compliance-oriented spending can generate near-term financial benefits through lower expected enforcement costs and disruption risk, greater access to policy-linked subsidies or financing, and stronger signals of regulatory credibility that ease financing frictions. Results are robust across alternative specifications and policy-based analyses. Overall, the study shows that measurement choice matters. Moving from an aggregate measure to a decomposition into investment types leads to different conclusions and provides a practical way to evaluate green investment portfolios and inform policy design. More broadly, the approach offers a measurement framework that can be extended to international settings with comparable corporate disclosure regimes.

title: LiteSegFormer: An End-To-End Optimization And Deployment Approach For Efficient SegFormer Based Semantic Segmentation
authors: Sreejith S, Savitha DK, Lakshmi Annamalai, Arshad Jamal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Semantic segmentation is a core component of scene understanding systems, requiring both high pixel-level accuracy and efficient execution for practical deployment. Transformer based architectures such as Seg Former have demonstrated strong representational capability; however, their direct deployment is often constrained by computational and memory overheads. This paper presents Lite Seg Former, an end-to-end optimization and deployment approach built upon the SegFormer-B0 architecture, designed to transform a standard model into a deployment-ready system without sacrificing segmentation performance. To reduce complexity, the segmentation task is reformulated to predict 50 target semantic classes along with a single background class, while aggregating all non-target categories into the background. The proposed pipeline systematically integrates structured channel pruning based on L2-norm analysis, distillation-based fine-tuning to recover post-pruning accuracy, and post-training static INT8 quantization to reduce numerical precision overhead. The optimized model is further converted to ONNX format and deployed using NVIDIA Tensor RT for accelerated inference. Experimental evaluation demonstrates substantial reductions in model size, GPU memory usage, and inference latency while maintaining reliable segmentation quality across diverse scene categories, confirming the suitability of Lite Seg Former for deployment-critical environments.

title: Audit-Ready Fraud Detection: Aligning Gradient Boosting and SHAP with GDPR and SOX Compliance
authors: Deepak Pai, Karthik Pappu, Yogeesh Kunigal Gangaiah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Financial fraud detection requires models that are both accurate on highly imbalanced transaction data and explainable for regulatory compliance under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 22 and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) Section 404. Most prior studies on fraud detection optimize for accuracy or area under the ROC curve (AUC) while neglecting post-hoc explainability and subgroup robustness required in audit contexts. This study compares four machine learning algorithms, namely Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and a multilayer perceptron (MLP), on 284,807 real credit card transactions exhibiting a 577.9:1 class imbalance. XGBoost achieves the highest discrimination with ROC-AUC of 0.983 and precision-recall AUC (PR-AUC) of 0.875, reducing the false positive rate to approximately 0.02% at optimized thresholds. SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis identifies the PCA components V14, V4, and V12 as the three most influential features driving fraud predictions. Subgroup stability analysis across transaction amount quartiles and temporal periods confirms consistent model performance (F1 ranging from 0.83 to 0.92) with no degradation in any segment. Threshold optimization at 0.73 yields 93.2% precision while maintaining 83.7% recall. These results provide empirical evidence that gradient boosting methods combined with SHAP explainability can deliver audit-ready fraud detection aligned with regulatory transparency mandates.

title: Dependency-Aware Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Planning: Graph-Based Optimization for Enterprise Infrastructure
authors: Saurabh Sharma, Karthik Pappu, Ankit Parashar, Navin Suvarna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The transition to post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) algorithms requires enterprises to migrate thousands of cryptographic assets from vulnerable algorithms (RSA, ECDSA, DH, DSA) to NIST-standardized replacements (ML-KEM, ML-DSA), but uncoordinated migration introduces interoperability failures when migrated assets cannot communicate with unmigrated dependents. This paper presents an automated migration planning platform that builds a cryptographic dependency graph, computes quantum risk scores with graph centrality, and generates an optimized migration plan using reversed topological ordering with risk-weighted prioritization. A factorial experiment (3 scales × 3 strategies × 10 seeds = 90 runs) on simulated enterprises with 100 to 10,000 assets shows that the dependency-aware strategy reduces cumulative quantum risk exposure 26% faster than random ordering (p < 0.001), eliminates 88.6% of interoperability failures (p = 0.028), and scales from 14% AUC improvement at 100 nodes to 41% at 10,000 nodes. The core mechanism is processing dependency graph leaves first, ensuring that when a server transitions to PQC, its dependent applications already speak the new protocol.

title: Fault Classification in Transmission Lines Using a Hybrid CNN–LSTM Model With Aquila Optimization
authors: V.Rajesh Kumar, Divya Muralitharan, Ravindran S, P.Thiruselvan, Mahesh K, Elaiyaraja P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Fault detection and classification in high-voltage transmission lines are essential for maintaining the reliable and stable operation of modern power systems. In practical environments, conventional protection techniques often suffer from reduced accuracy due to noise, parameter uncertainties, and nonlinear transient behaviour. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, optimized using the Aquila Optimizer (AO) for improved fault classification performance.In the proposed model, CNN is employed to extract spatial features from voltage and current waveforms, while LSTM captures the corresponding temporal dependencies. The Aquila Optimizer adaptively tunes critical hyperparameters, including the learning rate, number of filters, and LSTM units, thereby enhancing convergence speed and generalization capability. The framework is evaluated using simulated data from a 400 kV transmission line system under twelve fault categories. The results show that the proposed approach achieves an accuracy of 99.36%, while reducing training time by 58% and energy consumption by 35% compared to existing methods.

title: Ultra-Flat Surface and Gradient Etching for Advanced Application Using Ion Beam Trimming
authors: Zichao Li, Jiao Li, Shuangyan Song, Dongdong Hu, Kaidong Xu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Ion beam trimming technology (IBT) represents a precise method for fabricating ultra-flat thin films and gradient films. Utilizing a small, focused ion beam, IBT enables high-precision modification of film thickness, offering significant value for advanced applications in optics, semiconductor manufacturing, and nanotechnology. Through a meticulously crafted IBT process, an ultra-flat surface with a thickness variation of less than 1 nm has been successfully achieved. Furthermore, IBT has been employed to prepare gradient surfaces with controlled, large slopes, demonstrating its versatility in surface engineering. Film thicknesses were accurately characterized using a Filmetrics F5X optical measurement system.

title: Adaptive Semiconductor Process Optimization Based on Batch Bayesian Optimization
authors: Xuanpei Zhai, Huijie Mao, Xinnan Ren, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This study proposes a data-efficient and adaptive semiconductor process optimization framework based on Bayesian Optimization (BO). Using Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), we construct a probabilistic surrogate model to map process parameters to performance metrics. The framework integrates a q-Expected Improvement (q-EI) acquisition function to generate parallel experimental candidates, which are screened by domain experts for feasibility. Experiments on a synthetic benchmark (Rosenbrock function) demonstrate rapid convergence, high predictive accuracy, and significant reduction in sample cost, validating the method’s effectiveness for practical semiconductor manufacturing.

title: A Hybrid Asset Scoring Framework Combining Deterministic Quantitative Screening with RAG-Informed LLM Qualitative Evaluation for Portfolio Universe Reduction
authors: Joude Samir Azzam, Saber Elsayed, Saad Harous
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Existing optimization methods mainly utilize backwards looking quantitative averages to construct investment portfolios, disregarding all qualitative dimensions that could potentially add to the optimization process . In this study, we combine deterministic quantitative scoring with RAG-enhanced Large Language Model (LLM) qualitative processing capability to produce a two-layer comprehensive asset scoring framework. In the first layer quantitative, profile-specific composites are calculated deterministically and fed as read-only context to the LLM mitigating financial figure hallucination risk. As for the second layer, the LLM is responsible for qualitative composite scores using up-to-date news and analysts’ insights. Final investability scores are obtained by combining quantitative and qualitative composites, producing ranked and tagged outputs suitable for downstream optimization. Evaluated on 100 firms from the S&P 500, the hard disqualifier gate eliminates 47 percent of firms prior to LLM application. RAG-retrieved context produced bidirectional classification changes in 12 firms relative to a non-RAG baseline. Furthermore, low Spearman correlation coefficient between quantitative and qualitative composites indicated that the two layers capture independent dimensions of investability. Cross-profile validation further shows that the capital appreciation profile produces a statistically significant correlation with analyst consensus, while the income profile does not, confirming that the profile conditioning mechanism produces structurally differentiated scoring outcomes aligned with each investor objective.

title: An Intelligent Decision Support Framework for Relevant and Responsive Capacity-Building in Start-Up Cooperatives
authors: Dahlee S. Pascua
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The sustainability of start-up cooperatives depends on the timely identification of organizational needs and the design of interventions that are both relevant and responsive to local conditions. This study presents an intelligent decision support framework for capacity-building in start-up cooperatives based on an empirical needs assessment of a savings and credit cooperative in Isabela, Philippines. A descriptive mixed-methods design was employed, involving 32 respondents composed of cooperative members, officers, and board directors selected through purposive sampling. Data were gathered using structured questionnaires, interviews, and focused group discussions, then analyzed through descriptive statistics and thematic analysis. Findings revealed strong demand for training and technical assistance in financial management, bookkeeping, strategic planning, cooperative governance, leadership, entrepreneurship, marketing, and livelihood development. The assessment also identified critical operational gaps, including insufficient policies and guidelines, limited training access, weak debt collection controls, inadequate bookkeeping capacity, and a lack of governance-related competencies among officers and board members. In response, the proposed framework structures decision-making into four phases: needs identification, intervention planning, mentoring and capability support, and monitoring and governance reinforcement. By translating assessment results into prioritized capacity-building actions, the framework serves as a decision support mechanism for higher education institutions, government agencies, and development partners engaged in cooperative extension. The study highlights how data-informed and structured decision processes can improve the design of sustainable support programs for emerging cooperatives.

title: Testing of Large Language Model in Indian Legal Datasets: A Comparative Semantic Study
authors: Jigar Kumar, Divyansh Singh, Gunjan Aggarwal, Swati Singal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: From answering multipurpose questions to focused applications Large Language Models (LLM) are being utilized everywhere. Nevertheless, their reliability in high-stakes, domain-specific areas such as the legal system remains underexplored. Legal question answering requires not only linguistic fluency but also precise handling of domain knowledge, making it an ideal testbed for evaluating model effectiveness. This paper presents a systematic comparison of open-source and proprietary LLMs on a curated dataset of 24,000 Indian legal question–answer pairs. We evaluate open-weight models such as Flan-T5-XL (3B), LLaMA-2-7B-Chat-HF, and Mistral-7B-Instruct, and contrast their performance with frontier proprietary systems including GPT-4, Claude, and Gemini Pro. Model outputs are assessed against gold answers using semantic similarity with multiple thresholds (0.5, 0.6, 0.7), and results are recorded in terms of F1-score, recall, precision, and accuracy. Our findings show that while open-source mid-sized models demonstrate moderate capability (e.g., Mistral-7B achieving an F1 of 0.71), they fall short compared to proprietary models, which deliver substantially higher and more balanced scores. The study not only Illustrates the promise of LLMs for legal applications but also the gap that remains for open-weight models in achieving domain-level reliability.

title: Multimodal Signal Acquisition System for Estimation of Time Varying Elastance of Left Ventricle
authors: S R Ragaja, G Jaganathan, V Raj Kiran, P M Nabeel, Jayaraj Joseph
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Left ventricular elastance is a measure that characterizes the mechanical behaviour of the left ventricle while it pumps blood. Elastance is derived from pressure and volume measurements obtained via invasive catheterization. Non-invasive approaches to study elastance typically involve determining ventricular volume and pressure through imaging techniques such as magnetic resonance imaging or echocardiography or use mathematical models to derive these parameters. In this study, we propose a novel method for non-invasively estimating elastance using measured carotid artery distention data, ejection fraction, cardiac output, and seismocardiogram data. Parameters derived from the sensed outputs are carotid pressure, ventricular volume at the end of systole and diastole, and the timing of left ventricular valves’ opening and closing. These parameters are integrated into the Double Hill function model for computing left ventricular elastance. Using the proposed method, time-varying elastance curves were generated for 20 subjects (9 females and 11 males). Visual analysis of the normalised elastance plots indicates that the shape of the estimated elastance curve aligns well with expected physiological patterns. Additionally, the simulated elastance plots derived from the model exhibit strong beat-to-beat repeatability over 20 cardiac cycles of a single participant.

title: The Role of Generative AI in Audiovisual Production: Opportunities, Limitations and Emerging Workflows
authors: Sónia Ferreira, Vanessa Campos
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is significantly reshaping audiovisual production processes, introducing new possibilities for automation, efficiency, and creative support. This paper presents a conceptual and critical analysis of the role of GenAI tools across the main stages of audiovisual production: preproduction, production, and post-production. Drawing on a systematic review of recent literature, the study examines how these technologies are being integrated into workflows, highlighting their potential to assist in tasks such as scriptwriting, content generation, editing, and distribution. The analysis identifies key benefits of GenAI adoption, including increased productivity, reduced operational costs, and greater access to audiovisual creation. However, it also emphasizes important limitations, particularly regarding creative control, output quality, technical constraints, and ethical concerns. Based on these insights, the paper proposes a conceptual framework for integrating GenAI tools in audiovisual production, positioning artificial intelligence as a complementary system that enhances – but does not replace – human creativity and professional expertise. The findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the transformative impact of GenAI in the audiovisual domain and provide a structured perspective for researchers and practitioners seeking to integrate these technologies effectively into creative workflows.

title: Naut AI: Design of a Novel AI-Driven Robotic Fish for Neritic Zone Exploration
authors: Ryan Zou
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Environmental issues such as global warming and pollution have created a cascade of consequences that ultimately affect the health and functionality of the ocean. To tackle this issue this paper aims to design and implement an AI-Driven Robotic fish through biomimicry, in order to address the lack of non-invasive monitoring techniques and the inability to influence the behavior of fish. Naut AI employed a mass adjusting system to control the pitch/roll, and a single servo motor was used to create undulatory locomotion. Through the single servo the design is able to achieve a top speed of 26.40 cm/s. In addition to this, the proposed solution achieved a minimum turning radius of 27 cm when turning left and 12 cm when turning right. Additionally, the mass-adjusting system allowed for pitch-controlled diving at a max speed of 21.10 cm/s. Naut AI also uses computer vision in order to track colored objects. In the future, this idea can be used to create advanced monitoring systems that are able to identify changes in appearance that cannot be detected through traditional sensors. Furthermore, Naut AI is capable of swimming alongside the Koi fish, without disturbing or scaring the Koi away, laying the foundation to influence the movements and behaviors of real fish through an independent robotic device. Ultimately, Naut AI has various applications such as non-invasive monitoring in mariculture farms and shallow oceans, leading or herding fish away from pollutants and other hazards, and even zoning in the fishing industry to reduce bycatch.

title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches in Wireless Sensor Networks Challenges: Survey
authors: Bayan Hashr Alamri, Ahmed Barnawi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Real-time wireless sensor network (WSN) technology and applications increasingly becoming integral to numerous aspects of daily life, driving the demand for advanced artificial intelligence (AI) technologies that can replace traditional methods in this field. These AI technologies use a variety of techniques, including machine learning (ML), neural networks (NNs), deep learning (DL), fuzzy logic, swarm intelligence, and more. This survey presents current state-of-the-art research on the primary challenges related to WSNs, focusing on contributions, methodologies, strengths, and limitations. Key interrelated areas of investigation include node localization, data collection, aggregation and dissemination, monitoring, and security within WSNs. The findings demonstrate the potential of AI techniques to significantly improve and optimize the efficiency, accuracy, and robustness of WSN applications and operations. Several challenges and open research directions remain, including reducing transmission power, minimizing computational complexity, and ensuring adaptability to various network topologies.

title: Vision-Language Model Classification of Neonatal Resuscitation Interventions
authors: Zarif Khan, Arun Karki, Anthony Fuller, Erika Barciak, JoAnn Harrold, James R. Green
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: One in ten newborns requires assistance to establish cardio-respiratory stability. The Neonatal Resuscitation Program (NRP) defines a standardized algorithm of specific interventions to be administered in sequence. These interventions are performed rapidly and often simultaneously by multiple team members. A complete narrative summary of each resuscitation is manually recorded by a healthcare team member for quality assurance and medicolegal documentation, which motivates the development of an AI Scribe to automatically detect, classify, and accurately summarize all interventions administered. Such a system will likely require processing multimodal and multiview sensor data, but currently no such system exists, nor does the data required to create it. As a first step, we investigate the potential to fine-tune a state-of-the-art video-language model (VLM), LLaVAVideo, for the purpose of classifying neonatal resuscitation interventions from RGB video. In this pilot study, we collect 20 diverse, publicly available neonatal resuscitation training videos, supplemented with video recorded during simulated resuscitations in clinical and lab environments. Through lowrank adaptation (LoRA), our fine-tuned VLM can differentiate eight different interventions with high accuracy (88.3%) and micro-average precision (88.7%). This study represents a first step towards developing a multimodal AI Scribe for neonatal resuscitations. Such a system would help alleviate clinical documentation burden while improving documentation accuracy and, ultimately, patient care.

title: Too Nice to Be Human: Cross-Lingual Consistency in Personality-Related Response Patterns of Large Language Models
authors: Jasmin Heierli, Ilias Ehrensperger, Maurice Rüegg, Alexandre de Spindler, Elena Gavagnin
publish: 2026 IEEE Swiss Conference on Data Science and AI (SDS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in advisory and collaborative roles in multilingual contexts where behavioral appropriateness matters alongside factual correctness. One common way to operationalize such behavioral tendencies is through personality-related assessment frameworks. However, it remains unclear how the personality-related response patterns generated by aligned LLMs compare with empirically observed human variability, particularly with respect to the breadth and structure of their response space. We examine personality-related response patterns of three contemporary LLMs using the HEXACO framework in four languages (English, German, Spanish, and Finnish). We contextualize facet-level response profiles using published human reference data for the corresponding language versions and analyze them using repeated controlled questionnaire-based elicitation. Our results show that human personality assessments exhibit structured cross-linguistic variation embedded within a relatively balanced response range, whereas LLM-generated Likert-scale responses cluster within a narrow, normatively positive region of the scale. The pronounced ceiling effects on socially desirable dimensions, such as fairness and prudence, persist across models and languages. Although LLMs display some language-dependent variation at the facet level, these shifts remain localized and lack the coordinated structure observed in human reference profiles.

title: Analysis of Gate Oxide Defect Formation Causes and Improvement Methods in Poly Gate Etching
authors: Cheng Fang, Yang Wei, Jiafu Fan, Ruyi Gao, Jimmylu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Poly Gate Etching is generally applied in products such as DDIC (Display Driver Integrated Circuit), CIS (CMOS Image Sensor), and Logic chips. Due to the influence of the loading effect, temperature, and plasma distribution, the etching rates vary across different regions of the wafer, including the center/edge and Dense/ISO areas. Therefore, it is difficult to maintain completely consistent actual etching uniformity. However, we have found that by adjusting the number of cycles of Pre wafer treatment, the selectivity ratio in the over-etch step can be better controlled, thereby avoiding the risk of AA damage caused by the gate oxide being etched through.

title: A Power Optimized 32-Bit Carry Look Ahead Adder Employing PTL and NMOS Power Gated Technique for VLSI Circuits
authors: Padma Challa Y. Deekshitha, Y. Sudharani, Y. Sree Latha, T. Chandana, T. Manoj Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a 32-bit Carry Look-Ahead (CLA) Adder using Pass Transistor Logic (PTL) combined with a Power Gated NMOS technique to achieve enhanced power efficiency in deep sub-micron technology. The proposed architecture is constructed using modular 4-bit CLA blocks and hierarchically expanded to form a 32-bit structure. To reduce leakage power during idle conditions, a footer Power Gated NMOS transistor is incorporated between the logic circuit and ground, effectively cutting off the leakage current path in standby mode. The PTL based implementation reduces transistor count and switching capacitance, thereby minimizing dynamic power consumption, while the Power Gated NMOS mechanism significantly suppresses static power dissipation. The entire design is implemented using Tanner EDA tool in 22nm technology. The proposed design reduces total power consumption to 23.01% compared to conventional PTL design. Although a slight increase in transistor count is observed due to the inclusion of the Power Gated NMOS structure, the overall energy efficiency and power-delay performance are significantly improved. Therefore, the proposed 32-bit PTL based CLA with Power Gated NMOS technique is highly suitable for VLSI circuits.

title: Vision-Based Pose Measurement System for Intermodal Containers
authors: Luocheng Zheng, Mengjie He, Yujie Zhang, Zhiwei Zhang, Chao Mi
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: In container transshipment operations, environmental disturbances and limited equipment accuracy often cause coupled deviations in in-plane translation and yaw, which pose significant challenges for automated alignment and hoisting. This paper proposes a vision-based container measurement framework designed for accurate automatic alignment on a transfer platform. Targeting future deployment on industrial edge-computing units, the proposed approach adopts YOLOv11 (You Only Look Once) with MobileMamba integrated into the backbone to enhance global dependency modeling and improve target localization. Given the detected keypoints, we enforce a coplanarity constraint and leverage standard container dimensions to estimate the container pose and compute the corresponding deviations. Evaluations using image data from real port operating conditions show that the method achieves a position mean absolute error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4. 2 ~ m m}$</tex> and an angular MAE of 0.15°. These results demonstrate high accuracy and robustness in complex scenarios, validating the framework's strong potential to provide reliable, real-time deviation measurement for automated transshipment operations.
