title: Deep Learning NER Pipeline for Automated Basel III / IFRS 9 Risk Parameter Extraction from Climate Narratives
authors: Rohit Nimmala, Jagrut Nimmala, Milan Parikh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Banks must manually extract credit risk parameters, including probability of default (PD), loss given default (LGD), exposure at default (EAD), and expected credit loss (ECL), from qualitative climate scenario narratives published by central banks and TCFD-reporting firms. Existing financial NER systems target generic entity types or XBRL tags, and climate NLP methods classify disclosures at the document level but do not extract quantitative risk parameters. We introduce ClimRiskNER, a novel end-to-end pipeline that combines dual-domain adaptive pre-training (financial and climate corpora) with constrained seq2seq decoding to extract structured Basel III/IFRS 9 parameters from unstructured climate text. The pipeline uses FinBERT-Climate, a FinBERT model further pre-trained on over 2 million climate paragraphs, for token-level NER, followed by a T5-based seq2seq module with prefix-trie constrained beam search enforcing output conformity to a Basel III/IFRS 9 ontology. On a curated dataset of ECB, BoE, and NGFS climate stress test reports and TCFD disclosures, the pipeline achieves entity-level F1 of 0.89 for PD, 0.85 for LGD, and 0.82 for EAD, outperforming the next-best baseline (GPT-4 few-shot) by 9 F1 points. The system reduces extraction time by over 99% versus manual review (0.3 seconds per page versus 720 seconds) and provides SHAP-based token-level provenance for regulatory audit trails.

title: Intelligent Hybrid 6G Communication Framework with Cloud-Based Intrusion Detection and Privacy Preservation
authors: Manthan S. Manavadaria, S.V. Sugin, Manya Gupta, Dineshkumar P, Shilpa Abhang, Sunil Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: The future 6G communication network will provide ultimate connection and ultra data-rates with ultra-low latency but at the same time, it faces potential cyber-attacks on very-diffused infrastructures along with big concerns in privacy. Old style intrusion detection systems suffer from centralized information processing, slow in responding to threat and not having privacy guarantees. Here, the objective was to design advanced hybrid 6G communication system with cloud-based intrusion detection and unified privacy preservation techniques which is proposed in this study. The proposed framework makes use of edge-level traffic analysis, federated learning and differential privacy to enable collaborative real-time threat investigation while protecting raw data from direct exposure. A hybrid deep learning Architecture, analyses multi-dimensional traffic features while cloud intelligence performs global correlation and adaptive model optimization. The extensive experimentation on a large synthetic dataset indicates better performance than classical machine learning and deep learning baselines, achieving a detection accuracy of 99.21%, precision of 98.95%, recall of 99.10% and less false positive rate (0.42%). The results verify the efficiency of the proposed framework in strengthening the security resilience, scalability and privacy guarantee of future 6G communication systems.

title: Optimizing Photoresist Line-Width Roughness via Combined ICP and IBS Process Control
authors: Xiaojian Wu, Shuo Dong, Shiwei Zhuang, Kaidong Xu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of micro/nanofabrication technology nodes, LWR of PR has become one of the key factors limiting device performance and reliability. At the nanoscale, large LWR leads to increased electrical resistance in interconnects and a significant degradation in carrier mobility in fin field-effect transistors (FinFETs). This study investigates the optimization effect of the processing sequence involving ion beam shaping (IBS) and inductively coupled plasma (ICP) on LWR of PR layers. Experimental results demonstrate that adopting the sequence of IBS-ICP significantly optimizes LWR, with an improvement exceeding 25%. Power spectral density (PSD) analysis in the frequency further indicates that this combined process effectively optimizes both high and low frequency roughness, leading to comprehensive enhancement of edge quality across spatial frequencies. This work provides valuable experimental insights into the optimization of post-lithography processing sequences and has practical significance for high-resolution micro/nanofabrication.

title: Engineering Ethics in the Age of Climate Crisis: Evaluating Big Oil’s Responsibility
authors: Sydney Bakir, Zachary Mitchell, Simone Nelson, Nia Wiggins
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: For over a century now, the reliance on fossil fuels has been a leading cause of global greenhouse gas emissions and subsequently, climate change along with extreme weather events. Research suggested major fossil fuel production companies had knowledge of the environmental risks. Extreme weather events like hurricanes and floods are only increasing, leaving communities devastated while fossil fuel companies profit. Our work highlights why accountability is essential for the oil industry so that less of these weather events will occur. As we continue to face the often extreme consequences of climate change, the societal costs of fossil fuel usage has become a focal point of legal and ethical debate. Many states have filed lawsuits against these companies alleging that they misled the public about the dangers of their products. This work focuses on examining climate accountability lawsuits against major oil companies through an ethical lens, evaluating whether claims of deception and harm are ethically justified and whether states have a legitimate basis for holding these corporations accountable. The methodology of this research involves a comparison of competing approaches to the utilitarian ethical framework. By analyzing the legal position of the Big Oil Companies against the plaintiff states and environmentalists, this study explores the extent to which each claim results in a net benefit to society. The National Society of Professional Engineers Code of Ethics was used to evaluate the decision making by major fossil fuel companies on the climate issue. We compared this case to the tactics and legal challenges of the tobacco industry over the danger of its products to society. We contrasted the oil industry’s emphasis on energy security against the states' focus on safety revealing the ethical failings of Big Oil from utilitarian and virtue ethics perspectives. From the ethical perspective, climate accountability lawsuits challenge the fundamentals of these entities’ corporate moral responsibility and obligation.

title: Behind the Prompt: Privacy Risks in Large Language Models
authors: Bedri Chaparov, Abid Khan, Munam Ali Shah, Ovidiu Bagdasar, Muhammad Moazam Azeem
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Large language models (LLMS) are progressively adopted since their inception yielding tremendous impact in domains such as finance, healthcare, and cybersecurity. However, they also introduce new vulnerabilities as well among them privacy of the underlying sensitive data is very alarming. This paper explores the problem of data privacy by performing theoretical threat modeling as well as providing an experimental validation. We develop a custom LangChain-based local LLM agent to simulate a controlled environment for testing responses to sensitive, adversarial, and benign prompts. Our analysis considers passive threats, such as data memorization, and active adversarial attacks, including prompt injection and training data extraction. A variety of privacy-preserving methodologies are evaluated in comparison to proprietary models such as OpenAI’s GPT-4o. Finally, we propose a conceptual framework for mitigating privacy risks in practical LLM deployments.

title: A Hybrid CNN–Vision Transformer Architecture for Enhanced Document Understanding in Higher Education Archiving
authors: Rahma Mohammed Al Kharusi, Abdul Rahman Mohammed Al Abri, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Mohammed Abdulla Al Husaini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The growing volume and diversity of digital documents in higher education institutions demand robust, automated approaches for reliable classification and archiving. Conventional rule-based systems perform poorly on low-resolution scans, non-standard layouts, and heterogeneous document types prevalent in institutional archives. This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViT) in a late-fusion design for document image classification. The CNN branch (ResNet-50) captures local texture and glyph-level features, while the ViT branch (ViT-Base/16) models global layout and long-range structural dependencies. The combined embeddings are mapped to document-type labels via a lightweight classification head. Experiments are conducted on a stratified subset of the RVL-CDIP benchmark (38,396 training / 4,800 validation / 4,800 test images across 16 document categories). The proposed HybridCNNViT model achieves a top-1 test accuracy of 84.25% and a macro-average F1-score of 0.8428, outperforming single-encoder baselines (ResNet-50 only: 82.30%; ViT-Base/16 only: 81.60%) under identical training conditions. Ablation results confirm the complementary contribution of both encoders to the late-fusion representation. These findings demonstrate that hybrid CNN-ViT architectures are a promising foundation for automating large-scale document archiving workflows in higher education environments.

title: BMS Metrology Consistency and In-Service Mutual Recognition: Type Evaluation and ATE Test Coverage Methodology Based on Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei Coordination
authors: Yan Yang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a metrology-based technical framework for the integration and supervising of Battery Management Systems (BMS) in the shared/commercial energy storage sector within the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei region. The framework aims to establish a traceable and auditable type evaluation and Automated Test Equipment (ATE) assessment system, grounded in IEEE standards. It defines performance metrics, uncertainty budgets, and validation methods to support mutual recognition among regional laboratories, facilitating pilot deployment in the region.

title: Confidence-Aware Adaptive Neural Computation for Energy-Efficient Deep Learning
authors: Dharmateja Reddy Kethireddy, Nanda Kishore Kande, Suhas Reddy Yarrrabothu, Ashish Hastimal Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Deep neural networks provide good predictive performance but have fixed-depth inference pipelines which introduce unnecessary computational burden to easy samples and shall limit energy-efficient deployment in resource-constrained intelligent systems. This paper introduces a confidence-aware adaptive neural computation framework for energy-efficient image classification. where the multi-exit deep architecture includes dynamically changing inference depth which depends on the difficulty of the input without executing the entire model for all instances. Using Tiny ImageNet as a challenging benchmark, the proposed approach relies on the combination of early-exit classifiers and threshold-based adaptive routing to ensure a balance between the predictive performance and computational efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate an exit-wise improvement and the test accuracy of Exit 1 is 17.82%, the test accuracy of Exit 2 is 30.13% and the test accuracy of the final exit is 61.49%. The best value of the threshold setting [0.5, 0.6] in adaptive inference achieves an accuracy of 61.33% with an average exit depth of 2.70, compared with an accuracy of 60.18% of inference full with depth of second exit, 3.00, while the routing is 8% and 14% of samples through first and second exits, respectively. These results show the novelty and practical importance of confidence-guided adaptive inference as a promising direction for sustainable, computation-aware deep learning.

title: Data-Driven Assessment of Health Status for Semiconductor Equipment
authors: Kai Zhang, Huijie Mao
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Equipment reliability is critical in semiconductor manufacturing; however, traditional preventive maintenance, which is reliant on experience rather than quantitative analysis, often leads to either over- or under-maintenance. To address this issue, we propose a data-driven health assessment method for semiconductor equipment. By leveraging multi-source sensor data, the approach maps the feature space to chamber health status, thereby enabling quantitative performance monitoring. The workflow includes sensor signal cleaning, feature engineering, and nonlinear dimensionality reduction, followed by the construction of a health indicator in the compressed feature space for rapid evaluation of equipment health. Validation using real equipment data demonstrates that the method accurately reflects equipment condition and supports applications such as chamber matching analysis, predictive maintenance, and maintenance effectiveness evaluation.

title: DCMT-SegNet: Mitigating Confirmation Bias in Semi-Supervised Rib Segmentation via Dual Competing Students
authors: Poornanand Purushottam Naik, Mahendra Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Precise rib segmentation on chest radiographs is indispensable for detecting thoracic diseases and evaluating trauma; however, manual annotation processes are still quite time-consuming and expensive. This paper presents DCMT-SegNet, a dual competing mean teacher framework for semi-supervised rib segmentation that adeptly exploits both labeled and unlabeled data. The architecture is composed of two competing student networks, supervised by mean teacher models, with exponential moving averages, advanced data augmentation, multi-component loss functions, and consistency regularisation. The proposed method achieves outstanding results on the public VinDr-RibCXR dataset under conditions of 5-100% labeled data: only 5% supervision yields a Dice coefficient of 0.8253, HD95 of 7.9651mm, and ASD of 1.570mm. When 75% of the data is labeled, the DSC goes up to 0.8811, and 0.8850 at 100%. The dual-competitive strategy delivers far better results than single mean teacher baselines, thus, it is more accurate in terms of delineating boundaries and is more robust.

title: A Novel End-to-End Single Image Dehazing Using Pix2Pix GAN
authors: Vinaya Varshini Ravichandran, Priyan Malarvizhi Kumar, Abhignya Jagathpally, Jeeva Selvaraj
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Haze is a significant deterioration of the image quality, which undermines visibility and limits the interpretation of the scene in the computer-vision applications. In the current research article, an advanced single-image dehazing model is suggested that is founded on the Pix2Pix conditional Generative Adversarial Network (cGAN). Its generator is a U-Net architecture that has an encoder-decoder structure, and skip connections to retain small details, and a PatchGAN discriminator that enhances local realism. The training process of dehazed images using adversarial training allows approximating the clarity of the expected images. The effectiveness of the framework is supported by empirical assessment, which includes generator loss, discriminator loss, and L1 loss. Across more than 50 epochs, L1 loss decreases from 21.13 to 6.43, demonstrating a marked improvement in visual fidelity. The method is superior to conventional dehazing methods in the restoration of structural detail and texture and can be used in practical settings by virtue of the latter.

title: The Profile Control of High Aspect Ratio Amorphous Carbon through Polymer Gas Distribution Optimization
authors: Hongqiu Wang, Hui Liu, Wenlong Jin, Morlan Chen, Xiaoqi Zheng, Hu Zhou, Yuping Ren, Rich Chen
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: The influence of polymer gas flow distribution on the profile control of the amorphous carbon layer (ACL) was investigated, and its mechanisms were explored. Polymer gas flow and its distribution exhibited a significant effect on the passivation of ACL sidewall and surface properties of ACL mask, regulating the neck critical dimension (CD) and restricting the specific plasma diffuse into the ACL holes. During the plasma etching of ACL, an optimized polymer gas flow distribution maintained a proper necking CD with excellent uniformity and modified specific angle of ion sputtering. Consequently, the ACL was regulated to achieve a desired profile.

title: A Domain-Specific Neural Network for High-Accuracy Mm Wave-Only Human Activity Recognition
authors: Noel D'Avis, Silvia Faquiri
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) with mmWave radar offers a privacy-preserving and robust alternative to visionbased sensing, but remains challenging due to noisy signals, intersubject variability, and the difficulty of distinguishing symmetric activities. This capability is particularly relevant for real-world applications such as elderly care, home-assisted living, and continuous monitoring of vulnerable individuals, where unobtrusive, privacy-aware sensing is essential. In this paper, we present a feature engineering pipeline that extends a 21-dimensional baseline with left-right asymmetry descriptors and temporal dynamics, resulting in a 41 -dimensional representation that improves recognition of laterally symmetric actions by 5-6%. We design a compact one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) tailored to these sequential features, combining two temporal convolutional stages with global average pooling and a lightweight dense head. Evaluated on the MM-Fi dataset using subject-wise splits and 40 randomized runs, our approach achieves a mean test accuracy of 92.04 % and macro-F1 score of 0.920, outperforming previous mmWave-only baselines (Table I). These results indicate that mmWave sensing, combined with radar-specific feature design and efficient deep models, can support reliable and reproducible HAR systems suitable for deployment in safety-critical real-world environments.

title: Construction of Learning Anomaly Detection and Intervention Model Based on Isolation Forest-LSTM in Massive Online Education
authors: Weiyu Cao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Big Data and Informatization Education
abstract: None

title: AI Enhanced Code Fault Detection and Automated Fixing
authors: V Arun Antony, T.A.Karthikeyen, N.Kannan, S.Gowtham, V. Jai Akash, R.Kamalica
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Software development can be characterized by complex programs, which can carry with them, syntactic errors and logical bugs, therefore debugging becomes a very time consuming and a demanding undertaking. Conventional debugging systems are intensive in their utilisation of manual effort and rule-based tools which have some limitations in identifying run-time problems and supplying automatic solutions. We present a new AI-based automated debugging system in this paper that combines dynamic validation and pre-trained large language models with a static code analysis to detect bugs and code fixes efficiently. The system makes use of parsing based on Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) to retrieve the functional level components, create test cases, and also detect possible errors as well as logical inconsistency. Pretrained models include CodeLlama, Mistral 7B, CodeT5, and StarCoder which are used to produce context-aware fixes, and a memory module based on vectors is used to increase efficiency by retrieving similar patterns of past bug-fixes. The system is based on an iterative validation procedure to provide accuracy on generated solutions. The experimental analysis based on similarity-based measurements proves that CodeLlama has the best mark of 91.2, which reflects the competence of the offered method in automated program repair and code optimization assignments.

title: An Interactive Visualization of a Single-LLM Multi-Agent Educational System
authors: Alberto Matilla-Molina, Juan M. Dodero, Andrés Muñoz
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: This demo paper presents an interactive HTMLbased visualization and an accompanying video that make the internal workflow of a single-LLM multi-agent educational system explicit and inspectable. The system instantiates three rolespecialized pedagogical agents (Teacher, Adapter, and Evaluator) on top of a single open-source large language model (LLM), coordinated by an explicit orchestrator component and equipped with four alternative memory schemes (C0-C3), ranging from no explicit memory to lightweight retrieval-based memory. The contribution of this paper is a visual and narrative artifact that exposes how agents, memory, and the orchestrator interact during a typical educational episode. The demo is intended both as a research tool to reason about design choices in LLM-based multi-agent systems and as a pedagogical resource for courses on intelligent learning environments, agentic AI, and educational technology.

title: Cost-Efficient Partial-Port Wavelength-Convertible Multi-Band Optical Cross-Connects: Architecture and Upgrade Strategies
authors: Feifei Jin, Ningning Guo, Yongcheng Li, Gangxiang Shen
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Multi-band transmission is a promising approach for network capacity expansion by exploiting the underutilized low-loss spectrum of single-mode fibers. As a key component of multi-band optical networks, the architecture of multi-band optical cross-connects (MB-OXCs) critically affects network performance; however, existing designs are constrained by scalability, blocking performance, and deployment cost. To address these challenges, we propose a partial-port wavelength convertible (PPWC) MB-OXC architecture that integrates ports with and without wavelength conversion capability. While wavelength conversion enhances wavelength utilization, it also degrades the generalized signal-to-noise ratio (GSNR) of lightpaths, making it essential to balance this tradeoff by identifying an optimal ratio between convertible and non-convertible ports. Simulation results demonstrate that, with an optimally configured conversion ratio, the PPWC architecture achieves low blocking probability while effectively controlling network cost. Furthermore, we investigate the upgrade of conventional nodes to PPWC nodes and propose four node upgrade strategies to balance performance improvement and deployment cost. Among them, the Maximum-Benefit-First Strategy (MBFS) achieves the largest reduction in blocking probability with minimal cost increase and outperforms an all PPWC deployment in terms of overall cost efficiency. Together, the proposed PPWC architecture and the MBFS upgrade strategy provide an effective and cost-efficient solution for multi band optical networks.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Smart Insole for Early Detection and Risk Prediction of Peripheral Artery Disease and Foot Ulcers in Diabetic Patients
authors: A. Lakshmi, K. Nashroon Fazila, S. Sanchavi Priya, R. Sowmiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Diabetic foot complications like Peripheral Artery Disease (PAD) and Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFU) can occur slowly due to poor perfusion and abnormal plantar pressure. A sensor–based smart insole for continuous, non-invasive diabetic foot risk assessment is presented in this paper. To identify structural alterations linked to foot ulcers, the insole incorporates a temperature sensor, Light Dependent Resistor (LDR)-based plantar pressure sensors, and a bone strength sensing module. Vascular parameters like blood flow and oxygen saturation are monitored using a non-contact liquid level sensor for blood flow measurement and a MAX30102 sensor for SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and heart rate monitoring in order to detect ischemic conditions associated with peripheral artery disease (PAD). An anomaly detection algorithm based on Isolation Forest is used to analyze sensor data obtained with a Raspberry Pi. This system enables early detection using a low-cost wearable monitoring system by classifying conditions into normal, PAD risk, foot ulcer risk, or combined risk.

title: Measurement-Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution With Hybrid Integrated Self-Injection Locking Lasers
authors: Xiao-Lei Jiang, Yu-Yao Guo, Yan-Yang Zhou, Yu Zhou, Xin-Hang Li, Yi-Fei Lu, Yang Wang, Jia-Ji Li, Chun Zhou, Hong-Wei Li, Lin-Jie Zhou, Wan-Su Bao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Breakthroughs in chip integration technology have accelerated the practical deployment and scalable implementation of quantum key distribution (QKD). On-chip quantum light sources are critical components of fully integrated QKD systems. Measurement-Device-Independent QKD (MDI-QKD) offers significant advantages in network communication by eliminating vulnerabilities to side-channel attacks at the detector side, while relying on high-visibility Hong-Ou-Mandel interference between photons emitted by two independent lasers. Here, we present an innovative design of the silicon-based hybrid integrated self-injection locking (SIL) laser chip that achieves high-frequency-stability operation with the frequency detuning between independent lasers suppressed to within 15 MHz, enabling high-visibility HOM interference in MDI-QKD. Leveraging this design, we experimentally demonstrate a loss-tolerant MDI-QKD system employing the setting-choice-independent correlation security framework explicitly accounting for state-preparation uncertainty. The system achieves a finite-key secret rate of 17 b/s under 34 dB channel loss (or 170-km standard fiber), surpassing previously reported MDI-QKD experiments that incorporated state preparation uncertainty under equivalent transmission loss conditions. Our result makes a promising contribution to practical applications of fully integrated quantum communication networks.

title: TeethID: Teeth Gesture-Independent Earable Authentication Robust to Environmental Noise
authors: Huijie Chen, Yongxu Xia, Xiaobin Xu, Shuopeng Li, Haoru Su, Youqi Li, Juan Fang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Earbuds are increasingly used as human-centered computing platforms that store sensitive user data, creating a need for secure and unobtrusive authentication. Teeth-gesture-based authentication has emerged as a promising solution for implicit user verification, but existing approaches are often susceptible to environmental noise and variability in gesture execution, which can degrade authentication performance. This paper proposes a teeth-gesture-independent earbud authentication system (TeethID) that is robust to environmental noise. TeethID leverages the microphones embedded in commodity earbuds to capture teeth-gesture sounds from out-ear and in-ear microphones and constructs a user-specific out-ear-to-in-ear audio mapping function using predefined excitation signals, effectively suppressing environmental interference. In addition, TeethID incorporates an adversarial learning-based model to mitigate variations across different teeth gestures and inconsistencies across repeated occlusion instances. Extensive experiments involving 20 participants under diverse environmental noise levels demonstrate that TeethID achieves a balanced accuracy (BAC) of 97.11%, with a false acceptance rate (FAR) of 1.12% and a false rejection rate (FRR) of 4.66%. Security evaluations further show that TeethID is resilient to both replay and mimic attacks.

title: Interactive Self-Supervised Pansharpening Network Guided by Joint Satellite Data and Intermediate Loss
authors: Qing Guo, Qingze Zhou, Yu Tian, Letian Yu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pansharpening integrates high-resolution panchro matic (PAN) images with low-resolution multispectral (MS) data to produce high-spatial resolution multispectral images (HRMS). Mainstream deep learning-based pansharpening methods often rely on single satellite dataset training and lack effective control over the inference process of fusion networks. To address these limitations, we propose an interactive self-supervised pansharp ening network guided by joint satellite data and intermediate loss (ISPN). ISPN incorporates three inputs- the original PAN, the original MS, and the prior image constructed by inter pixel correlations (IPC). IPC leverages inter-pixel correlations to extract local relationships among PAN-MS images to guide the detail injection during fusion. Furthermore, the designed in termediate loss function further constrains the extracted features to ensure stable training. Unlike conventional methods that rely on single satellite data, our approach first employs a joint dataset, significantly enhancing generalization and adaptability. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method out performs some state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of key architectural components and design choices in the proposed architecture. The code is available at the following link https://github.com/qguo-cas/ISPN.

title: A Solidly Mounted Resonator Based on c-Axis-Tilted Scandium-Doped Aluminum Nitride Films for Cardiac Troponin I Detection
authors: Chengzhang Han, Jinli Li, Zhenyu Wang, He Lv, Yusuf Suleiman Dambatta, Jingjie Dai
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain a leading cause of death worldwide, with acute myocardial infarction (AMI) accounting for a significant proportion of fatalities. The rapid and accurate detection of cardiac troponin I (cTnI) is critical for early diagnosis of AMI. We report an innovative, highly sensitive, solidly mounted resonator (SMR) biosensor based on a c-axis-tilted scandium-doped aluminum nitride (Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.1</sub>Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.9</sub>N) film for label-free cTnI detection. The SMR device, fabricated by magnetron sputtering and incorporating an acoustic Bragg reflector, is designed to operate in the shear acoustic mode. The sensor demonstrates a quality factor of 2030 and an effective electromechanical coupling coefficient of 2.6%. Functionalized with a self-assembled monolayer and monoclonal antibodies, the sensor achieves a detection limit of 3 pg/mL for cTnI in serum, with a rapid response time of 100 s and high selectivity against common interferents. The device also exhibits significant potential for integration with CMOS electronics, supporting the development of miniaturized, point-of-care (POC) diagnostic systems for cardiovascular diseases.

title: NeuroKinect: A Deep Learning Framework for Hand Kinematics Reconstruction from EEG Signals
authors: Amita Giri, Sidharth Pancholi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) technology facilitates direct brain-to-device communication, empowering individuals to control their surroundings through brain signals. However, prevalent BCI methods encounter challenges: a) time-intensive preprocessing, b) improper loss function use, and c) non-intuitive hyperparameter settings. To overcome these challenges, we propose NeuroKinect, a novel deep learning framework designed to reconstruct hand kinematics from electroencephalography (EEG) signals. Trained on Grasp and Lift (GAL) task data with streamlined preprocessing, NeuroKinect enhances computational efficiency. NeuroKinect introduces a task-specific loss function, LStat, which balances correlation and error metrics to better align predicted and actual motion. Driven by scientific intuition, we analyze motor task dynamics via event-related potentials and brain source localization to optimize hyperparameters. Extensive evaluation on EEG data collected during grasp-and-lift tasks demonstrates NeuroKinect’s ability to accurately decode 3D hand movement trajectories. The model achieves strong correlation between predicted and actual motion across all spatial dimensions (mean Pearson coefficients: 0.92±0.015 for X, 0.93±0.019 for Y, and 0.83±0.018 for Z dimensions), supported by low mean squared error values (MSE: 0.016±0.001 for X, 0.015±0.002 for Y, and 0.017±0.005 for Z). These results highlight NeuroKinect’s potential for enhancing real-time BCI systems for motor control and rehabilitation.

title: Satellite Anomaly Prediction using Time Series Analysis of Telemetry Data
authors: Puli Shravan Kumar, T K Ramesh, J K Kishore, Aravinda Koithyar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Satellites operate in a remote and inaccessible environment, making in-orbit maintenance and repair extremely challenging. Continuous telemetry is therefore monitored to assess spacecraft health and support timely decision-making. With the availability of large volumes of historical telemetry data, data-driven approaches have become essential for automated health monitoring and early anomaly warning. This paper presents a forecasting-based early anomaly detection framework for satellite telemetry. The proposed approach first predicts future telemetry values and then detects anomalies based on violations of statistically derived upper and lower control limits. Classical time-series models, namely Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) and Seasonal Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA), are evaluated alongside deep learning models, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks. A case study using offline telemetry from the Dynamically Tuned Gyro (DTG) subsystem of a decommissioned satellite is presented. The models are trained on historical data to forecast upcoming telemetry values, and the predicted values are used to provide early warning of anomalies. The results demonstrate that the proposed forecasting-based detection approach can successfully provide advance indication of anomaly occurrence, with predicted anomaly timing closely matching the actual event.The case study focuses on a known anomaly observed . The proposed model successfully generated an anomaly alert, demonstrating a one-day early warning capability. The presented framework can be extended to real-time monitoring of critical telemetry parameters for in-service satellites, enabling proactive health management and improved operational reliability.
