title: Beyond Human Coaches: Evaluating LLM-Powered Leadership Coaching for Underrepresented Groups
authors: Amna Asif, Luise Frohberg, Jörg Frohberg
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Accessing leadership coaches can be challenging due to availability constraints or high costs, limiting opportunities for diverse professionals, especially underrepresented groups seeking leadership development. Artificial Intelligence (AI) coaches present a viable alternative. This study investigates participant engagement and emotional responses in leadership training scenarios, comparing interactions with AI and human roleplay partners. Analyzing 78 roleplay sessions, we found that AI-human roleplay partners evoked significantly higher positive emotions of determination, confidence, and appreciation, while negative emotions like frustration, concern, and disappointment were common in human-human roleplay sessions. This study explored the feasibility of AI-human roleplay partners, which showed high engagement and provided a psychologically safer environment for participants. These results demonstrate the potential of AI-human roleplay partners to support effective leadership coaching without compromising emotional well-being.

title: Design Technology Co-Optimization of a Hybrid Dual-κ Spacer Strategy for Sub-3-nm Nanosheet Gaafets and Circuits Performance Enhancement
authors: Meihe Zhang, Lei Cao, Qingkun Li, Renjie Jiang, Kun Yang, Hang Zhang, Qingzhu Zhang, Huaxiang Yin, Jun Luo
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Spacer engineering is crucial for advanced GAA devices. To overcome the limitations of conventional single-κ spacers, a hybrid dual-κ spacer strategy in nanosheet (NS) GAAFETs at 3 nm node is proposed. It employs higher-κ inner spacers for better drive current and low-κ outer spacers to reduce parasitic capacitance. This practical design is validated through device simulation and circuit-level analysis. The results demonstrate the best design with over 25% decrease in capacitance than single SiN spacer, 17.01% (PMOS)/10.00% (NMOS) increase in on-state current and 108% frequency improvement in ROs over mainstream approaches, providing a valuable guide for the industrial fabrication of GAA transistors at advanced nodes.

title: A Robust Cascaded Posture Classification Network Under Different Sleep Conditions
authors: Selwan Abdussalam, Abdallah Naser, Salisu Yahaya, Ahmad Lotfi
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: Accurately monitoring sleep in an intelligent environment, such as a smart home, using a non-invasive system, is challenging. The challenge here is the real-world sleep component, particularly the blanket. The blanket plays a crucial role in sleep posture monitoring, influencing the thermal signature and, consequently, the classification accuracy. This paper presents a novel computational solution, the Cascaded Posture Classification Network (CPCN), designed to address the critical issue of thermal attenuation resulting from bedding conditions. The CPCN uses a dual-stage intelligent system that processes thermal images captured by an unobtrusive thermal sensor array. The system first employs a dedicated Blanket Classifier Convolutional Neural Network. It first analyses the bedding condition (No Blanket, Medium Blanket, Thick Blanket). The classification and routing at this stage achieved an accuracy of 94.44 %. The image is then routed to one of three specialised CNNs based on the blanket condition that developed in response to the thermal challenge. The four sleep postures (Prone, Supine, Left, Right) for the blanket conditions (No Blanket, Medium Blanket, Thick Blanket) achieved accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.25 \%, 84 \%$</tex>, and 77 %, respectively. The system achieved an accuracy of 81.64 % across both stages. This novel cascaded design establishes a necessary methodological phase, moving thermal monitoring from laboratory settings to reliable, robust, and applicable real-home environments.

title: AI-Driven Self-Supervised Transformer Framework for Explainable and Early Insider Threat Detection
authors: Venkateswaran Radhakrishnan, Dheeraj P, S Heshika Varthini, Kirubavathi G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Insider threats pose major cybersecurity risks due to privileged access misuse and the scarcity of labeled malicious data for supervised detection methods. This paper proposes a self-supervised Transformer framework for early and explainable insider threat detection using CERT enterprise log data without requiring labels. User-level behavioral sequences from heterogeneous logs are used to train a Transformer encoder with masked event modeling, temporal gap prediction, and contrastive behavioral learning objectives. A composite anomaly score enables unsupervised detection of abnormal behaviors. Adaptive thresholds and sliding window drift detection provide proactive early warnings, while SHAP-based explanations improve interpretability. The framework was evaluated on over five million CERT log events from 3,997 users and successfully identified anomalous behavioral sequences and high-risk users, demonstrating its practical effectiveness for label-free insider threat detection.

title: Reconfigurable Neuronal-Synaptic Transistors for Speech Processing
authors: Zuqi Zhu, Hailiang Wang, Yuxin Li, Chuan Qin, Xinwei Zhang, Yang Xu, Bin Yu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have attracted considerable attention owing to their high energy efficiency and intrinsic parallelism. However, the functional separation of neurons and synapses imposes significant challenges for large-scale hardware realization. Here, we experimentally demonstrate a floating-gate transistor-based neuronal-synaptic transistor (NSFG) that exhibits typical synaptic characteristics and converts harmonic inputs into highly biomimetic neuronal spikes. The SNN with NSFG devices achieves higher accuracy with reduced training time in speech classification, underscoring its potential for efficient neuromorphic computing.

title: Digital University Transformation: Readiness Analysis of the Mongolian University of Science and Technology
authors: Narantsetseg Yadmaa, Bayar-Erdene Lkhagvasuren, Serchmaa Tserenbaljir
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This study examines the concept of a digital university. It analyzes the readiness of the Mongolian University of Science and Technology (MUST) to transition from a traditional to a digital university model. Digital universities play a vital role in the sustainable development of nations by expanding access to high-quality education and fostering innovation. Mongolia, one of the world's largest countries by land area but sparsely populated, stands to benefit significantly from digital education initiatives. MUST aims to become a national leader in digital higher education. This paper evaluates the university's digital transformation readiness using five key dimensions, assessed through qualitative methods. The findings indicate that the strongest area is technological infrastructure, which serves as a major enabler for transformation. However, the least developed dimension is the institutional culture, skills, and human capacity required for a digital university. To successfully advance, MUST should focus on building a digital culture, enhancing digital competencies among faculty and students, and investing in human resources with digital expertise.

title: AI Literacy Among University Personnel: A Cross-Sectional Assessment of Teaching and Non-Teaching Staff at a Philippine State University
authors: Ma. Julieta B. Borres
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into higher education policy and practice presupposes a workforce that understands its foundational principles; however, empirical data on AI literacy among university personnel remain scarce particularly in the Global South. This study assessed the AI literacy of selected teaching (n = 62) and non-teaching personnel (n = 14) at Bicol University, Philippines, using a validated 31-item instrument (α = 0.855) spanning five conceptual domains: AI fundamentals, machine learning processes, data literacy, societal implications, and ethics. Descriptive statistics, one-way ANOVA, and item difficulty analyses were employed for data analysis. Results revealed a uniformly moderate literacy levels across both groups (teaching: M = 12.66, SD = 5.25; non-teaching: M = 13.79, SD = 4.95), with no statistically significant difference between them (p = 0.693). Item-level analysis uncovered a consistent pattern: personnel demonstrated stronger grasp of conceptual and ethical dimensions of AI (items ≥ 0.50 difficulty index) but struggled with procedural and technical content, particularly machine learning workflows and model evaluation (items < 0.40). These findings challenge assumptions about faculty technological advantage and point to a shared literacy gap that cuts across occupational roles. Implications for differentiated professional development and institutional AI readiness are discussed.

title: Cyberattack Identification in Smart EV Charging Systems Using Remaining Useful Life-Guided Autoencoder - LSTM Networks
authors: Rathika S, Charles Raja S, Nelson Jayakumar D, Ashok Kumar L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) Charging infrastructure has increased and is incorporating smart grid technology. EV charging networks are being adopted quickly and easily through smart grid technologies. The real-time integration of smart grid technology enables the rapid development of infrastructures for electric vehicle (EV) charging. These systems are often developed concurrently because of the need to have cybersecurity. EV charging will be exposed to cyberattacks, and this may cause a major disruption in their operations. Battery charging stations are unsafe to the users. The proposed study has taken a radical approach of integrating an Autoencoder and LSTM hybrid system that will improve the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) to increase cybersecurity and threat resilience of electric vehicle charging infrastructure. The deep learning architecture employs the autoencoders based on the EV infrastructure to detect cyber intrusion and study the complex intrusion patterns to Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The predictions generated provide RUL estimators, which are assembled on testing data based on Mendeley and Kaggle datasets. The performance of the frameworks is evaluated with the help of the described datasets mentioned above and compared to the traditional approaches like Isolation Forest, One Class SVM, and GAN-driven algorithms. The hybrid RUL-guided Autoencoder-LSTM has proven to be better in terms of detection accuracy and lowering false positives and resilience than other methodologies found in literature. Furthermore, the framework complies with industry cybersecurity requirements such as IEC 62443, ISO/SAE 21434, and ISO 15118, ensuring that intelligent EV charging infrastructure can be quickly implemented.

title: Real Time Bus Tracking and Smart Seat Availability System with Route Visualization and Estimated Time of Arrival Prediction
authors: Rajan S, Kumaraguru M, Kavya K, Mohamed Haris Khan G, Sabareesh S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: The importance of ETA is to improve the public transport by minimizing the waiting time and measuring the traveling time. This is implemented through the development of Real-Time Bus Tracking and Smart Seat Availability System through Route Visualization and ETA. The proposed hardware includes Arduino, Node MCU, GPS module, and force sensors which automatically measure the location of the bus and seats availability. Data will be collected by the backend programmed in Python and the collected data can be used for generating an interactive website via HTML, CSS, and React.js. The user can know about the movement of the bus, the ETA, and seats availability accurately. The previously developed applications based on the use of QR/RFID system, mobile application, or even data entry manually will be expensive, require an internet connection to function and less reliable. Due to our teams' aim for developing automated, accurate, and cost-effective design, we have designed the system in such a way as to detect real-time seat availability and GPS location of the buses.

title: Deep Learning-Based Access Point Placement Optimization for Indoor Wireless Network Planning
authors: Erdenetuya Erdenebileg, Uuganbayar Purevdorj, Purevtseren Bayarsaikhan, Erdenebayar Lamjav
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: Efficient wireless network planning in indoor environments remains a critical challenge due to complex spatial layouts, material-induced attenuation, and dynamic coverage requirements. This paper proposes a novel AIassisted framework that combines deep learning with automated optimization to predict wireless signal propagation and strategically place access points (APs) with minimal blind zones. A convolutional neural network based on SDU-Net architecture is trained using synthetic floorplan datasets that incorporate material properties and randomized AP configurations. The model predicts coverage heatmaps, which are then used to evaluate and select optimal multi-AP placements through blind spot minimization. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves coverage uniformity and reduces planning time compared to traditional manual and rule-based strategies. This approach provides a scalable, data-driven solution for indoor wireless deployment scenarios, with potential applications in smart buildings, industrial networks, and next-generation communication systems.

title: Detection of Malicious Websites Based on Hybrid Machine Learning (HML)
authors: Maitha Alarjani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Nowadays, the internet plays a vital role in daily life, with people increasingly relying on online services such as shopping, banking, bill payments, and reservations. As internet usage continues to grow, users face a rising number of online threats. One of the most common attack vectors employed by cybercriminals is malicious Uniform Resource Locators (URLs), which are used in phishing attacks to steal sensitive information or to distribute malware that compromises users’ devices. Due to the severe consequences of malicious websites—including financial loss, data breaches, and reputational damage—it is essential to develop effective techniques for detecting such threats. In this paper, we propose an intelligent hybrid machine-learning model capable of identifying suspicious characteristics commonly associated with malicious websites. The proposed approach aims to enhance the detection and prevention of malicious URLs. To address class imbalance in the dataset, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is applied. The dataset used in this study was obtained from Kaggle. The proposed hybrid model combines Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and AdaBoost classifiers using a hard voting strategy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several recent studies in malicious website detection.

title: Generative AI for Cold-Start E-Commerce Product Review Generation
authors: Siddhanta Rawal, Mahreen Nasir
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Cold start remains a persistent challenge in collaborative filtering based recommender systems, where products with limited user interactions often remain effectively invisible. This paper presents a novel approach for synthesizing product reviews using Large Language Models to mitigate data sparsity for new and low-visibility items. Using the Amazon Reviews 2023 dataset across the Beauty and Appliances categories, we demonstrate that generated reviews can bootstrap early helpfulness predictions for cold-start products. Experimental results show that synthetic reviews substantially improve product visibility, with mean ranking gains increasing from 47,919 to 59,554 positions and top-500 inclusion rates rising from 0% to 6.1%. We further observe that review density plays a critical role; for example, generating 10 reviews per product yields up to a threefold improvement compared to using only 3 reviews. Importantly, the enhanced model maintains recommendation quality for warm items while exposing 85% of previously unseen products within the recommendation space. These findings indicate that synthetic review generation provides an effective and scalable solution to the cold-start problem in e-commerce recommendation systems.

title: Design and Fabrication of a Biomass Briquette Machine for Recycling of Agricultural Waste
authors: Abdullah Alarfaj, Hamzah Almarabia, Jihad Alkhalifah, Abdulmohsen Albeladi, Abdulsalam Alharbi, Kamran Shah, Mohammed Ismail, Hussain Altammar
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and fabrication of a biomass briquette machine aimed at converting organic waste into usable fuel. With growing environmental concerns and the need for sustainable energy alternatives, biomass offers a renewable and efficient solution. The project explores various types of biomass and their applications, particularly in producing briquettes for heating, industrial use, and energy generation. The machine is built using accessible materials and components, including a carbon steel frame, an auger, bearings, an electric motor, and interchangeable dies. Through research, design, and hands-on construction, the performance of the proposed design was evaluated and tested in transforming agricultural and organic waste into dense fuel briquettes. This proposed design, showcases environmental and energy solutions that contribute to sustainable waste management.

title: A System for Movement-Based Behavior Modeling and Anomaly Detection in Security Contexts
authors: Lorena Quesada Ilmert, Ana M. Bernardos, José R. Casar
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: Location data has become central to many services, requiring effective modeling of movement patterns and detection of irregular behaviors. This paper presents a system for behavior modeling and anomaly detection based on georeferenced movement data, evaluated in security scenarios. Points of interest are identified using an adapted Time- and Distance-Based Clustering (TDBC) algorithm, and behavior patterns are extracted to build a historical model. Deviations from this model are then detected through statistical inference, with anomaly types and degrees defined by a dedicated taxonomy. Evaluation in a synthetic drug investigation scenario shows that the proposed clustering method outperforms classical density-based approaches for moderate and high-frequency data, while anomaly detection accuracy improves with larger historical datasets and clearly defined anomalies, yet remains effective in less obvious cases.

title: Three-Phase Solar PV Integrated UPQC System
authors: Lokesh T R, Marutesh. M. Gowda, Jyothi. R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Advanced systems leads to a combine clean energy produces an power quality development which is needed due to increased need for sustainable energy and precise power quality requirements in the contemporary electronic gadgets. A three-phase multipurpose solar energy system is built to solve power quality difficulties in the load side. The proposed system, known as PV-UPQC. There are two types of voltage compensators shunt and series connected back to back over a shared DC-link. Shunt compensator has two functions: it draws electricity from the solar panel and helps eliminate harmonic distortions in the load current. This technique delivers sustainable power generation and greater power quality. It has been verified using a MATLAB-Simulink simulation with a nonlinear load. This study provides an essential information for resolving power quality concerns in solar systems and improving their grid connection reliability and stability.

title: Proactive Seat Belt Retractor Locking System Based on Vehicle Speed Threshold for Enhanced Pre Crash Occupant Safety
authors: Paavai Arumugam, Manoharan Marimuthu
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Traditional seat belt retractor systems are reactive, engaging only after the onset of significant vehicle deceleration during a crash event. This delayed activation permits initial forward occupant excursion, which can adversely influence occupant kinematics and interaction with supplemental restraint systems. This paper presents a proactive seat belt retractor locking methodology that utilizes vehicle operating parameters, specifically speed based triggers, to initiate controlled belt tensioning prior to impact conditions. The proposed system integrates real time vehicle data with a motorized retractor mechanism to achieve occupant prepositioning, thereby enhancing restraint system readiness. A hysteresis-based control algorithm with a progressive tensioning profile is implemented to ensure system stability and maintain occupant comfort during normal driving scenarios. The approach is evaluated through simulation under representative frontal crash conditions, with comparative assessment against conventional emergency locking retractors. Results indicate improved occupant positioning and more favorable interaction with restraint systems, demonstrating enhanced control of occupant motion. The proposed methodology establishes a shift from reactive to proactive occupant restraint strategies and offers a feasible pathway for integration within existing vehicle architectures while maintaining usability and comfort considerations.

title: Computer Vision Approach for Parking management
authors: Fatma Masmoudi, Norah Saad Al-Garnas, Reema Nasser Al-Turki, Remaz Khaled Al-Assaf
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Parking management is a major challenge in modern urban areas. In our project, a system based on image processing has been developed to improve parking management. The system aims to accurately classify images to determine parking status and categorize vehicles into classes such as good parking or bad parking. The system also focuses on detecting license plates to verify whether a vehicle is parked in its designated area, such as spaces reserved for individuals with disabilities, ensuring proper usage or highlighting violations. The system relies on a carefully prepared and labeled dataset using image classification tools, contributing to improved detection accuracy, reducing traffic violations, enhancing the user experience, and minimizing the negative impacts on traffic infrastructure.

title: Electric Vehicle Range Estimation using Fuzzy Logic Model
authors: Andrew Myshkevych, Chase Hoffman, Steve Kang, Suk Jin Lee, B. Brian Park
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: The transition to Electric Vehicles (EVs) is vital for mitigating greenhouse gas emissions, yet the fear of insufficient battery charge, so-called "range anxiety", remains a primary barrier to widespread adoption. This anxiety is often exacerbated by standard manufacturer estimations that fail to account for real-time environmental factors and individual driving habits. This study addresses these limitations by developing an open-source, dynamic range estimation model using a fuzzy logic (FL) system. Unlike traditional static models, the proposed framework integrates critical real-world variables, including ambient temperature, battery State of Charge (SoC), and driver behavior. Utilizing On-Board Diagnostics (OBD-II) data from a 2012 Chevrolet Volt, the methodology processes four key inputs: acceleration aggressiveness, cruise steadiness, temperature, and SoC. These inputs are mapped through physics-aligned membership functions and human-readable rule sets, employing centroid defuzzification to generate a dynamic range multiplier between 0.6 and 1.1. This allows the system to adjust baseline estimates by accounting for up to a 40% reduction in adverse conditions or a 10% gain in optimal scenarios. Experimental results indicate that the FL model provides more realistic estimates than baseline onboard computer readouts. Evaluation metrics demonstrate high predictive accuracy, outperforming the standard ≤5% benchmark typically observed for more complex architectures. This initiative proves effective at adapting to complex driving scenarios, offering a real-time solution to mitigate range anxiety and validate the operational viability of EVs for prospective consumers.

title: Vaporfront Engineering and Morphological Evolution of Monolayer Semiconductor WSe2
authors: Jianing Liu, Xiaonan Deng, Meng Song, Yifeng Li, Simian Zhang, Ke Wang, Chen Wang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Controlling the synthesis of tungsten diselenide (WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) with various morphologies is crucial for its semiconductor properties. This work introduces sodium chloride as a growth promoter and glass blocks as an inhibitor to balance layer accumulation and size expansion by vaporfront engineering. Using confined CVD growth, 7 distinct monolayer semiconductor WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> morphologies are achieved. A mechanism for morphology evolution is proposed, providing a strategy to obtain WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> with diverse shapes and band structures, enhancing its potential for practical applications.

title: Improvement of Thickness Uniformity and Surface Roughness in PECVD SiN Waveguides
authors: Kang Lin, Xiaoxin Li, Zhenhua Hu, Caigan Chen, Jiayu Sun
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Silicon nitride (SiN) waveguides are favored in silicon photonics for their high refractive index contrast to oxide cladding layers (Δn≈ 0.5), CMOS compatibility, and broad optical transparency (400-2350 nm). Low pressure chemical vapor deposition (LPCVD) and plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) are the two most popular methods of depositing high quality SiN films. LPCVD films are grown at high temperatures (800-1200°C), the high temperature requirement for this deposition combined with the high tensile stress in these films make them incompatible for co-integration with back end of line (BEOL) CMOS platforms. In comparison, PECVD films are growing at lower temperatures (300-500°C), low temperature SiN films deposited using PECVD offer a compromise between the high temperature processing requirement and the optical propagation loss. While PECVD SiN usually suffers high thickness non-uniformity causing phase errors in optical arrays and high surface roughness inducing scattering losses. This work presents a systematic optimization of PECVD process parameters (RF power, pressure, gas ratio SiH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>/N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/NH₃, electrode spacing) to achieve <1% intra-wafer uniformity (1σ) and atomic-scale surface roughness (Ra <1nm) on 300-mm wafers with Lam VECTOR<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> PECVD system. By optimizing process, we demonstrated low uniformity and low surface roughness SiN waveguides with low propagation loss. This process enables high-yield manufacturing of low-loss photonic circuits.

title: Benchmarking Link Prediction in Complex Networks: A Leakage-Aware Evaluation Protocol and Practical Selection Guide
authors: Gowthami Vusirikkayala, Madhu Viswanatham V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Link prediction on complex networks remains challenging. A method that performs well on one instance of the train–test split, negative sampling (i.e., the set of nodes that are not connected), or candidate set may fail under alternative scenarios, and this problem is greatly exacerbated for temporal graphs where future interactions are often to flow before all interactions have occurred. In this paper, we take an evaluation-first perspective on link prediction in graphs: rather than adding to the already crowded and diverse space of methods that quickly age, we aim to categorize the current state of the art into heuristic scores, embedding-based methods, graph neural networks (GNNs), temporal methods, and discuss under which scenarios each of these families of predictors are useful in practice. We then present a leakage-aware evaluation protocol and a brief reporting checklist to make it easier for practitioners to report their results. Finally, we present a guide for model selection to help select methods that are practical for use on real-world graph problems, by considering factors such as graph sparsity, node features, graph size, and temporality. This talk deals with temporal graph characteristics, typical failures in benchmarking and some open problems concerning reproducibility and practical applicability.

title: A Survey of Text and Speech Resources for Hausa and Fongbe: Availability, Quality, and Gaps for NLP Development
authors: Mahounan Pericles Adjovi, Victor Olufemi, Roald Eiselen, Prasenjit Mitra
publish: 2026 IEEE Swiss Conference on Data Science and AI (SDS)
abstract: We survey publicly available text and speech resources for two West African languages: Hausa (Afroasiatic, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80-100 \mathrm{M}$</tex> speakers) and Fongbe (Niger-Congo, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2 \mathrm{M}$</tex> speakers, Benin), representing contrasting points on the resource availability spectrum. Through systematic search of academic repositories, data platforms, and web sources, we catalog parallel corpora, monolingual text, speech datasets, pre-trained models, and benchmarks, documenting size, domain, format, licensing, and accessibility for each resource. Hausa benefits from broad text diversity across news, encyclopedic, and educational domains, while Fongbe has attracted targeted speech data collection initiatives despite limited text coverage. Hausa is represented in a broad set of evaluation benchmarks spanning sentiment, emotion, and reasoning tasks, whereas Fongbe has a far more limited benchmark presence; both languages share coverage in NER (MasakhaNER 2.0), POS tagging (MasakhaPOS), and machine translation evaluation (FLORES-200, MAFAND-MT). We provide task-specific resource recommendations and identify priority gaps, including domain-diverse Fongbe text corpora and multi-speaker Hausa ASR data.

title: Fraud Detection in Cryptocurrency Markets with Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks
authors: Lidia Losavio, Luca Persia, Madan Sathe, Dimosthenis Pasadakis
publish: 2026 IEEE Swiss Conference on Data Science and AI (SDS)
abstract: Technological advancements in cryptocurrency markets have increased accessibility for investors, but concurrently exposed them to the risks of market manipulations. Existing fraud detection mechanisms typically rely on machine learning methods that treat each financial asset (i.e., token) and its related transactions independently. However, market manipulation strategies are rarely isolated events, but are rather characterized by coordination, repetition, and frequent transfers among related assets. This suggests that relational structure constitutes an integral component of the signal and can be effectively represented through graphical means. In this paper, we propose three graph construction methods that rely on aggregated hourly market data. The proposed graphs are processed by a unified spatio-temporal Graph Neural Network (GNN) architecture that combines attention-based spatial aggregation with temporal Transformer encoding. We evaluate our methodology on a real-world dataset comprised of pump-and-dump schemes in cryptocurrency markets, spanning a period of over three years. Our comparative results showcase that our graph-based models achieve significant improvements over standard machine learning baselines in detecting anomalous events. Our work highlights that learned market connectivity provides substantial gains for detecting coordinated market manipulation schemes.

title: Autonomous AI Whiteboard Systems: Transforming Modern Learning Spaces
authors: Noor Hafsa
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Traditional whiteboard maintenance necessitates manual intervention, causing disruptive interruptions in educational settings - a limitation persisting even in semi-automated solutions. This research introduces a fully autonomous whiteboard management system that intelligently initiates maintenance operations through integrated computer vision and motion detection technologies. Our framework combines three key innovations: (1) A transfer learning-based classification model deployed on Raspberry Pi that continuously analyzes whiteboard states via ceiling-mounted cameras, (2) Motion-activated sensors triggering automated digital archiving during user inactivity periods, and (3) Precision wiping mechanisms with bidirectional cleaning capabilities. Utilizing a DenseNet121 classifier for whiteboard state identification (>90% accuracy), the system enables full autonomy by triggering a clean-preserve-erase cycle within minutes of confirming user absence. By seamlessly maintaining writing surfaces while preserving educational content, this technology significantly enhances classroom operational efficiency and enables truly uninterrupted smart learning environments. Experimental validation in active classrooms demonstrates the system's effectiveness, positioning it as a transformative solution for modern educational infrastructure.

title: Fault Isolation Using FIB to Mitigate Interference in Failure Analysis Caused by OBIRCH
authors: Jiwei Zhou, Mian Rong, JianFang Huang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: IR-OBIRCH is widely employed in the failure analysis of integrated circuits as a crucial technique for failure localization. However, the limitations of the equipment can result in the identification of fault hotspots that are not always accurate and may even mislead analysts. Using the case of the MOM finger capacitive metal bridge as an example, this paper proposes a rapid and effective Focused Ion Beam (FIB) isolation scheme to systematically isolate faulty units, thereby enhancing Electrical Failure Analysis (EFA) methods in identifying the actual defect points. This approach offers a guiding framework for the identification and improvement of similar process defects.

title: Domain Specific Large Language Models: Potential, Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Sajid Iqbal
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: The invention of large language models has boosted NLP research to new heights and has changed the question-answering mechanism from smart to very smart level. Natural language processing (NLP) research has seen significant evolution in almost all of its sub-domains. The development of general-purpose language models like GPT-3 and BERT have shown great success however, their limitations in understanding domain-specific tasks argue for the development of domain-specific models (DS-LLMs).This paper provides an in-depth exploration of DS-LLMs, emphasizing their importance in addressing industry-specific challenges. We outline the limitations associated with general LLMs and highlight how these could be solved using DS-LLMs. As case studies, we examine a few application areas like education and healthcare. The adoption of DS-LLMs, for commercial applications, has also posed multiple challenges which are also discussed. The paper concludes with the increasing role of DS-LLMs in advancing language processing in the context of specific current and future domains/applications.

title: An Adaptive Framework with Optimized Machine Learning Models for Real-Time Zero-Day Cyber Threat Detection
authors: Swati Mohanta, Sunil Kumar Mohapatra, Raj Kumar Mohanta, Sneha Tewary, Sudiksha Bharadwaj, Sujata Chakravarty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Zero-day cyber threats pose a critical challenge in cybersecurity as they exploit unknown vulnerabilities before defenses are available. As a result, existing intrusion detection systems often struggle to detect such unseen attacks. This paper analyzes network flows from the CIC-Darknet2020 dataset using several machine learning models to identify previously unseen threats. Several algorithms—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, Naive Bayes, Decision Tree and XGBoost—were trained and fine-tuned using Optuna hyperparameter tuning framework to improve detection accuracy. To further enhance the performance, ensemble techniques including hard voting, soft voting and stacking were implemented to combine the strengths of individual models. Among these, the stacking ensemble achieved the highest accuracy resulting in 98.36%, reliably classifying darknet traffic flows such as Tor, VPN, Non-Tor, and Non-VPN. This work highlights the combination of hyperparameter tuning with ensemble learning, improving in zero-day threat detection and contributing to the development of real time cyber security systems.

title: Presymptomatic ALS Detection using Multimodal Machine Learning: A Review
authors: Snehalata Rout, Purna Chandra Sethi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) is one of the neurodegenerative diseases which grow rapidly and within few months of the disease the patient dies. The presymptomatic detection of the disease may lead to early ALS diagnosis due to which the death rate will reduce significantly. The pre-symptomatic detection of the disease involves various biomarkers such as speech, digital bio-marker, RNA sequence, and neuroimaging, etc using machine learning. Various researchers applied individual biomarkers following unimodal approach for early detection of this disease. The result show that ensemble and deep leaning machine learning model outperforms in uni-modal approach in comparison to traditional approaches. i.e. even with higher accuracy with uni-modal approach, problem remains unsolved with clinical data interpretation, validation with external data which has relative strength along with standardization of data. Recent advancement in this disease diagnosis shows that multimodal approach could be considered by integrating two or more biomarkers which will show better result in comparison to the traditional approach. The result becomes more trustworthy due to clinical dataset consideration for the prospective testing, training, validation as well as explainable AI integration.

title: Transformer-Based Rhythm Quantization of Performance MIDI Using Beat Annotations
authors: Maximilian Wachter, Sebastian Murgul, Michael Heizmann
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Multimedia (ICSM)
abstract: Rhythm transcription is a key subtask of notation-level Automatic Music Transcription (AMT). While deep learning models have been extensively used for detecting the metrical grid in audio and MIDI performances, beat-based rhythm quantization remains largely unexplored. In this work, we introduce a novel deep learning approach for quantizing MIDI performances using a priori beat information. Our method leverages the transformer architecture to effectively process synchronized score and performance data for training a quantization model. Key components of our approach include dataset preparation, a beat-based pre-quantization method to align performance and score times within a unified framework, and a MIDI tokenizer tailored for this task. We adapt a transformer model based on the T5 architecture to meet the specific requirements of rhythm quantization. The model is evaluated using a set of score-level metrics designed for objective assessment of quantization performance. Through systematic evaluation, we optimize both data representation and model architecture. Additionally, we apply performance and score augmentations, such as transposition, note deletion, and performance-side time jitter, to enhance the model’s robustness. Finally, a qualitative analysis compares our model’s quantization performance against state-of-the-art probabilistic and deep-learning models on various example pieces. Our model achieves an onset F1-score of 97.3% and a note value accuracy of 83.3% on the ASAP dataset. It generalizes well across time signatures, including those not seen during training, and produces readable score output. Fine-tuning on instrument-specific datasets further improves performance by capturing characteristic rhythmic and melodic patterns. This work contributes a robust and flexible framework for beat-based MIDI quantization using transformer models.

title: Auditing myocardial infarction detection models: Spatial and temporal XAI-based evaluation of Bi-LSTM networks using Grad-CAM and LIME
authors: Deivid Smarzaro, Mariana Rampinelli, Stephanie Moulin-Mares, Rodrigo Varejão Andreão
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVD) are the leading cause of global mortality, accounting for approximately 19 million deaths annually, with myocardial infarction (MI) being a critical manifestation. The 12-lead electrocardiogram (ECG) remains the gold standard for initial diagnosis; however, its manual interpretation is complex and prone to specialist scarcity. Deep learning models, particularly Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) networks, have shown high accuracy in automating MI detection. Despite this, the inherent “black-box” nature of these architectures hinders clinical adoption due to a lack of transparency and auditability. This study proposes a comprehensive framework to audit an MI detection model by applying two distinct Explainable AI (XAI) techniques: Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME). Using the PTB-XL dataset (21,799 ECGs), this study selected $11,601 \mathrm{MI}$ and Normal records sampled at 100 Hz, applying baseline-wander removal via median filters and high-frequency noise attenuation through 35 Hz Butterworth low-pass filtering. The three-layer Bi-LSTM ($\mathbf{3 2 / 1 6 / 4}$ units) reached 89.73% test accuracy. Auditability was evaluated using temporal and spatial (lead-wise) relevance across True Positive (TP), True Negative (TN), False Positive (FP), and False Negative (FN) exemplars. Grad-CAM highlighted clinically meaningful regions (QRS and ST) in TP cases (Sample #514, $P_{\text {MI }}=0.9950$), where detections relied on recurrent patterns across multiple beats. Conversely, FP errors (Sample #2302, $P_{\mathrm{MI}}=0.6461$) were largely triggered by early signal artifacts and baseline drift. LIME provided a granular decomposition of these decisions, identifying specific lead contributions. In the FN case (Sample #1343, $P_{\text {MI }}=0.2410$), LIME revealed that although the model detected MI-related features in leads V1 and V2, these were numerically outweighed by competing local weights favoring the “Normal” class in leads aVL and aVR. This dual-XAI approach effectively transforms automated ECG interpretations into an auditable process, providing a necessary layer of verification for clinical decision-support systems.

title: High Resistance Induced SRAM Reverse Vmin Ratio
authors: Yue Yang, Luping Wu, Haibo Chen
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This paper investigates an anomalous yield phenomenon in SRAM technology where elevated voltage operation results in lower yield compared to nominal voltage conditions, producing a Vmin ratio (Yield_LV/Yield_HV) > 1. This violates the conventional expectation that higher voltage improves stability. Through detailed failure analysis, we identify that the high R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">external</inf> of NMOS is a key contributing factor. The study establishes a critical link between this process module and the circuit-level performance anomaly, providing vital insights for process optimization.

title: Integrated Strategy for High Efficiency and Low Defect Wafer Planarization Processes
authors: Lifei Zhang, Xinchun Lu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This work establishes a comprehensive integrated strategy for optimizing the performance of chemical mechanical polishing in semiconductor manufacturing at advanced technology nodes. These results demonstrate that the slurry injection position plays a critical role in determining flow distribution, which affects the material removal rate. By implementing a dynamic slurry sweep mode, the within-wafer non-uniformity could be enhanced by 53% and the slurry consumption could be reduced by 15%, concurrent with a relatively high material removal rate of ~4666 Å/min. Furthermore, a pre-cleaning process using cationic surfactants achieves 99.94% removal of particle residues, delivering an atomically smooth surface. Our proposed integrated strategy for high efficiency and low defect wafer planarization offers a holistic approach to addressing the challenges of advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Edge-Based Auto-Labeling for Multiclass TinyML Application in IoT Environments
authors: Floreal Acebrón, Javier Prades, Erika Rosas, Juan-Carlos Cano, Pietro Manzoni, José.M Cecilia
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: Intelligent Environments require perception systems capable of adapting to evolving tasks, heterogeneous sensing conditions, and the resource constraints of large-scale IoT deployments. This work shows an edge-centric pipeline in which a highcapacity model, specifically YOLO12n, operates at the gateway to automatically label visual data and guide the specialization of TinyML models deployed on low-power devices. Focusing on two representative mobility-related classes, cat (present in COCO) and scooter (absent from COCO), we analyze the behavior of YOLO models under zero-shot conditions, during finetuning, and when incrementally integrating new classes. Results show that YOLO12n consistently outperforms YOLO11n in zeroshot evaluation and reaches near-perfect detection accuracy (mAP@50 up to 0.995) after only a few epochs of fine-tuning, with inference times below 5 ms on GPU-equipped edge nodes. When adding the new scooter class, the model rapidly adapts, yet subsequent fine-tuning on cats reveals strong catastrophic forgetting. Rehearsal-based retraining effectively mitigates this degradation, even when using replay buffers as small as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 2 0 o r i g i n a l}$</tex> dataset. These findings demonstrate that lightweight edge auto-labeling combined with efficient replay mechanisms enables sustainable, privacy-preserving, and continuously adaptive perception pipelines for next-generation Intelligent Environments and IoT monitoring infrastructures.

title: SmartNano: Data-Intelligent Modeling of Macrobrachium rosenbergii Chitosan Nanoparticles for Predictive Antibacterial Analysis
authors: S.John Justin Thangaraj, Divya Vijayakumar, Deepak. V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This paper explains the process of making and antimicrobial assay of the chitosan nanoparticles ( chNPs ) produced using Macrobrachium rosenbergii. The X-ray diffraction (XRD) revealed that the nanoparticles were semi-crystalline.FTIR spectra were used to verify the presence of functional groups of chitosan with the following absorption bands and their positions; 3410 cm -1 corresponding to N-H stretching vibrations of hydroxyl and amine groups, 1639 cm -1 (amide I), and 1072 cm -1 (C-O-C stretching). UV-Vis had a specific peak of 278 nm and XRD had semi crystalline nature with the largest reflections at 2θ of 22.3 and 31.5. The chNPs synthesized displayed a good concentration-dependent antibacterial activity on ten bacteria strains. The highest inhibition zone was observed with Escherichia coli (22 mm) followed by Salmonella typhi (20 mm) and moderate with Klebsiella pneumoniae, Streptococcus pyogenes and relatively lower with Vibrio species, which has selective antimicrobial potential. In order to optimize predictive analysis three machine learning algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Support Vector regression (SVR), and Elastic Net Regression (ENR) were used to predict experimental variables with the achievement of antibacterial efficacy. The RF model also proved to be the most effective, in terms of R <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.936, RMSE = 0.625, MAE= 0.498, being better than either SVR proves R <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.854 and ENR R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.778. Experimental validation combined with computational modeling is a good strategy to predict the antibacterial efficacy and further develop marine-derived bioactive nanomaterials in biomedical fields.

title: Development of Breast Tissue Phantoms for X-ray Diffraction Medical Imaging
authors: Fleur Helmantel, Scott Wolter
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Biomedical imaging has transformed breast cancer care by enabling earlier detection, more accurate staging, and minimally invasive treatment strategies. For non-metastatic cancer, breast conserving lumpectomy surgery is generally the best treatment option. Unfortunately, nearly half of these patients will require additional surgery due to the existence of residual cancer in the lumpectomy margin surrounding the tumor. Breast cancer pathology results can take several days leading to patient anxiety and treatment delays. Surgeons are therefore turning to new diagnostic approaches that allow tumor pathology to be performed in the operating room for immediate assessment and rapid treatment.This research focuses on the development of non-biological tissue-mimicking material (TMM) phantoms to replicate x-ray diffraction spectra for active tissue involved in the treatment of breast cancer: adipose, fibroglandular, and cancerous tissue. These physical models that simulate human tissue will be fashioned into different shapes and replicant bio tissue combinations to optimize systems performance. A MATLAB<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> computer program was written to access a database of organic solvents and water spectra that when combined would produce superposed spectra similar to that of the bio tissue specimen. Spectra fitting involved minimizing the residuals - the difference between the superposed spectra and the bio tissue spectra. Interesting observations were made with regard to water content for each of the fitted bio tissue results.

title: MDL Variability in Semiconductor Wafer Analysis: The Critical Role of the Extraction Solution and the Contaminants States
authors: Mike Ma, Sophia Wen
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This study demonstrates, using experimental data, that the Method Detection Limit (MDL) for analyzing metallic contamination on semiconductor wafers is a variable dependent on numerous factors inherent to sample preparation and measurement processes. Critically, the MDL applies only to contaminants extractable into the specific solution employed, as extraction efficiency varies with the solution's composition and concentration, as well as the contaminant's properties and size. Consequently, an MDL must be defined using the exact chemical solutions involved in the extraction process.

title: Smarter Vision: An Innovative Framework for Intelligent Object Identification Using YOLOv11x
authors: Ganesan P, M. Padmapriya, M. Easwaran, V. Elamaran, A.Aranganathan, Merlin Sheeba G, G. Sajiv
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Object recognition has been made to be a central component of computer vision, and allows computer systems to analyze and interpret the visual content of images as they do it more precisely. In this paper, Smarter Vision, an innovative framework, uses the power of YOLOv11x to detect objects in an efficient and smart way. The suggested approach is experimented with standardized images to prove the power and accuracy of its detection of numerous objects. Assessments show that YOLOv11x is better compared to other approaches in terms of mean average precision, performance speed, and ability to work in diverse conditions. Not only does this framework ensure high accuracy, but the inference speed is also quite reasonable, and that is why it can be used in research as well as in practice. It has been experimentally demonstrated that YOLOv11x is a reasonable option for object recognition since it balances the performance of detecting objects and implementation feasibility. This work contributes to the growing body of work in deep learning-based vision systems by establishing the scope of what YOLOv11x can do in transforming the landscape in the field of intelligent object detection.

title: Machine Learning-Based Hand Gesture Recognition Using Wearable Smart Glove
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Hand gesture recognition using wearable smart gloves has gained increasing attention in rehabilitation and assistive technology applications, particularly for post-stroke hand-function monitoring. However, many studies report overly optimistic performance due to improper data-splitting strategies that introduce data leakage between training and testing sets. This paper presents a leakage-free evaluation framework for glove-based hand gesture recognition using a publicly available American Sign Language (ASL) sensor dataset, with a focus on rehabilitation-relevant hand functions. Two supervised classifiers—Support Vector Machine with RBF kernel (SVM-RBF) and Decision Tree (DT)—are evaluated using Grouped K-Fold cross-validation to enforce trial-level separation. The results indicate that the SVM-RBF achieves the highest performance with a mean accuracy of 0.97 ± 0.06, followed by DT (0.92 ± 0.12). Results emphasize leakage-free evaluation for robust wearable gesture detection, with future work focusing on individual patient rehabilitation data validation. This paper presents a leakage-resistant, smart-glove-based hand gesture recognition evaluation pipeline that incorporates structured feature extraction alongside grouped cross-validation in an attempt to reduce trial-level data leakage.

title: Adaptive Edge Data Synchronization via Dual-Stream Attentional Hybrid Networks and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Nithya G, Nivetha E, Poornisha K, Navitha Sri M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Smart Health Care and Autonomous Systems require Efficient Data Synchronization on Edge Devices for Dynamic IoT Environments; Traditional Methods such as Static Priority Queues and Rule-Based Priorities cannot accommodate dynamic changes in network conditions and Real-Time Data Requirements. In this Paper we propose SyncMind - an Intelligent Edge Data Synchronization Framework utilizing a Dual-Stream Attention Hybrid Neural Network (DSAH-Net), combined with a Dueling Deep Q-Network (DQN) to Optimize Actions for Reliable Low-Latency Data Transmission - including; Sync, Defer or Compress. Results from experimental validation demonstrate that SyncMind provides 98.6% accurate classification of priority level, exceeding performance compared to Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) 84.2% and Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) 79.8%. The Dueling DQN is able to learn optimal synchronization policy with convergence stability, and Ablation Studies are used to validate the performance impact of each Architectural Component. Therefore, SyncMind facilitates Autonomous and Context-Aware Data Synchronization in Edge Computing Environments.

title: Bioimpedance Spectroscopy Meter for in vivo Human Rectal Tissue Measurements
authors: Sa Jaimes-Morales, Ve Aguirre-Cardona, Lo Tapasco-Tapasco, Ca González-Correa, Elkin Roman-Gomez
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: This work presents the development of a bioimpedance spectroscopy meter intended for in vivo rectal tissue measurements. The device is based on the Analog Discovery 2, a compact and robust platform that integrates a waveform generator, a two-channel oscilloscope, and dual power supplies, thereby simplifying the implementation of several essential stages required in a bioimpedance measurement system while enabling a flexible instrumentation architecture. The analog front-end, including a Howland current source and voltage and current measurement stages, was designed and implemented by the authors. The main design parameters, such as frequency range, current amplitude, and number of frequency points, were defined based on previous experimental studies conducted by our research group on colorectal tissue bioimpedance characterization. The developed device exhibited errors below one percent during resistor validation tests and minimal deviations in saline solution measurements after calibration, demonstrating adequate measurement stability and accuracy. Furthermore, the system demonstrated the capability to measure ex vivo porcine colorectal tissue and in vivo human rectal tissue, successfully identifying the characteristic dispersions typically observed in biological tissues. These results indicate that the proposed system provides stable impedance measurements and represents a compact instrumentation platform for bioimpedance spectroscopy studies of colorectal tissue under both experimental and preliminary clinical conditions.

title: 3D Alphanumeric Recognition in Arabic Sign Language Using WiLoR-Based 3D Hand Pose Features
authors: Yosr Mansour, Mariam Alaa, Asmaa Elsaid
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: This research proposes a robust Arabic Sign Language (ArSL) recognition approach utilizing 3D hand pose features extracted via the WiLoR framework and MANO parametric model. Departing from traditional RGB or 2D landmark methods, this study leverages a 151-dimensional feature space—including global orientation, articulated pose, and geometric distances—to achieve viewpoint-invariant hand characterization. The discriminative power of these 3D representations was validated through PCA and feature importance analyses, then evaluated using Random Forest, XGBoost, and MLP classifiers across alphabetical and numerical ArSL datasets. Notably, the optimized MLP achieved 95.72% accuracy on letters, while the Random Forest reached 99.75% on numbers. Furthermore, the system demonstrates exceptional computational efficiency, maintaining inference speeds between 837 and 1,126 FPS, proving its viability for real-time deployment on resource-constrained hardware. Visualizations of the latent feature manifolds further confirm that 3D structural data provides a highly effective foundation for high-speed, high-accuracy sign language recognition.

title: Decision Support for Resilient Foundation Model Scaling in Orbital Computing Systems
authors: Michael Chung, Peter A. Beling, Tyler Cody
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Future geospatial foundation-model pipelines will be shaped by the allocation of orbital infrastructure between data return, onboard computation, and the associated potential of orbital edge computing to serve those pipelines. These constraints induce distinct regimes for training, updating, and deploying foundation models, ranging from Earth-trained models operating on downlinked data to partially or fully in-space training enabled by orbital computing infrastructure. This paper develops a decision-support model that converts satellite launch growth into scenario-based estimates of downlink-oriented and compute-oriented capacity. Using payload launch data as an empirical launch-growth backbone, we compare logistic, exponential, and linear capacity forecasts under three compute-allocation assumptions. We then evaluate when cumulative in-space compute becomes order-equivalent to selected terrestrial foundation-model training thresholds. The results are not forecasts of specific in-space training programs. They provide an architectural planning tool for identifying when onboard computation becomes relevant to resilient geospatial foundation-model design.

title: A Dynamically Anchored Autoregressive Graph Network for Predicting ELF Wave Propagation in the Earth-Ionosphere Waveguide
authors: Hui Ran Zeng, Chen Yang, Nan Nan Sun, Tong He, Yi Long Liu, Kai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this work, we propose a novel deep learning model, GNN-ARGR (Dynamic Anchored Autoregressive Graph Neural Network), designed to emulate multi-step extremely low frequency (ELF: below 30 Hz) propagation in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide with physical consistency and numerical stability. The model learns an evolution operator using a graph-based representation of the Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) domain, enabling autoregressive multi-step prediction. To suppress the accumulation of prediction errors, the architecture integrates three core innovations: (1) a residual learning scheme that predicts field increments rather than absolute values; (2) a Dynamic Gated Anchoring (DGA) mechanism that adaptively blends evolution states with initial anchors to counteract drift; and (3) a Scheduled Sampling strategy that enhances rollout robustness. The results demonstrate that the GNN-ARGR model closely matches the benchmark numerical solution while improving computational efficiency. Furthermore, the model demonstrates robust long-term stability, accurately reproducing complex transient dynamic phenomena, including steep pulse rise and decay oscillations. The GNN-ARGR architecture provides a method for calculating the propagation of ELF waves in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide.

title: DM-UNet: Enhancing spatial awareness via dynamic Mamba for lightweight brain tumor segmentation
authors: Chengcheng Jin, Nor Safira Elaina Mohd Noor, Haidi Ibrahim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate brain tumor segmentation in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is crucial for clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. Deep learning-based glioma segmentation can assist doctors in making more informed decisions. Some segmentation models are computationally intensive and memory-heavy, making them impractical for real-time deployment in resource-constrained environments. To further realize the clinical potential of these models, it is essential to ensure both high segmentation accuracy and lightweight design for efficient operations. However, these two goals are usually difficult to achieve simultaneously. Therefore, this paper proposes a Dynamic Mamba U-Net (DM-UNet) model, based on Mamba, fully leveraging the linear computational complexity of the Mamba architecture. Considering the morphological variability of brain tumors, both axial and coronal scan results are utilized, and a dynamic Mamba mechanism is designed. To capture shallow features, channel attention and dynamic Mamba are employed in a parallel manner. Partial convolution is integrated into multi-scale convolutional layers to obtain more detailed tumor features. With these approaches, the number of model parameters is reduced to less than 0.1M, the model size is merely 0.36M, and the computational cost is 14.96G FLOPs. We conduct 5-fold cross-validation on BraTS2023, obtaining an average Dice of 89.60% and an HD of 4.11 mm. The performance is superior to other advanced glioma segmentation models, providing further support for the deployment of lightweight models on edge devices.
