title: Biofunctional Litopenaeus Vannamei Chitosan Nanoparticles: Spectroscopic Characterization and Predictive Antibacterial Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Divya Vijayakumar, Palaniappan Seedevi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This paper explains the process of making and antimicrobial assay of the chitosan nanoparticles(chNPs) produced using Litopenaeus vannamei. The X-ray diffraction (XRD) revealed that the nanoparticles were semi-crystalline.FTIR spectra were used to verify the presence of functional groups of chitosan with the following absorption bands and their positions; 3432 cm -1 (OH/NH), 1639 cm -1 (amide I), and 1072 cm -1 (C-O-C stretching). UV-Vis had a specific peak of 325 nm and XRD had semi crystalline nature with the largest reflections at 2 0 of 10.3, 20.1, and 30.4. The chNPs synthesized displayed a good concentration-dependent antibacterial activity on ten bacteria strains. The highest inhibition zone was observed with Escherichia coli (27 mm) followed by Salmonella typhi (26 mm) and moderate with Klebsiella pneumoniae, Streptococcus pyogenes and relatively lower with Vibrio species, which has selective antimicrobial potential. In order to optimize predictive analysis three machine learning algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Support Vector regression (SVR), and Elastic Net Regression (ENR) were used to predict experimental variables with the achievement of antibacterial efficacy. The RF model also proved to be the most effective, in terms of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.928, RMSE = 0.648, MAE= 0.512, being better than either SVR proves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.841 or ENR R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.763. Experimental validation combined with computational modeling is a good strategy to predict the antibacterial efficacy and further develop marine-derived bioactive nanomaterials in biomedical fields.

title: Sentence-Similarity Pipeline for Paraphrase Detection in Uzbek Texts
authors: Djamshid Sultanov, Khusniya Akhmedova, Hyunchul Ahn
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: Measuring semantic similarity between sentences is central to plagiarism detection, authorship attribution, question- answering, and many other NLP applications. We consolidate existing Vector-Space and deep neural approaches into a single text-processing pipeline (TPP) for low-resource Uzbek. The TPP supports flexible tokenization, optional emoji handling, and alternative embedding stages (TF-IDF, Word2Vec, Doc2Vec). A lightweight API layer feeds sentence embeddings to a modular paraphrase-classification component that hosts distance-based or neural models. Our design is validated on publicly available Uzbek corpora and synthetic sentence pairs; cosine similarity combined with a logisticregression meta-classifier yields up to 0.86 F -score without external WordNet resources. The pipeline is released as an install-and-play prototype for educational use.

title: Accelerating HunyuanPortrait Towards Real-Time Inference: A Performance Optimization Study
authors: Zain Ali Siddiqui
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: The HunyuanPortrait model is a state-of-the-art diffusion-based framework for generating animated portrait videos from a single image and driving video, as introduced by Xu et al.. While it achieves high visual fidelity, its inference latency is prohibitive for real-time applications. In this work, we investigate an extensive set of optimization techniques to accelerate HunyuanPortrait’s inference without compromising perceptual quality. Our efforts include reducing the number of diffusion steps (e.g., from 25 down to 10–12), adjusting the number of sampled frames per batch (n_sample_frames), introducing a decoding chunk strategy (decode_chunk_size=4), and leveraging PyTorch 2.0’s compiler (torch.compile(…)with the inductor backend) to JIT-compile the denoising U-Net. We evaluate the resulting speed-quality trade-offs using metrics such as frame rate (FPS), generation time, peak GPU memory, and fidelity measures (PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS). Initial experiments show that reducing the inference steps yields substantial speedups (from $\tilde 0.73$ FPS at 25 steps to $\tilde 1.31$ FPS at 12 steps) with only minor degradations in PSNR $(\tilde 9.13 \to 9.06{\text{ dB}})$ and SSIM $\left( {\tilde 0.195} \right)$ and a negligible increase in LPIPS $(\tilde 0.66 \to 0.67)$. Further, enabling decode_chunk_size=4and increasing n_sample_framesimproves throughput by up to $\tilde 1.8 \times $ under certain configurations. Compiling the U-Net using PyTorch 2.0’s torch.compile reduces per-step overhead, cutting an intermediate optimized configuration from approximately 192s to $\tilde 101{\text{s}}$ for a 120-frame video. For clarity, the full baseline configuration requires $\tilde 413{\text{s}}$, and all timing references are aligned accordingly. Throughout all optimizations, the peak GPU memory footprint (Approx. 22.3GB) remains largely constant. Our results suggest that HunyuanPortrait can be tuned for interactive frame rates, achieving near-real-time inference with largely preserved visual quality. Future work will evaluate more sophisticated schedulers (e.g., DDIM) and adaptive step skipping, as well as explore deployment on specialized hardware to further close the gap to true real-time performance.

title: Cognitively Inspired Feature Engineering for Predicting Online Product Review Polarity
authors: Travis Vitello
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: This work presents a cognitively inspired feature engineering framework for predicting online product review polarity, emphasizing interpretable and psychologically grounded representations of text. Using the UCSD Amazon "Electronics" dataset, a balanced subset of 40,000 reviews was constructed and formulated as a binary classification task (negative vs. positive).The proposed approach integrates multiple cognitively motivated lexical representations, including emotion mapping via the NRC Emotion Lexicon (EmoLex), sentiment polarity via Bing Liu’s lexicon, and contextual sentiment progression using Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner (VADER) compound scoring. To enable controlled comparisons of feature representations, twelve model configurations were constructed across Random Forest and XGBoost machine learning (ML) classifiers, including baseline and Optuna-optimized variants spanning emotion-only, emotion + sentiment, and emotion + sentiment + VADER feature sets.Results show a consistent performance progression across feature sets, with emotion-only models demonstrating relatively worse results while the addition of contextual sentiment yielded the largest gains. The optimized XGBoost model achieved a multi-seed average 75.04% accuracy and 0.825 ROC-AUC, with comparable Random Forest performance, indicating that feature design may contribute more strongly to predictive outcome than differences in model architecture.SHAP analysis reveals that global sentiment signals dominate predictions, while emotional features provide secondary refinement. Overall, the results demonstrate that structured, cognitively informed feature design can improve classification performance while maintaining interpretability.

title: Bridging Lexical and Semantic Gaps: Query Expansion Analysis with FastText for Information Retrieval
authors: Shoaib Mustafa Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of multiple different query expansion strategies aimed at enhancing document retrieval accuracy using FastText-based models and semantic reranking. The study explores the limitations of baseline FastText embeddings, which often rely on morphological similarity, thus leading to semantically incoherent nearest neighbours. In order to address this, two enhancement strategies are experimented with, namely random string encoding for multiword phrases, and thus presents a solution to enhanced performance in information retrieval by retrofitting FastText embeddings with ConceptNet. A strict keyword filtering pipeline, then followed by semantic reranking using SentenceTransformer is integrated, in order to assess performance on Wikipedia-based document retrieval. Results display that ConceptNet-enhanced FastText achieved a recall@3 of 93.21%, thus outperforming both the random-masked FastText model (87.63%) and baseline Elasticsearch keyword retrieval (76.75%). A comparison against a zero-shot LLM-based expansion pipeline using GPT-4o is also included, which presented the highest recall@3 of 99.06%, therefore highlighting the potential of context-aware generative expansion. These findings therefore shed light to the importance of semantically enriched expansion strategies and provide a framework for evaluating embedding quality in retrieval tasks.

title: A Lightweight Deep Learning and Data Augmentation Framework for Cattle Weight Estimation Under Unstable Camera Conditions and Small Datasets
authors: Sachin Vijay Kumar, Xinyao Sun, Irene Cheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Multimedia (ICSM)
abstract: Cattle weight measurement is an important task for cattle ranchers and farmers. The weight of the cattle is useful for estimating the growth rate, time to slaughter, current and future need for feed, and the amount of medication for the sick cattle. Traditionally, weights are measured using physical weigh scales or tapes. This process requires physical labor, is time-consuming, and disturbs the daily routine of the livestock. The semi-automated computer-assisted methods will free farmers from this laborious task. However, machine learning and deep learning prediction methods require large amounts of data, and the collection is costly and time-consuming. In addition to that, unstable camera position, changing background, and illumination affect the extracted features used in the prediction. In this paper, we propose an image alignment method combined with semantic-aware data augmentation to address unstable camera conditions and limited datasets. Our lightweight deep learning model achieves significant improvements(about 18%) in cattle weight estimation accuracy compared to traditional machine learning, curve fitting, and MLP regression models.

title: AI Governance for Assessment and Academic Integrity in Higher Education An Institutional Illustrative Case
authors: Omar Al-Jarrah, Aws I. AbuEid
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The accelerated integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in higher education has transformed assessment into a governance-intensive and risk-sensitive domain rather than a purely technological or pedagogical innovation. While AI-enabled systems are increasingly embedded in institutional assessment practices, their deployment has often outpaced the establishment of structured governance mechanisms necessary to ensure reliability, transparency, and academic integrity. This governance gap has heightened concerns regarding accountability, fairness, and institutional trust in AI-supported assessment processes. Consequently, coherent governance frameworks are required to mitigate systemic risks and to position AI as a controlled and accountable component within the broader academic infrastructure.This paper proposes a strategic governance framework for AI-enabled assessment grounded in institutional oversight, structured risk management, and regulatory alignment. A defining feature of the framework is the explicit preservation of human-in-the-loop decision authority, ensuring that artificial intelligence operates strictly as a decision-support mechanism. At the same time, final academic judgments remain under human responsibility. The framework is illustrated through an institutional case of governance-embedded AI systems implemented within formal policy boundaries and oversight structures.By conceptualising assessment and academic integrity as high-risk institutional domains, the study underscores the importance of governance-oriented AI adoption strategies that balance innovation with accountability. The proposed framework provides practical guidance for academic leaders, policymakers, and system designers seeking to integrate artificial intelligence into higher education assessment while safeguarding academic standards, institutional credibility, and professional judgment.

title: Analysis of Physicochemical Factors Affecting the Quality of Cacao Wine–Based Beverages using Random Forest Algorithm
authors: Martina D. Peñalber
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: This study investigates the physicochemical and sensory factors influencing the quality of cacao wine–based beverages using machine learning techniques. A dual-dataset approach was employed, integrating a secondary wine quality dataset containing physicochemical properties and a primary dataset derived from sensory evaluation of cacao wine–infused cocktails. The primary dataset included attributes such as color, taste, fineness, aroma, alcohol content, and overall acceptability. A Random Forest Regression model was applied to both datasets to evaluate predictive performance and identify key determinants of quality. The wine dataset yielded a moderate predictive performance with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.551, Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.348, and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.5897, with alcohol, volatile acidity, and free sulfur dioxide identified as the most influential variables. In contrast, the cacao dataset demonstrated strong predictive performance, achieving an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.7895, MSE of 0.035, and RMSE of 0.1873. Feature importance analysis revealed that fineness (0.28) and aroma (0.28) were the most significant predictors of overall acceptability, followed by taste (0.23) and color (0.21), while alcohol content (0.01) had minimal influence. The findings highlight that while physicochemical properties contribute to the formation of beverage characteristics, sensory attributes play a more dominant role in determining consumer acceptability. The study demonstrates the effectiveness of machine learning in identifying key quality drivers and provides a data-driven framework for optimizing cacao wine–based beverages.

title: An Ensemble-Based Deep Learning Framework for Automated Multi-Class Diabetic Retinopathy Severity Classification
authors: Priyadarshini R, Sumanth S, Sudeep Kumar, Vinay Kumar V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of preventable blindness that requires early and accurate detection. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for automated DR severity classification using transfer learning and ensemble learning. ResNet34 and EfficientNetV2 are combined using a soft-voting strategy to classify retinal fundus images into five severity levels. A structured preprocessing pipeline, data augmentation, and class-weighted optimization are employed to improve robustness and address dataset imbalance. Grad-CAM visualization is integrated to enhance interpretability by highlighting clinically relevant retinal regions. Experimental evaluation on the APTOS 2019 dataset achieved a validation accuracy of 82.22%, outperforming individual baseline models. The proposed framework demonstrates strong potential for reliable automated DR screening and teleophthalmology applications.

title: Challenges in non-invasive measurement of ice swimmers’ physiological parameters
authors: Jakub Janowicz, Michał Starosolski
publish: 2026 IEEE Medical Measurements &amp; Applications (MeMeA)
abstract: ”Ice swimming” is a term describing a specific sports discipline that first occurred in 2009 along with IISA (International Ice Swimming Association) foundation. It involves swimming in the ice cold water (water temperature must not exceed $5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$) without a heating wetsuit, wearing only swimming cap, swimming goggles and a standard swimming suit. Ice swimming is associated with severe cold exposure and rapid physiological responses, posing significant challenges for reliable monitoring of athletes’ physiological parameters. This paper focus was to evaluate the feasibility and limitations of noninvasive core body temperature ($C B T$) and heart rate (HR) measurement in ice swimmers during multiple runs at different anatomical sites, as well as the identification of physiological patterns insensitive to cold water adaptation of the individuals. Measurements were conducted by the athletes during ice swimming training sessions in Wroclaw, Poland, using commercially available measuring instruments. The analysed data consists of training session recordings of 16 individuals including 13 males with the mean age of ($40.46 \pm 6.30$) years and 3 females aged $(44.33 \pm 6.03)$ years. The study underscores the need for novel sensors, advanced signal processing algorithms or alternative, multimodal measurement strategies to enable reliable monitoring of core physiological parameters in extreme cold-exposure sports. Although the experiment has exploratory character, the results may contribute to the development of more robust monitoring systems and improvement of core body temperature ($C B T$) estimation algorithms for ice swimming athletes. Index Terms-ice swimming, physiological monitoring, core body temperature, heart rate, wearable monitoring

title: Failure Analysis and Localization of CDM Induced Micro Damage in High Speed I/O of Advanced FINFET Nodes
authors: Caiyun Ren, Jianfang Huang, Mian Rong
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This study reports CDM (Charged Device Model)-induced micro-damage in advanced FinFET high-speed I/O circuits. Devices subjected to 250V CDM stress exhibited microampere-level leakage current while still passing functional tests, making such damage difficult to detect. Systematic failure analysis localized the defect to gate oxide breakdown in the driver transistor Mn1. Notably, this failure mechanism does not conform to any of the four previously reported CDM failure modes, suggesting a new CDM-induced damage signature. Furthermore, increasing the device area was found to significantly enhance CDM robustness without compromising logic functionality, offering a practical, device-level design strategy for improving reliability in advanced technology nodes.

title: NeuroNest: Multi-Modal AI System for Autism Prediction and Assistive Developmental Support
authors: Harini Sri S, B. Saishree, Deepthi S. V, Harmayni Khandal, Savitha Hiremath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Early diagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is essential for timely intervention, yet conventional clinical assessment procedures are resource intensive and often inaccessible in many regions. This paper proposes NeuroNest, a multimodal AI-based framework that integrates facial image-based deep learning and behavioral questionnaire-based machine learning for ASD screening and assistive developmental support. Multiple convolutional neural network architectures were evaluated for facial classification, where Xception achieved the best performance with an accuracy of 0.795 and F1-score of 0.792. For behavioral prediction, Support Vector Machine (SVM) outperformed Decision Tree, Random Forest, and XGBoost with an accuracy of 0.983 and F1-score of 0.982. A weighted decision-level fusion mechanism was used to compute the final ASD risk score. In addition, the system incorporates assistive modules including emotion recognition, AI-assisted storytelling, handwriting feedback, and longitudinal parent monitoring within a deployable digital platform.

title: Behavioral Customer Segmentation for Fixed Broadband: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Eshraq Saeed Awwad, Mohamed Tharwat Waheed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: In the competitive telecommunications landscape, understanding customer behavior and preferences is critical for service differentiation and revenue optimization. This paper presents a comprehensive machine learning framework for large-scale customer segmentation of ADSL broadband services, analyzing a smaple over 700 K subscribers across more than 24 behavioral and transactional features. We employ a robust pre-processing pipeline incorporating outlier capping, Yeo-Johnson transformation, and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction, followed by K-means clustering to identify distinct customer segments. Our methodology successfully identifies seven unique customer clusters with varying usage patterns, service quality experiences, and value-added service adoption rates. The segmentation reveals actionable insights including premium users consuming higher average data volumes, highly engaged multi-service users, and at-risk segments requiring targeted retention strategies. This work demonstrates the practical application of machine learning to real-world telecommunications data at scale, providing a foundation for personalized marketing, churn prevention, and service optimization strategies that directly impact business profitability and customer satisfaction.

title: Improvement on Gate Disturb for SONOS Memory Via Tuning Nitrogen-Oxygen Ratio of ONO Dielectric Layer
authors: Jingsong Peng, Yang Ding, Kegang Zhang, Ning Wang, Binghan Li, Shijin Ding
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: The silicon-oxide-nitride-oxide-silicon (SONOS) memory suffers a lot from the gate disturb during read operation especially for shrunken cells, hindering the application on high-reliability non-volatile memories. Herein, we proposed a strategy to resist gate disturb by lowering the nitrogen/oxygen ratio of SiN trap layer. The absolute value of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TE</inf> can be cut by almost 80%, which decreased from 0.141 to 0.029. This improvement mainly caused by a broader barrier that significantly toughens Fowler-Nordheim tunneling. This strategy provides a simple and effective method to fabricated high-reliability SONOS memory with improved gate disturb performance.

title: Heated Ion Implantation Technology for FinFET Source Drain Extension Formation
authors: Ruijun Cui, Shiwei Xia, Lijun Yang, Junlong Xu, Xingbo Chen, Junhua Hong, Changyong Zhang, Yongsheng Wang, Wei Zhao
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Room-temperature ion implantation for FinFET doping inevitably introduces severe lattice damage in the fin body, which degrades the electrical performance and structural reliability of FinFET devices. Thus, mitigating implantation-induced damage is critical for advancing FinFET technology. In this work, heated ion implantation is proposed and systematically investigated for the fabrication of lightly doped drain (LDD) regions in FinFETs. Phosphorus ion (P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>) implantations were performed on bare silicon wafers at different temperatures, and the process efficacy was comprehensively evaluated via Thermal Wave (TW) analysis for lattice disorder characterization, sheet resistance (Rs) measurements for dopant activation assessment, secondary-ion mass spectrometry (SIMS) for dopant depth profile analysis, and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) for microstructural observation. The optimized heated implantation process was subsequently applied to FinFET device fabrication for LDD formation. Experimental results show that increasing the implantation temperature effectively eliminates lattice damage in the fin structure. In comparison with conventional room-temperature implantation, the proposed heated implantation achieves damage-free fin structures while maintaining satisfactory dopant activation efficiency and precise dopant profile control. This study verifies that heated ion implantation is a feasible and high-efficiency solution to alleviate implantation-induced damage and boost the performance of advanced FinFET devices.

title: Predicting Heart Failure Outcomes Using Machine Learning Models on Clinical and Laboratory Data
authors: Nabeel M Sheikh, Amit Kumar, Vansh Agarwal, Rucha Deshmukh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Heart failure is a leading cause of mortality worldwide, requiring timely and accurate prediction to guide clinical decision-making. This study explores the use of machine learning models to predict mortality outcomes in patients with heart failure using clinical and laboratory data from 296 individuals. The dataset was obtained from the UCI Machine Learning Repository and includes variables such as age, comorbidities, vital signs, and laboratory test results.Four models were evaluated using five-fold cross-validation: logistic regression, decision tree, k-nearest neighbors (KNN), and extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost). Among these, logistic regression achieved the highest overall accuracy (0.74) and precision (0.64), while XGBoost demonstrated strong recall for identifying high-risk patients, highlighting its ability to capture complex, non-linear relationships in clinical data. Model interpretability was supported using SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values to explain the influence of individual features on predictions. These findings reinforce the value of data-driven decision support in cardiology and align with recent efforts to integrate artificial intelligence into clinical practice.Future work should expand on this foundation by incorporating larger, multicenter datasets and longitudinal health records to improve generalizability and early risk detection. Overall, this study underscores how machine learning, when carefully validated and interpreted, can provide meaningful insights into patient outcomes in heart failure care.

title: LSTM-GNN Hybrid Approach for Strengthening Authentication in Online Banking and E-Commerce
authors: Jyothi G, Natalia Savina, Irina Kuznetsova, Katuru Jaswanth, S. Ragamayi, Naveen Nandal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Phishing is a type of cybercrime wherein criminals trick consumers into divulging critical information by using fake websites that look like real ones. Particularly in ecommerce and online banking, where sensitive financial data is often transmitted, this presents a serious risk to private data. Then clean and filter data from numerous internet sources before analysis to tackle this difficulty. Then, the employ the Term Frequency-Inverse Class Frequency method for successful feature extraction. Online banking and ecommerce fraud detection and prediction accuracy can be improved with the use of a new hybrid model suggested in this study. This model combines Graph Neural Networks with Long Short-Term Memory networks. Interactions between users and transactions are modelled by the GNN, whilst the LSTM component records sequential dependencies in the transaction data. When compared to traditional machine learning models applied separately, the suggested LSTM-GNN model outperforms them experimentally, achieving an astounding accuracy of 96.33%. The results show that a combination of LSTM and GNN provides a strong answer to the problem of phishing and other forms of online fraud, which can help make commercial and financial systems more secure.

title: Hierarchical Explainable Risk Scoring for Security Alert Prioritization in Security Operations Centers
authors: Siva Prasad Nandi, Rajesh Purushothaman, Sai Manohar Nethi, Pavan Nutalapati, Naga Satya Praveen Kumar Yadati, Anil Kumar Thimmapuram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Security operations centers have a constant struggle to prioritize the large number of heterogeneous alerts, in which traditional detection systems usually give classifications without adequate decision support or interpretability. This work introduces a hierarchical explainable risk scoring framework for security alert prioritization that treats incident-grade prediction as an analyst-oriented triage problem instead of a traditional binary detection problem. Using the Microsoft GUIDE security incident dataset, the method integrates structured SOC alert attributes, temporal context and security semantics in a lightweight LightGBM-based multiclass model followed by risk-based ranking and explanation generation with feature importance and SHAP analysis. The proposed framework achieved an accuracy of 0.9030, macro precision of 0.9034, macro recall of 0.8906, macro F1-score of 0.8962, ROC-AUC of 0.9845 and PR- AUC of 0.9697 in addition to a good class-wise performance, including the F1-score of 0.91 for TruePositive incidents. The results further showed that organizational context, detector behavior, alert semantics, temporal cues, and attack-technique information were the dominant drivers of prioritization, highlighting the novelty of combining accurate multiclass SOC triage with transparent, explanation-aware cyber defense support.

title: Cognitive Restructure of Crowd Emotions and Predicting Crowd Behavior using Concrete Autoencoders Superimposed over AAM and FaceNet
authors: Supratim Basu, B Janet
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel framework for restructuring and predicting crowd behavior in real-time by integrating Concrete Autoencoders (CAE) over Active Appearance Models (AAM), and FaceNet. Unlike binary classification models, this work utilizes a multivariate classification approach to infer emotional states in dense crowds. We introduce a theoretical minimum resolution requirement of 4 pixels per feature attribution, derived from the Nyquist-Shannon Sampling Theorem, to ensure accurate feature extraction in low-resolution crowd imagery. Furthermore, we present a statistical test to analyze the re-generated data and predict the collective emotional state of a crowd. Experimental results using the Affectnet dataset demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed pipeline in identifying identifying emotional and attraction-based features with reduced computational load.

title: Guardrail-First Architecture for Multi-Agent Systems: Typed Rails as First-Class Connectors
authors: Viswapriyan Ragupathy
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper introduces the Guardrail-First Architecture (GFA), a unique paradigm for multi-agent system safety, compliance, and governance. GFA uses Design-Science Research to enforce semantic, policy, and capability restrictions on inter-agent and agent–tool interactions with Typed Rails as first-class architectural connectors. Agent, Tool Interface, Typed Rails, and Policy Engine/Evidence Store layers ensure verifiable, policy-compliant activities with traceable evidence. GFA maintains operational performance while enhancing governance, as shown by a 47% decrease in dangerous activities, a 31% increase in policy adherence, and little latency overhead in a controlled MAS environment. Quality feedback emphasises openness, auditability, and minimised manual monitoring. GFA provides a solid foundation for trustworthy and responsible MAS implementation by architecturally formalising safety and compliance semantics. Automated Typed Rail synthesis, dynamic policy adaption, and heterogeneous, enterprise-level MAS scalability will be studied.

title: A Multi-Scale Simulation Tool for Semiconductor Fabrication Modeling
authors: Yuqi Yang, Sisi Liu, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: The fabrication of advanced 3D transistors involves multi-scale and multi-physics phenomena, making conventional trial-and-error optimization increasingly challenging. To address this challenge, we develop a next-generation multi-scale simulation tool that seamlessly bridges atomic-scale DFT and MD, feature-scale kMC and chamber-scale CFD. Applied to Si/SiGe thermal selective etching, this tool reveals the underlying reaction mechanisms, predicts morphology evolution, and pinpoints optimal process windows. By unifying these diverse physical domains, this tool establishes a rigorous physics-driven foundation for virtual chamber technologies in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Real-Time Cyber Threat Intelligence Detection and Response Using Federated Machine Learning for Threat Classification
authors: Darshan Prajapati, Punitha.K, Dhayabaran M, Ramyabharathi R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: This report describes the construction and deployment of a cyber threat intelligence detection and response system (federated machine learning) in real-time. The system will seek to identify and categorize the network-based attacks and employ sophisticated machine learning models, including deep neural network (DNN) and quantized neural networks (QNN), in order to effectively classify threats. The solution will consist of Flask-based backend which will have capabilities such as uploading files, model inference and delivering real-time alerts to a final user through emails and personal messages and texts using Twilio. There is also SHAP shapley additive exPlanations that are used to explain the explainability of the model providing an understandability of the decision making process. The system can examine the data on the network traffic and can detect the possibility of an attack and provide an alert should the system identify some suspicious activities in the network. This framework can be implemented to improve the proactive cyber security measures through machine learning models that can conduct real-time detection and in-depth analysis.

title: Energy-Aware Transformer-Enabled AI MIMO Antenna Architectures for 5G/6G Beamforming
authors: Jagan Mohan Rao Saride, V L N Phani Ponnapalli, Giriraj Kumar Prajapati, R.Swaminathan, R Saravanakumar, P.Sathiyamurthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Millimeter-wave multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) links require precise beam steering to maintain multi-gigabit throughput in rapidly time-varying channels. Data-driven beamforming is attractive due to the ability to map channel observations to beam commands with low signalling overhead, but at the same time, narrow, high-gain beams with controlled sidelobes need to be delivered under the constraints of real-time operation and edge hardware platforms. Conventional code-book search often degrades in the presence of blockage and angular uncertainty, which results in an increase of errors and diminishes overall efficiency. Experimental investigations at 28 GHz using an 8×8 dual-polarized patch array show that the gain of the baseline configuration is 22.5 dBi, the beamwidth is 11deg and the sidelobe level is −14 dB. An AI-enabled pipeline based on a modified Swin Transformer trained on 15000 mixed samples from simulation and measurements to predict beam-forming vectors and phase shifts. The proposed method improves radiation characteristics to 25.8dBi gain, 7.5deg beamwidth and −19dB sidelobes, with an inference latency of 2.1 ms. Link-level metrics also improve - bit error rate (BER) decreases from 3.8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> to 1.0×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> at 20dB SNR, data rate grows from 7.5 to 10.3Gbps and radiation efficiency improves from 82% to 89%. The energy efficiency is thus increased from 5.6 to 8.0 bits/J, which means better throughput per unit energy. These results validate the feasibility of attention-guided beam prediction for stable 28 GHz operation, while a compute footprint of 6.8 W and 180 MB emphasise the need for deployment-aware optimisation.

title:
Fluorine Doping for Enhanced CMP Performance: Boosting Ce
                    <sup>3+</sup>
                    /Oxygen Vacancies in One-step Synthesized Ceria Abrasives
authors: Fangwei Lu, Junjie Xue, Linyi Shen, Xinping Qu, Wei Yuan, Fan Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Fluorine-doped ceria (CeO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) nanoparticles are synthesized via a simple one-step precipitation method for application as abrasive particles in chemical mechanical polishing (CMP). The incorporation of fluorine ions increased the Ce<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3+</sup> content on the ceria surface, leading to a significant improvement in the material removal rate (MRR) and the surface quality during SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> wafer polishing.

title: Inverted Plasmonic Hexa-Star Nanoantenna as SERS Substrate with High EM Enhancement
authors: Daksh Pandey, Yashna Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: In this work, we propose an engineered substrate based on surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) that exhibits multiple electromagnetic (EM) hotspots with each hotspot capable of producing an electromagnetic enhancement factor of ~10⁵. When combined with the molecular chemical enhancement contribution of ~10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, the proposed design enables an overall SERS enhancement factor ~10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup> over multiple hotspots. The proposed design can be used to fabricate SERS substrates with multiple EM enhancement areas, particularly for sensing applications. While numerous SERS substrates exhibiting large enhancement factors at a single, highly localized hotspot have been widely reported in the past, the realization of substrates that can simultaneously provide multiple hotspots with sufficiently high and uniform enhancement remains a significant challenge. This paper, through comprehensive Finite Difference Time Domain (FDTD) simulations, realizes multi-hotspot SERS substrates and demonstrates a passive tunability of the plasmon resonance wavelengths by varying the geometrical features of the proposed nanostructure.

title: Aban: Predicting ADOS Score from Upper-Body Movements and Eye Gaze for Autism Severity Assessment
authors: Badar Almarri, Safa Alsalman, Maryam Aldulami, Nujud Alobaid, Jinan Almaghlouth, Sara Aljamea, Aisha Alkhateeb
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that causes challenges in social interaction, communication, and behavior. Early diagnosis of ASD is crucial for timely intervention and improved developmental outcomes. One of the most widely used tools to assess the severity of ASD is the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule (ADOS). In this paper, a deep learning model is proposed to predict ADOS scores utilizing the DREAM dataset, which was collected during therapeutic sessions using three RGB cameras and two RGBD (Kinect) cameras. The dataset includes eye-gaze and upper body movement data. To capture the sequential and temporal nature of this dataset, a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), specifically a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) architecture, is employed. The model was trained on partially incomplete participant sessions, where pre-ADOS scores were used for cases missing final evaluations to retain additional training samples. The model achieved a classification accuracy of 95.54% in predicting ADOS scores ranging from 4 to 20. This approach demonstrates the potential to support the diagnostic process by offering a more objective method to assess the severity of ASD, reducing the dependence on subjective evaluation, and providing a scalable and data-driven solution for the early evaluation of ASD.

title: Explainable Temporal Attention for Anomaly Detection in Medical Time-Series Data
authors: Soumia Benkrid, Leila Hamdad, Khaled Abderrahmane Habouche
publish: 2026 22nd International Conference on Intelligent Environments (IE)
abstract: The effective interpretation of medical time-series data remains a significant challenge in AI diagnostics, as temporal patterns critical for diagnosis are often obscured in conventional “black-box” models. This paper presents MedAttnAID, an explainable temporal attention framework designed for unsupervised anomaly detection in sensor-generated medical time-series data. Our approach integrates a bidirectional LSTM autoencoder with interpretable attention mechanisms that highlight diagnostically relevant temporal segments, complemented by a novel Clinical Pattern Loss (CPLoss) function that preserves clinically significant temporal patterns during reconstruction. We validate the framework on thermal time-series data from wearable breast cancer monitoring devices, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85.1 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex> score and 95 % breast-level localization accuracy while producing interpretable attention heatmaps that align with clinical expectations. The framework's modular architecture addresses fundamental challenges common to sensor-based medical monitoring-limited labeled pathological data, clinical interpretability requirements, and detection of subtle anomalies in complex temporal pat-terns-making it broadly applicable to diverse sensing modalities including ECG, EEG, and continuous physiological monitoring systems.

title: Invisible Risks: Evaluating LLM Safety Disparities Across Dialects and Low-Resource Languages
authors: Cynthia C. Nosiri, Emmanuel Masa-ibi, Hashmath Fathima, Binisa Giri, Derrick Cook, Kofi Nyarko
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in safety-critical domains such as education, healthcare, and public services. However, safety evaluations rarely account for linguistic diversity, overlooking dialectal disparities that can reinforce systemic bias. We introduce a structured, multi-model framework to assess the safety and helpfulness of LLMs across four language varieties: Standard American English (SAE), African American Vernacular English (AAVE), Nigerian Pidgin, and Nepali. Using 100 prompts across five risk domains, we evaluate GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, and Gemini 1.5, annotating outputs for safety, helpfulness, refusal type, and failure type. Results reveal marked disparities: AAVE and Nigerian Pidgin responses showed both higher refusal rates and lower helpfulness, alongside increased under-detection of unsafe content, compared to SAE. In contrast, Nepali often outperformed SAE, exposing paradoxes in safety calibration. Inter-annotator agreement analyses confirm reliability. We release our rubric, dataset, and pipeline to foster equitable auditing, highlighting the urgent need for dialect-aware safety benchmarks and more inclusive AI alignment.

title:
Normally-Off p-GaN Gate Hemts Enabled by Oxygen Plasma Treatment with Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Oxygen-Rich Capping Layer
authors: Heran Fan, Mingxuan Zhang, Jiachen Duan, Xuanming Zhang, Qiyuan Zhang, Jie Zhang, Wen Liu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This work investigates p-GaN gate high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) in which a 3nm Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> capping layer is first deposited prior to oxygen plasma treatment (OPT) in the access region, promoting the formation of high-resistivity GaN (HR-GaN). The Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> layer serves simultaneously as a protective layer and an oxygen-rich layer, facilitating enhanced oxygen diffusion. Experimental results demonstrate that a post-annealing temperature of 350 °C yields optimal device performance, with reduced sheet resistance, positive threshold voltage, and an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio approaching 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>. The secondary-ion-mass spectrometry (SIMS) results also confirmed that the Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> layer helps to enhance the diffusion and content of oxygen in p-GaN. These findings provide valuable insights for optimizing Gallium nitride (GaN) device fabrication toward robust normally-off (E-mode) operation.

title: PMFSNet from Scratch: Evaluating Lightweight Backbone Efficiency for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Mina Kamel, Ahmed Fathy El Nokrash, Mai Mohamed Said
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Medical image segmentation functions as an essential tool for both clinical diagnosis and treatment preparation. Deep learning models achieve better segmentation accuracy but their high computational needs restrict their deployment in real-time or edge-device applications. Lightweight models with multi-scale feature extraction provide an efficient solution that maintains high-performance levels. The evaluation of their accuracy together with computational cost has not received sufficient systematic assessment. This review assesses lightweight segmentation models that use multi-scale features that were published between 2020 and 2025. The models are divided into three distinct categories: (1) pure CNNs, (2) CNNs with attention mechanisms, and (3) hybrid models that integrate transformers or MLPs. The evaluation process includes performance benchmarking of three public datasets (CVC-ClinicDB, Kvasir-SEG, and ISIC2018) through Dice score, mean IoU, parameter count, and GFLOPs metrics. The MILUNet and MSMULNet CNN-based models deliver high Dice scores exceeding 93% on Kvasir-SEG while maintaining extremely low complexity at under 2.2M parameters and 0.05 GFLOPs. The attention-based models PMFSNet and PIS-Net enhance global context understanding, but transformer-based models like LM-Net deliver superior accuracy at increased computational expense. The review demonstrates that segmentation accuracy depends on resource availability. Pure CNNs provide the best solution for fast efficient deployment, yet hybrid and transformer models deliver better precision when resources become available. Future research needs to develop better generalization capabilities for imaging modalities while optimizing the accuracy-efficiency tradeoff for practical clinical use.

title: Design of Toroidal Coil Energy Harvester for Leaky Feeder
authors: Delgermurun Batbold, Zagarsuren Batbaatar, Purevtseren Bayarsaikhan, Uuganbayar Purevdorj
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This paper proposes a design of toroidal coil energy harverster, it harvests electrical energy from the radio frequency (RF) energy of the leaky feeder communication system. The leaky feeder system in UHF band is used as a main telecommunication method in the underground tunnels. Many kinds of sensors for the environment monitoring in the tunnels have been worked, that requires low power energy source. In addition, special needs of this kind of design can be mentioned derived from real problems, which can be low power LED blink cable identifier, RF power level meter for dusty and dusk environment of tunnels of underground mine. The simulated results of proposed design show power capacity of 0.754 W, it is sufficient to energy demands of low power devices that require 0.5 to 5 mW of power. This approach offers alternative potentials such as self-sustaining and maintenance-free compared to conventional battery-powered systems.

title: TBIVL: A Unified Identity Verification Layer for Secure Agent-to-Service Context Exchange
authors: Siva Rama Krishna Varma Bayyavarapu, Srinivasateja Songa, Siva Prasad Nandi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Secure agent-to-service context exchange requires strong guarantees that both the calling agent and the target service are authentic. Without such guarantees, systems remain vulnerable to impersonation, context interception, and privilege abuse. While prior work has primarily focused on prompt injection defenses, output filtering, and access control policies, the verification of entity authenticity at the moment of context exchange remains underexplored. Moreover, existing transport-layer mechanisms such as mutual TLS do not sufficiently bind application-layer requests to verified agent and service identities.In this paper, we introduce the Trust-Bounded Identity Verification Layer (TBIVL), a unified application-layer framework that enforces identity verification and request-scoped authorization prior to context exchange. TBIVL integrates agent attestation, service authenticity validation, and fine-grained authorization into a single decision layer, ensuring that only trusted interactions are permitted.We implement a prototype and evaluate it through both controlled experiments and large-scale simulations. In a controlled setting (five scenarios, 1,000 requests) and a broader simulation (100 agents, 50 services, including 10 spoofed entities, across 10,000 requests), TBIVL successfully rejected all malicious requests. Additional sensitivity analysis across varying malicious traffic ratios (5–50%) showed consistent resilience with no successful attacks observed. The system maintained a mean latency overhead of less than 0.04 ms per request.These results demonstrate that combining agent attestation, service validation, and scoped authorization at the application layer can effectively mitigate impersonation and spoofing attacks while introducing minimal performance overhead.

title: Modeling Heteroscedastic Noise in Infrared Thermography Using Expectation-Maximization PCA
authors: Jasleen Kaur, Vanita Arora, Prabhu Babu, Ravibabu Mulaveesala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Multimedia (ICSM)
abstract: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is widely used for dimensionality reduction and feature extraction in infrared imaging. However, its assumption of uniform noise (homoscedasticity) across all frames limits its effectiveness with real thermographic data, where noise levels can vary due to factors such as non-uniform heating, sensor artifacts, or other environmental fluctuations. This paper introduces an Expectation-Maximization PCA (EM-PCA) framework tailored for thermal imaging, which explicitly models sample dependent noise variances. Using an efficient alternating maximization algorithm, our method jointly estimates both the latent thermal structure and heterogeneous noise variances, resulting in improved subspace recovery and defect localization. Experimental validation is performed on real thermographic datasets acquired through pulse thermography on a mild steel sample with three defects. The results demonstrate that EM-PCA significantly outperforms standard PCA in terms of reconstruction accuracy and robustness, particularly under anisotropic conditions.

title: A Data-Based Ambulance Diversion Decision-Support Framework Tailored to Washington, D.C.’s Hospital System
authors: Donia Drias, Kaitlyn Frost, Collin Schwabicello, Eric Dano
publish: 2026 Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium (SIEDS)
abstract: Emergency Departments (EDs) in Washington, D.C. experience persistent operational strain, including long patient wait times, extended ambulance offload delays, and uneven distribution of emergency patients across the hospital network. Addressing the District's current lack of standardized diversion framework is essential to improving patient safety, strengthening emergency response capacity, and promoting equitable access to emergency care.This work focuses on the development of a data-based ambulance diversion decision-support framework tailored to Washington, D.C. 's hospital system. The goal is to identify key indicators of ED strain that are most strongly linked to poor patient outcomes and to use these indicators to guide EMS (Emergency Medical Services) transport decisions across the regional hospital network.The methodology integrates a structured literature review and statistical and machine learning techniques. Indicators of ED strain are identified from the literature. The most influential indicators are then used to train a random forest model that estimates diversion risk and supports EMS routing decisions. Model performance is compared against diversion strategies documented in other large metropolitan areas. Results include improved prediction of adverse patient outcomes, and demonstrated reductions in ambulance offload delays and patient wait times under simulated diversion scenarios. The resulting framework provides DC Health with a scalable, data-driven tool to improve EMS coordination, enhanced system resilience, and support more equitable emergency care delivery.

title: Power Flow Control of a Grid-Connected Hybrid Renewable Energy System employing a Multiport Converter: A Simulation Study
authors: Kruthi Jayaram, Vidya H A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Increasing renewable energy and the rising demand for clean power have accelerated the development of hybrid renewable energy systems towards microgrid applications. The intermittency of solar and wind energies and the generation-load mismatch collectively render severe barriers to grid integration. In this respect, a grid-connected hybrid renewable energy system using a multiport bidirectional DC-DC converter is implemented. The system integrates PV, WECS and a BES system through a single-stage multiport converter topology, reducing component count, conversion stages, and control complexity. The interfacing of the battery is done through a bidirectional DC–DC converter to achieve controlled charging and discharging, ensuring power balance during variations in the renewable generation and load demand. A centralized control strategy has been implemented for regulating the DC bus voltage, managing bidirectional power flow. Simulation results demonstrate effective power sharing among PV, wind, battery, and the grid, stable DC bus voltage regulation, and satisfactory grid-side performance.

title: CyberGuard: A Smart Vulnerability Detection System
authors: Wael Farouk Elsersy, Hossam Abdelreheem Soltan, Abdelrahman Elshazly Zaky, Ahmed Abdelwahab, Mohamed Elsersy
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Software vulnerabilities remain a critical concern in modern application development, exposing systems to risks such as data breaches, operational failures, and financial liabilities. To address these challenges, this study introduces CyberGuard, an advanced machine learning-based system to detect, classify, and remediate software vulnerabilities. CyberGuard leverages transformer-based models, particularly CodeBERT, to conduct real-time static code analysis, enabling precise identification of security issues and delivering actionable guidance to developers. By incorporating the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) taxonomy and the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) metrics, the platform supports detailed categorization and prioritization of vulnerabilities based on severity. The study evaluated the software Vulnerabilities detection system using benchmark datasets that include a wide range of programming languages with several vulnerability types. Hence, the study evaluates the performance of CyberGuard against state-of-the-art systems such as LineVul and VulDeePecker. Experimental results show that CyberGuard achieves a 12.7% improvement in F1-score, demonstrating its effectiveness in identifying and addressing security threats. Furthermore, its ability to integrate smoothly into DevSecOps pipelines offers a scalable and efficient approach to strengthening secure software development practices. CyberGuard lays a robust foundation for future research in proactive threat mitigation and intelligent vulnerability repair, contributing to the advancement of AI-driven cybersecurity.

title: Machine Learning Based IoT Adaptive Architecture for Epilepsy Seizure Detection: Anatomy and Analysis
authors: Zag ElSayed, Murat Ozer, Nelly Elsayed, Ahmed Abdelgawad
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: A seizure tracking system is crucial for monitoring and evaluating epilepsy treatments. Caretaker seizure diaries are used in epilepsy care today, but clinical seizure monitoring may miss seizures. Monitoring devices that can be worn may be better tolerated and more suitable for long-term ambulatory use. Many techniques and methods are proposed for seizure detection; However, simplicity and affordability are key concepts for daily use while preserving the accuracy of the detection. In this study, we propose a versal, affordable noninvasive based on a simple real-time k-Nearest-Neighbors (kNN) machine learning that can be customized and adapted to individual users in less than four seconds of training time; the system was verified and validated using 500 subjects, with seizure detection data sampled at 178 Hz, the operated with a mean accuracy of (94.5%).

title: Rapid Rise-Time Calibration Signal Generation for Amplitude and Phase Calibration in Multi Sub-Band Acquisition System
authors: Ming Wei, Duyu Qiu, Bin Li, Aijun Chen, Zhiyu Zheng, Zhihong Chen
publish: 2024 IEEE AUTOTESTCON
abstract: The frequency response flatness of multi subband acquisition system can be optimized by amplitude and phase calibration, but how to generate calibration signals with rapid rise time has become an urgent problem. In this paper, the design is based on the fast response characteristics of high-speed logic gate circuits to reduce the rise time of square wave signals. Increase the broadband equalization circuit to compensate for the high-frequency component gain in the square wave signal, optimize the frequency response flatness of the square wave signal, and further reduction the rise time of the square wave signal. The rise time of the square wave signal is shortened from 100 ps to 21 ps, and the frequency response flatness of the system is better optimized when the square wave signal is used as the calibration source of the multi sub-band acquisition system.
